## LEGEND

| Marker | Meaning |
|---|---|
| ✓ | Fully implemented and wired |
| ⚠ | Partially implemented, or differs from PRD intent |
| ✗ | Present in the PRD but **not** implemented |
| 🔴 | Known bug or broken behaviour |

**Ground truths (verified, applied throughout):** icons are **Material Symbols Outlined** (no Tabler font is loaded — a few stray `ti ti-*` classes render blank, see Part 1.3 / Part 11); body font **Montserrat**; theme indigo `--p #1a237e` / teal `--accent #00897b` with LG navy `#2D3E51` / crimson `#E64D3D` accents inline in newer views; AI summaries use **Gemini 2.5 Flash** (`gemini-2.5-flash`); auto clock-out is a **daily midnight-UTC (05:30 IST) reset**, not an 18-hour cap.

## PART 1: APPLICATION SHELL

This part documents the global chrome shared by every view: the design-token system, typography scale, icon library, sidebar navigation, header bar, and the cross-cutting UI patterns (toast / loading / empty-state / modal shell). All field-level claims below were verified against `src/index.html` and `src/app.js.html` (line ranges in the trailer).

The shell markup lives in `src/index.html`; the behaviour (navigation, refresh, toast, empty-state, modal open/close) lives in `src/app.js.html`.

---

### 1.1 Design Tokens

All theme tokens are CSS custom properties declared in a single `:root{}` block at `index.html:12-18`. There is **one** canonical declaration set — no light/dark theme switch exists.

```css
:root{
  --p:#1a237e;--p2:#3949ab;--p3:#e8eaf6;
  --accent:#00897b;--danger:#c62828;--warn:#e65100;--ok:#2e7d32;
  --bg:#f0f2f5;--surface:#fff;--border:#e0e0e0;
  --text:#212121;--muted:#757575;--muted2:#9e9e9e;
  --sidebar:230px;--hh:68px;--r:8px;--sh:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.1);
}
```

| Token | Value | Category | Representative usage |
|---|---|---|---|
| `--p` | `#1a237e` (indigo 900) | Brand primary | `#header` bg, `.nav-item.active` text/border, `.btn-primary`, `.stat-val`, `.login-appname`, `.luc-name`, `.modal` accents |
| `--p2` | `#3949ab` (indigo 600) | Brand primary-2 | `.proj-card` top border, `.tc-project`, `.proj-group-icon`, `.ts-input` border, login-logo icon color |
| `--p3` | `#e8eaf6` (indigo 50) | Brand primary-tint | `.nav-item.active` bg, `.ms-chip` bg, `.login-user-card` bg, `.tc-row:hover` bg, `.pres-menu-item.active-status` bg |
| `--accent` | `#00897b` (teal 600) | Accent | `#hd-avatar` bg, `.btn-accent` |
| `--danger` | `#c62828` (red 800) | Error/destructive | `.btn-danger`, `.nav-badge` bg, `.toast.error`, `.tc-deadline`, `.login-error` text, `.task-card.p-Critical` border |
| `--warn` | `#e65100` (orange 900) | Warning | `.btn`? no — `.toast.warn`, `.tc-deadline.warn`, `.task-card.p-High` border |
| `--ok` | `#2e7d32` (green 800) | Success | `.toast.success`, `.tc-deadline.ok` |
| `--bg` | `#f0f2f5` (cool grey) | Page bg | `body` bg, `.tdm-card` bg, `.modal-icon-btn:hover`, `.wl-inp` bg |
| `--surface` | `#fff` | Card/panel surface | `#sidebar`, `.modal`, `.stat-card`, `.proj-card`, `.pres-menu`, `.ms-dropdown` bg |
| `--border` | `#e0e0e0` | Hairline border | `.nav-item` separators, `.modal-hd`/`.modal-ft` borders, `.fc` border, `.btn-ghost` border |
| `--text` | `#212121` | Primary text | `body` color, `.nav-item`, `.proj-name`, `.fc` color |
| `--muted` | `#757575` | Secondary text | `.ph-sub`, `.tc-meta`, `.modal-x`, `.fg label`, `.empty-state` |
| `--muted2` | `#9e9e9e` | Tertiary text/icon | `.nav-sec`, `.tc-id`, `.atm-new-btn`, `.task-card.p-Low` border, `.empty-state .ei` |
| `--sidebar` | `230px` | Layout dimension | `#sidebar` width, `#main{margin-left}` |
| `--hh` | `68px` | Layout dimension | `#header` height, `#main{padding-top}`, `#sidebar{top}` |
| `--r` | `8px` | Border-radius | `.btn`, `.modal`, `.stat-card`, `.wd-hdr-btn`, `.nav` badges? (`.nav-badge` uses fixed 10px) |
| `--sh` | `0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.1)` | Box-shadow | `.stat-card`, `.proj-card`, `.toast`, `.dash-proj-detail-col` |

> ⚠️ **GAP (low):** The newer Task-Sheet / Work-Log views use the LG brand palette `#2D3E51` (navy) and `#E64D3D` (crimson) **inline** rather than as `:root` tokens (e.g. `app.js.html:9049,9123,9251,9270`). These two brand colours are NOT in the token table, so the design system has two parallel, un-unified palettes (indigo/teal token-based vs. navy/crimson inline literal). This is a consistency gap, not a functional break.

> NOTE: `--warn` is `#e65100` (a deep orange). The `.task-card.p-Medium` / `.tc-row.p-Medium` priority colour is `#1565c0` (blue), a hardcoded literal not drawn from any token (`index.html:155,173`).

### 1.2 Typography

- **Font family:** `'Montserrat', sans-serif` loaded from Google Fonts (`index.html:8`) with weights `300;400;500;600;700;800` plus italic `400`. Applied globally on `body{font-family:'Montserrat',sans-serif}` (`index.html:21`).
- **Base size (fluid clamp):** `body{font-size:clamp(13px,0.89vw + 9.6px,15px)}` (`index.html:21`). Floor 13px (≈375px viewport) → ceiling 15px (≥768px viewport). Comment at line 19 documents the intent: "Fluid typography: scales from 13px (375px viewport) → 15px (768px+)".
- **Weights in use:** 300 (Material Symbols `wght`), 400 (body/options), 500 (`.nav-item-name`, `.tc-project`), 600 (`.btn`, `.nav-item.active`, `.modal-hd-title`, badges), 700 (titles, `.stat-val`, `.luc-name`, `.h-logo`), 800 (loaded but rarely referenced).

| Selector | font-size | weight | Role |
|---|---|---|---|
| `body` | `clamp(13px,0.89vw+9.6px,15px)` | 400 | Base text |
| `.h-logo` | `16px` | 700 | Header brand wordmark |
| `.ph-left .ph-title` | `20px` | 700 | View page-header title |
| `.ph-left .ph-sub` | `13px` | 400 | View page-header subtitle |
| `.stat-val` | `30px` | 700 | Dashboard stat number |
| `.stat-label` | `11px` | 600 | Dashboard stat label (uppercase, `.5px` tracking) |
| `.nav-item` | `13px` | 400 (600 active) | Sidebar item |
| `.nav-sec` | `10px` | 700 | Sidebar section header (uppercase, 1px tracking) |
| `.nav-icon` | `18px` | — | Sidebar Material Symbol |
| `.modal-hd-title` | `16px` | 600 | Modal header title |
| `.modal-x` | `20px` | — | Modal close glyph |
| `.btn` | `13px` | 600 | Buttons (`.btn-sm` → 12px) |
| `.fg label` | `11px` | 700 | Form field label (uppercase, `.5px` tracking) |
| `.fc` | `13px` | 400 | Form control text |
| `.toast` | `13px` | 400 | Toast body (15px on mobile, `index.html:1561`) |
| `.login-appname` | `22px` | 700 | Login app name |
| `.empty-state p` | `14px` | 400 | Empty-state caption |

Mobile typography overrides exist in the `@media` block (e.g. `index.html:1559 .modal h2,.modal-title{font-size:20px}`, `:1561 .toast{font-size:15px}`).

### 1.3 Icon System

- **Library:** Material Symbols Outlined (Google). Loaded via one stylesheet link:
  `index.html:9` →
  ```html
  <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Material+Symbols+Outlined:opsz,wght,FILL,GRAD@20,300,0,0" rel="stylesheet"/>
  ```
- **Base rule** (`index.html:93`):
  ```css
  .material-symbols-outlined{font-variation-settings:'FILL' 0,'wght' 300,'GRAD' 0,'opsz' 20;font-size:inherit;line-height:1;vertical-align:middle;user-select:none}
  ```
  Glyphs are unfilled (`FILL 0`), weight 300, optical size 20. Size is set per-call via inline `font-size` or via parent rules (`.nav-icon{font-size:18px}`, `.modal-icon-btn .material-symbols-outlined{font-size:16px}`, `.tl-team-hdr .material-symbols-outlined{font-size:18px}`, etc.). Usage is `<span class="material-symbols-outlined">home</span>` (text content = glyph name).
- **No Tabler font is loaded.** There is no `@font-face`, no `tabler-icons` CDN link, and no `font-family` referencing `ti` anywhere in `index.html` (verified — grep returned no font-face/tabler hits).

> 🔴 **BROKEN — stray Tabler `ti ti-*` icon classes (11 occurrences).** `app.js.html` emits `<i class="ti ti-…">` markup in the dashboard / clock widgets. Because the Tabler icon font is never loaded, these elements render **blank** (zero-width empty `<i>`). Exact locations:
>
> | Line | Class | Context |
> |---|---|---|
> | 2421 | `ti ti-clock-hour-4` | clock display |
> | 2499 | `ti ti-clock-hour-4` | clock display |
> | 9029 | `ti ti-calendar-off` | dashboard / leave block |
> | 9049 | `ti ti-alert-triangle` | overdue warning (color `#E64D3D`) |
> | 9106 | `ti ti-chevron-down` | dashboard "upcoming" toggle chevron |
> | 9123 | `ti ti-sort-descending-2` | "My Upcoming Tasks" header icon |
> | 9125 | `ti ti-arrow-right` | "view all" link arrow |
> | 9141 | `ti ti-arrow-right` | "view all" link arrow |
> | 9251 | `ti ti-alert-triangle` | overdue warning |
> | 9270 | `ti ti-calendar-week` | calendar-week header icon |
> | 9287 | `ti ti-calendar-off` | no-calendar block |
>
> The brief noted ~8 stray classes; there are in fact **11** (some duplicated glyph names). Each is a rendering gap — the surrounding text still shows, but the leading icon is invisible. Fix = replace each with the Material Symbols equivalent (`schedule`, `event_busy`, `warning`, `expand_more`, `sort`, `arrow_forward`, `calendar_view_week`).

### 1.4 Sidebar Navigation

`<nav id="sidebar">` — `position:fixed; top:0; height:100vh; overflow:visible; display:flex; flex-direction:column`. `overflow:visible` (changed from `overflow:hidden`) allows `#sb-collapse-btn` at `right:-12px` to protrude outside the sidebar right edge without being clipped; `.sb-scroll` carries `overflow-x:hidden` for nav item clipping instead. Width controlled by `--sidebar` CSS var (default 230px). **Collapsed state:** `#sb-collapse-btn` (floating circle arrow at `position:absolute; top:78px; right:-12px; z-index:10; width:24px; height:24px`) calls `toggleSidebarCollapse()`, which toggles `#sidebar.sb-collapsed` and sets `--sidebar` to 54px (collapsed) or `SB_EXPANDED_WIDTH` (expanded). **Resizable:** `#sb-resize-handle` (4px drag strip on right edge) allows 180–400px drag-resize. Neither collapse nor resize is persisted to `localStorage`. Both update `--sidebar` via `document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--sidebar', …)` so `#header{left}` and `#main{margin-left}` reflow live. All sidebar text labels use `<span class="sb-label">`; rule `#sidebar.sb-collapsed .sb-label{display:none!important}` hides all labels when collapsed.

**Internal structure:**

1. `#sb-collapse-btn` — `position:absolute; top:78px; right:-12px; z-index:10; width:24px; height:24px; background:#fff; border:1px solid #c7ccd1; border-radius:50%` — protrudes 12px outside the sidebar right edge. `overflow:visible` on `#sidebar` ensures it is not clipped.
2. `#sb-resize-handle` — 4px invisible strip position:absolute on right edge, full height.
3. **Logo row** (no id) — first pinned element, above Import Tasks; `display:flex; align-items:center; gap:7px; padding:11px 12px; flex-shrink:0; color:#28384a`. Material Symbol `task_alt` (17px) + `<span class="sb-label">LG Desk</span>`. Dark navy colours — logo was moved from header to sidebar; header-era white/rgba colours replaced with dark sidebar colours.
4. `#nav-import-btn` — **Import Tasks** pinned above the scrollable area; `background:#faece7; color:#28384a; font-weight:500`; icon `color:#993c1d`; visible to all logged-in roles (no `.hidden`, no RBAC gate); `onclick="openMigrateModal()"`.
5. `<div class="sb-scroll">` — the only scrollable container; `flex:1; overflow-y:auto; overflow-x:hidden`; thin scrollbar via `.sb-scroll::-webkit-scrollbar`; contains `#nav-main` (all three nav sections) and `#nav-chats-wrap`.
6. `#sidebar-profile-pin` — user profile card as a sibling AFTER `.sb-scroll`; `flex-shrink:0; position:relative`; never scrolls out of view.

Every item inside `#nav-main` is `<div class="nav-item" data-view="…" onclick="navigate('…')">` containing a `.nav-icon > .material-symbols-outlined` and `<span class="sb-label">Label</span>`; some carry a `.nav-badge` and/or a role-gate class.

Active-state mechanics (`app.js.html:1137-1139`): `navigate()` toggles `.active` on the matching item via `n.classList.toggle('active', n.dataset.view === view)`. CSS `.nav-item.active{background:var(--p3);border-left-color:var(--p);color:var(--p);font-weight:600}` (`index.html:91`).

| # | Label (verbatim) | Material Symbol | data-view | id / badge id | Role gate | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| — | **My Space** (section) | — | — | `.nav-sec` | none | always visible |
| 1 | Dashboard | `home` | `dashboard` | — | none | starts with `.active` in markup (`:1801`) |

**Role-gate logic** (`app.js.html:1044-1057`, inside `onPayloadLoaded`):
```js
var adminRoles   = ['Super Admin','Admin'];
var managerRoles = ['Super Admin','Admin','Team Captain','Team Facilitator'];
if (managerRoles.indexOf(u.role) !== -1) { document.querySelectorAll('.nav-mgr-only').forEach(e=>e.classList.remove('hidden')); }
if (adminRoles.indexOf(u.role)   !== -1) { document.querySelectorAll('.nav-admin-only').forEach(e=>e.classList.remove('hidden')); }
if (u.hasMisAccess) { el('nav-mis-report')?.classList.remove('hidden'); }
```

> NOTE: The `.nav-admin-only` reveal pass (line 1051) is functional — two non-nav elements carry the class: the Notice Board **Post** button (`#btn-nb-post`, `index.html:1916`) and the Calendar **Add Holiday** button (`#btn-add-holiday-cal`, `index.html:2383`). Both are `.hidden` by default and un-hidden for Admin/Super Admin by this pass. There is, however, no admin-exclusive **sidebar nav item** — these two are page-content buttons, not nav links, so admin-only gating exists only for those buttons, not at the nav level.

> NOTE: All `.nav-mgr-only` items use the SAME gate (4 roles). There is no separate visual distinction between TC/TF and Admin/SA in the sidebar — both managers and admins see the identical "Company" section including All Tasks / All Projects / Organisation / Forms. Per-row data scoping (not nav visibility) is what differentiates them server-side.

> NOTE: On mobile, `navigate()` redirects blocked views to dashboard (`app.js.html:1126-1128`, `_MOB_BLOCKED_VIEWS`), and CSS `index.html:1510-1517` + IIFE bypass `:1663` hide the calendar/meetings/org-chart/directory/team-tasks/team-mgmt/org-page/forms nav items.

**Sidebar profile card** (`#sidebar-profile-pin`): sibling AFTER `.sb-scroll`; `flex-shrink:0; padding:10px; border-top:0.5px solid var(--border); position:relative`. Two direct children:
1. `#pres-menu` (`.hidden` by default) — `position:absolute; left:10px; right:10px; bottom:calc(100% - 2px); z-index:30; background:#fff; border-radius:8px; box-shadow:0 -2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,.08)` — opens upward above the card; positioned relative to `#sidebar-profile-pin`.
2. `#user-chip` — `display:flex; align-items:center; gap:8px; padding:7px 8px; border-radius:8px; background:#f4f5f7; cursor:pointer`; `onclick="togglePresMenu(event)"`. Contains: `#hd-avatar` (28×28px circle, `background:#28384a`, white initials); then a `<div class="sb-label">` wrapper (hidden on collapse) containing: `#hd-name` (`font-size:12px; font-weight:600; color:#1a2533`), `#hd-status-dot` (`.pres-dot`, 7×7px), and `#hd-role` (`font-size:10px; font-weight:600; color:#28384a; background:rgba(40,56,74,.1); border-radius:8px; padding:1px 6px`).

Dark colours used throughout (`#1a2533`, `#28384a`, `rgba(40,56,74,.1)`) — element was redesigned for the white sidebar background; former header-era white/rgba-on-navy colours are gone.

### 1.5 Header Bar

`<header id="header">` CSS `index.html:62` → `position:fixed; top:0; left:var(--sidebar); right:0; height:var(--hh)(68px); background:var(--p); display:flex; align-items:center; padding:0 20px; gap:12px; z-index:100`. **Key change**: `left:var(--sidebar)` (not `left:0`) so the header starts beside the sidebar and resizes/collapses with it. Mobile override forces `left:0!important; right:0!important`. Right cluster DOM order (left→right): `#wl-widget-container` → `#wd-hdr-wrap` → `#global-refresh-btn`.

> **Post-UI-relocation note:** The user-chip / profile / presence elements that were previously in the header have been moved to `#sidebar-profile-pin` (sidebar bottom). The header right cluster now holds the clock widget and week-glance widget instead. `#user-chip` and `#hd-status-wrap` no longer exist in the header.

| Element | id / class | Type | Behaviour / wiring |
|---|---|---|---|
| Mobile menu button | `#mob-menu-btn` | `<button>` | `onclick="openMobNav()"`; Material Symbol `menu` (22px). Hidden on desktop, shown on mobile. `aria-label="Open navigation"`. |
| Spacer | `.h-spacer` | `<div>` | `flex:1` — pushes right cluster to the right. Logo was **moved to the sidebar** (no logo element in the header). |
| Week-glance widget | `#wl-widget-container` | pill `background:rgba(255,255,255,.08); border-radius:14px; height:46px; padding:6px 12px` | **First** in header right cluster. Week attendance/hours overview strip. Render target for `_wlLoadWidgetWeek()`. `_buildWlWeekWidget` outputs **3-row layout**: (1) day letters `M T W T F S S`, (2) nav arrow `‹` + 7 dot bubbles (`_attDot`) + nav arrow `›`, (3) date range + hours + days-logged summary. White/rgba colours contrast against navy header. **Initialized once at boot** (`_wlWidgetWeekOffset=0; _wlLoadWidgetWeek()` after `_presStart()`). |
| Clock widget wrapper | `#wd-hdr-wrap` | `<div class="wd-hdr-wrap">` | **Second** in header right cluster. Contains `#wd-hdr-btn` (pill `background:rgba(255,255,255,.08); border-radius:14px; height:46px; color:#fff`), `#wd-hdr-dot`, `#wd-hdr-label`, `#wd-popup`, `#wd-widget`, `#wd-timer`. **Initialized once at boot** (`loadWorkDuration()` after `_presStart()`). |
| Refresh button | `#global-refresh-btn` (icon `#global-refresh-icon`) | `<button>` | `onclick="globalRefresh()"`; Material Symbol `refresh` (16px). Inline styled: `width:46px; height:46px; background:rgba(255,255,255,.08); border-radius:14px; color:#fff`. **Last** in header right cluster. Icon-only (no text label). Spins during refresh. |

**Avatar, name, role, status dot, presence menu** — now in `#sidebar-profile-pin` (see §1.4 above), **not in the header**.

**Presence menu items** (`#pres-menu`, anchored to `#sidebar-profile-pin`):
| Item | id | onclick | Glyph |
|---|---|---|---|
| Online | `#pmi-online` | `setPresStatus('online')` | `.pres-dot.pres-online` |
| Away | `#pmi-away` | `setPresStatus('away')` | `.pres-dot.pres-away` |
| Do Not Disturb | `#pmi-dnd` | `setPresStatus('dnd')` | `.pres-dot.pres-dnd` |
| Appear Offline | `#pmi-offline` | `setPresStatus('offline')` | `.pres-dot.pres-offline` |
| *(divider)* | — | — | — |
| My Profile | (none) | `openProfileModal()` | Material Symbol `manage_accounts` |
| Change password | (none) | `openProfileModal('password')` | Material Symbol `lock` |
| *(divider)* | — | — | — |
| Sign Out | (none) | `logout()` | Material Symbol `logout` (red text) |

> NOTE: `logout()` (`app.js.html:1090-1103`) removes the `localStorage` session key, nulls `APP._sessToken`, fire-and-forget calls `invalidateSession(token)`, then `location.reload()` on both success and failure handlers.

> NOTE: `openProfileModal(initialTab?)` — new optional `initialTab` parameter. No-arg call defaults to `'info'` tab. `openProfileModal('password')` jumps directly to the Password tab, used by the "Change password" menu item.

---

### 1.6 Global Patterns

#### Toast

- **Function:** `toast(msg, type)` — `app.js.html:13663-13669`:
  ```js
  function toast(msg, type) {
    var d = document.createElement('div');
    d.className = 'toast' + (type ? ' ' + type : '');
    d.textContent = msg;
    el('toasts').appendChild(d);
    setTimeout(function() { d.remove(); }, 3500);
  }
  ```
- **Container:** `#toasts` — `index.html:666` → `position:fixed;bottom:20px;right:20px;z-index:999;display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:8px;max-width:360px`. Bottom-right stack, newest at bottom. Mobile: `left:10px;right:10px;max-width:none;bottom:14px` (`:1503`).
- **Lifetime:** auto-removed after **3500 ms** (hardcoded). No manual dismiss control.
- **Variant CSS** (`index.html:682-685`):
  - `.toast` → dark grey `#323232` (default / unrecognised type)
  - `.toast.success` → `var(--ok)` green
  - `.toast.error` → `var(--danger)` red
  - `.toast.warn` → `var(--warn)` orange

> 🔴 **BROKEN — toast variant class mismatch (`ok`/`info` never styled).** The CSS only defines `.success`, `.error`, `.warn`. But callers pass non-matching type strings:
> - `globalRefresh()` correctly passes `'success'` and `'error'` (`app.js.html:8276,8259,8281`). ✅
> - However **23 call-sites** pass `toast(…, 'ok')` or `toast(…, 'info')` (grep count = 23). Neither `.toast.ok` nor `.toast.info` exists in CSS, so these toasts fall through to the default dark-grey `.toast` style — they appear but **lose their semantic colour** (a "success" save shows grey instead of green; an info note shows grey). This is a wiring mismatch between the convention documented in CLAUDE.md (`type: 'ok' | 'error' | 'info'`) and the actual CSS class names (`success`/`error`/`warn`).
>
> Net: only `'success'`, `'error'`, `'warn'` produce coloured toasts. `'ok'` and `'info'` render grey. Fix = either add `.toast.ok{background:var(--ok)}` / `.toast.info{…}` aliases, or normalise all callers to `success`/`error`/`warn`.

#### Loading (spinner + skeleton)

- **Inline button spinners** (`index.html:132-133`): `.btn-spinner` (white-on-transparent ring) and `.btn-spinner-dark` (indigo ring) — both `12×12`, `animation:spin .7s linear infinite`. Keyframe `@keyframes spin{to{transform:rotate(360deg)}}` (`:134`).
- **Login spinner** (`index.html:43`): `.login-card.verifying .login-status::before` — 14×14 ring, same `spin .7s`.
- **Global-refresh spinner:** the header refresh icon spins via JS-set `icon.style.animation='spin 0.8s linear infinite'` during `globalRefresh()` (`app.js.html:8254`), cleared in both handlers.
- **Skeleton loader** (`index.html:136-140`):
  - `.view-skeleton{padding:16px 0}` wrapper
  - `.sk-row{height:44px;…animation:shimmer 1.4s ease infinite;border-radius:6px}` — table/list row placeholder
  - `.sk-card{height:90px;…animation:shimmer 1.4s ease infinite;border-radius:10px}` — card placeholder
  - `@keyframes shimmer{0%{background-position:100% 0}100%{background-position:-100% 0}}` — left-sweeping shimmer gradient `#f0f2f5 → #e4e7ec → #f0f2f5`.

#### Empty state

- **Function:** `emptyState(title, sub)` — `app.js.html:13658-13661`:
  ```js
  function emptyState(title, sub) {
    return '<div class="empty-state"><div class="ei material-symbols-outlined">inbox</div><p><strong>' + esc(title) + '</strong>' +
      (sub ? '<br>' + esc(sub) : '') + '</p></div>';
  }
  ```
  Returns an HTML string (caller injects via `innerHTML`). Always uses the Material Symbol `inbox` as the icon. Both `title` and `sub` are passed through `esc()` (XSS-safe). `sub` is optional.
- **CSS** (`index.html:948-950`): `.empty-state{text-align:center;padding:40px 20px;color:var(--muted)}`, `.empty-state .ei{font-size:40px;margin-bottom:10px;color:var(--muted);opacity:.5}`, `.empty-state p{font-size:14px}`.

#### Modal shell

The standard modal pattern is an overlay (`.modal-bg`) wrapping a box (`.modal`), built from a sticky header (`.modal-hd`), body (`.modal-bd`), and sticky footer (`.modal-ft`). CSS at `index.html:577-625`.

| Class | CSS (verbatim, abridged) | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `.modal-bg` | `position:fixed;inset:0;background:rgba(0,0,0,.5);z-index:200;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;padding:16px` | Backdrop + centering. Click on backdrop closes (via `closeModal(id,event)` target check). |
| `.modal` | `background:var(--surface);border-radius:var(--r);box-shadow:0 12px 40px rgba(0,0,0,.25);width:100%;max-width:540px;max-height:92vh;overflow-y:auto;animation:slideUp .2s ease` | The box. Default max-width 540px. |
| `.modal.modal-lg` | `max-width:760px` | Large modal (task/fn detail, attachments). |
| `.modal.modal-xl` | `max-width:96vw;width:96vw` | Extra-large modal (near full-width). |
| `.modal-hd` | `padding:16px 20px;border-bottom:1px solid var(--border);display:flex;align-items:center;gap:10px;position:sticky;top:0;background:var(--surface);z-index:1` | Sticky header bar. |
| `.modal-hd-title` | `font-size:16px;font-weight:600;flex:1` | Header title text. |
| `.modal-x` | `background:none;border:none;font-size:20px;cursor:pointer;color:var(--muted);line-height:1;padding:0 4px` | Close glyph (`✕`). |
| `.modal-icon-btn` | `32×32 bordered button` | Header action buttons (edit/delete). `.modal-icon-btn-danger:hover` = red. |
| `.modal-bd` | `padding:20px` | Body content. |
| `.modal-ft` | `padding:14px 20px;border-top:1px solid var(--border);display:flex;justify-content:flex-end;gap:10px;position:sticky;bottom:0;background:var(--surface)` | Sticky footer with right-aligned buttons. |
| `@keyframes slideUp` | `from{transform:translateY(24px);opacity:0}` | Entrance animation. |

> ⚠️ **GAP (low) — class-name divergence from brief.** The brief asked to document `.modal-bg / .modal / .modal-hd / .modal-bd / .modal-ft / .modal-lg / .modal-xl`. These all exist EXCEPT the modifier classes are applied as **compound selectors** `.modal.modal-lg` / `.modal.modal-xl` (not standalone `.modal-lg`). Additionally, `_mobileInit()` (per CLAUDE.md) references `.modal-box`/`.modal-content` for full-screen mobile overrides, but those class names do **not** appear in the `index.html` style block (the real classes are `.modal-bg`/`.modal`). So the mobile full-screen modal override targets selectors that may not match the actual modal markup — potential mobile modal-sizing gap (needs runtime verification; CSS `index.html:1487-1489` does correctly target `.modal`/`.modal.modal-lg`).

**Open/close mechanics** (`app.js.html:8352-8360`):
```js
function openModal(id) { el(id).classList.remove('hidden'); }
function closeModal(id, e) {
  if (!e || e.target === el(id)) {       // close only on programmatic call OR backdrop click
    el(id).classList.add('hidden');
    if (id === 'member-log-modal') { /* clear ML_DEBOUNCE timers */ }
  }
}
```
- Modals are toggled purely via the `.hidden` class (`index.html:944 .hidden{display:none!important}`).
- Backdrop-click close works because `.modal-bg` carries `onclick="closeModal('<id>',event)"` and `closeModal` only closes when `e.target === el(id)` (i.e. the click hit the backdrop, not the inner `.modal`).
- The `.modal-x` ✕ button calls `closeModal('<id>')` with no event → closes unconditionally.

**View toggle (related global pattern).** `navigate(view)` (`app.js.html:1108-1167`):
- Removes `.active` and ADDS `.hidden` on all `.view` divs, then removes `.hidden` + adds `.active` on `view-<view>`.
- Toggles `.nav-item.active` by `dataset.view`.
- Dispatches to the per-view render fn (`renderDashboard`, `renderMyTasks`, `loadMyWorkLogs`, …).

> NOTE: The CSS visibility rule is `.view{display:none}` / `.view.active{display:block}` (`index.html:116-117`), while `navigate()` ALSO toggles `.hidden{display:none!important}`. Both mechanisms agree on "hidden = none", so they coexist without conflict, but the `.view` base already hides — the extra `.hidden` toggle is belt-and-suspenders. Minor redundancy, not a bug.

---

#### Cross-references / load-bearing facts
- Auto clock-out is a **daily midnight-UTC (05:30 IST) reset**, not an 18-hour cap (per ground truths; relevant to the clock widget icons at `app.js.html:2421,2499` which are broken `ti ti-clock-hour-4`).
- Gemini model id `gemini-2.5-flash` (key `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — not part of the shell but referenced by the MIS/weekly-summary feature that owns the `#nav-mis-report` item.


## PART 2: AUTHENTICATION & REGISTRATION

> Source read for this slice:
> - `src/index.html` — `#login-screen` block lines 1679–1758; registration modal `#register-modal` lines 3305–3367; team-mgmt / org-page pending-reg containers lines 2350–2374; `.reg-card*` CSS lines 645–654; `#login-screen` gradient line 37.
> - `src/app.js.html` — auto-login `DOMContentLoaded` lines 143–200; `login()` / `showLoginError` / forgot-pw `showForgotPw`/`hideForgotPw`/`sendResetCode`/`resetPasswordSubmit` lines 744–864; `enterDashboard`/`onPayloadLoaded` lines 992–1063; `TEAM_HIERARCHY` client copy lines 705–714; `ROLES` line 202; `_SESS_KEY` line 20; registration funcs `openRegisterModal`→`submitRegistration` lines 7723–7946; reg-card renderer + `_loadPendingRegs` lines 6079–6119; `approveReg`/`rejectReg` lines 6418–6491; `openModal`/`closeModal` lines 8352–8360; `_btnLoading`/`_btnReset` lines 13538–13551; `togglePw` line 11782.
> - `src/auth.gs` — `requestPasswordReset` 287–319; `resetPasswordWithOTP` 321–349; `changePassword` 353–367; `loginWithPassword` 526–574; `getTeamCaptainByTeam` 1525–1585; `setDefaultManager` 1590–1595; `submitRegistration` 1597–1655; `getRegistrationRequests` 1659–1681; `approveRegistration` 1683–1741; `rejectRegistration` 1743–1775; session helpers `_SESS_TTL_MS`/`_sp`/`createSession`/`validateSession`/`invalidateSession` 1855–1900; `hashPassword` 261–267; `_empName` 255–257.

The login screen is a single fixed-position card (`#login-screen`, line 1679, gradient background `linear-gradient(135deg,#1a237e 0%,#283593 50%,#1565c0 100%)`) that overlays the app shell (`#app`) until a payload loads. It hosts THREE mutually-exclusive panels inside one card (`#login-card`):

1. `#login-form-wrap` — email + password sign-in (default visible)
2. `#forgot-pw-wrap` — 2-step forgot-password flow (`display:none` by default)
3. `#login-user-card` + `#btn-enter` — the "verified account" confirmation step (shown after `loginWithPassword` succeeds)

Icon library throughout is **Material Symbols Outlined** (e.g. `task_alt` logo line 1681, `visibility` eyes). No Tabler font is present in this slice.

---

### 2.1 Login View

#### Two-step verified-card → Enter flow

The login is intentionally a **two-click flow**, not a single submit:

1. User enters email+password → clicks **Sign In →** (`#btn-login`, `onclick="login()"`).
2. `login()` (line 744) calls `loginWithPassword(email, password)`. On `{ok:true}` it does NOT navigate. It populates the verified-account card (`#login-user-card`: avatar/name/email/role/team/designation), hides `#login-form-wrap`, sets status text "Account verified. Click below to continue.", and reveals **Enter Dashboard →** (`#btn-enter`).
3. User clicks **Enter Dashboard →** (`onclick="enterDashboard()"`, line 992) → second round-trip `getInitialPayload(APP._verifiedEmail)` → `onPayloadLoaded(p)` (line 1005) loads the app.

The session token is created **inline in `loginWithPassword`** (auth.gs line 552–558, `Utilities.getUuid()` stored as `sess_{token}` in ScriptProperties with `_SESS_TTL_MS` = 7 days) and returned in the response (`r.token`). The frontend saves it to `localStorage['tm_sess']` immediately on Sign-In success (line 767–770), **before** the Enter-Dashboard round-trip — so even if the user closes the tab on the verified card, auto-login still works next time.

> ⚠ **Redundant fallback `createSession`**: `onPayloadLoaded` (lines 1014–1025) ALSO fire-and-forgets a second `createSession(APP._verifiedEmail)` call when `!APP._sessToken`. After a normal password login `APP._sessToken` is already set at line 768, so this branch is skipped; it only fires on the session-restore path where it is unnecessary (the token already exists). Low-impact dead-ish branch. Severity: low.

#### Auto-login (silent session restore)

On `DOMContentLoaded` (app.js.html line 148) the page reads `localStorage['tm_sess']`. Guard at line 153 rejects `null`/empty/`'null'`/`'undefined'`. If a token exists it sets status "Restoring session…", dims the form (`opacity:0.4`), and calls `validateSession(saved)` (backend auth.gs line 1871).

- `{ok:true}` → `APP._sessToken = saved; onPayloadLoaded(r)` → straight into the app (skips both the verified card and the Enter button).
- `{ok:false, reason:'expired'|'not_found'}` → clears the token, shows "Session expired. Please sign in again." (expired) or blank (not_found).
- `{ok:false, reason:'error'|'payload_error'}` or `withFailureHandler` (transient) → **keeps** the token, silently restores the login form (status cleared). Correct anti-logout-on-transient design.

Backend `validateSession` (line 1871) null-guards garbage tokens, checks TTL, applies **sliding 7-day expiry** (line 1886), then returns `Object.assign({ok:true}, getInitialPayload(sess.email))`.

#### Login fields — field-by-field

**Field: Email (sign-in)**
- element:     `<input type="email">`
- id/class:    `id="login-email"` class `fc`
- placeholder: `you@company.com`
- required:    yes (client-checked in `login()` line 747)
- default:     (none) — but boot handler (line 735) focuses it for quick typing
- options:     n/a
- handlers:    `onkeydown="if(event.key==='Enter')el('login-password').focus()"` (Enter moves focus to password, does NOT submit)
- validation:  `login()`: `if (!email || !password) showLoginError('Enter your email and password.')`
- wired:       yes
- data source: passed to `loginWithPassword` GAS call
- gaps:        none

**Field: Password (sign-in)**
- element:     `<input type="password">` + show/hide eye button
- id/class:    `id="login-password"` class `fc`; eye `<button class="pw-eye">` → `togglePw('login-password',this)`
- placeholder: `Password`
- required:    yes
- default:     (none)
- options:     n/a
- handlers:    `onkeydown="if(event.key==='Enter')login()"` (Enter submits); eye `onclick="togglePw('login-password',this)"`
- validation:  same combined check as email
- wired:       yes. `togglePw` (line 11782) flips input type and swaps the icon.
- data source: passed to `loginWithPassword`
- gaps:        none

**Button: Sign In →**
- element:     `<button>` id `btn-login`, classes `btn btn-primary btn-full`
- handler:     `onclick="login()"`
- effect:      disables button + sets text "Signing in…", clears error, calls `loginWithPassword`. On success → verified card. On `{ok:false}` → `showLoginError(r.error)`. On network failure → `showLoginError(err.message)`.
- wired:       yes

**Link: Forgot password?**
- element:     `<a>` (styled link), `onclick="showForgotPw()"`
- effect:      hides `#login-form-wrap`, shows `#forgot-pw-wrap`, status "Reset your password", pre-fills `#fp-email` from `#login-email` if typed.

**Button: Enter Dashboard →**
- element:     `<button id="btn-enter">` (starts `hidden`; revealed after verify)
- handler:     `onclick="enterDashboard()"`
- effect:      disables + spinner "Loading…", calls `getInitialPayload(APP._verifiedEmail)` → `onPayloadLoaded`. On failure re-enables and shows error.

**Verified-account card (`#login-user-card`)** — read-only display populated by `login()` success handler:
- `#luc-avatar` first-letter avatar · `#luc-name` · `#luc-email` · `#luc-role` (badge) · `#luc-team` · `#luc-designation` (hidden if none).

**Link: New here? Register →**
- element:     `<a onclick="openRegisterModal()">` (line 1755)
- effect:      opens the registration modal (see 2.2).

#### Forgot-password flow (2-step)

**Step 1 (`#fp-step1`)**

**Field: Email (reset)**
- element:     `<input type="email">` id `fp-email` class `fc`
- placeholder: `you@company.com`
- required:    yes (`sendResetCode` line 823: `if(!email) showLoginError('Please enter your email.')`)
- handlers:    `onkeydown` Enter → `sendResetCode()`
- wired:       yes → `requestPasswordReset(email)` (auth.gs 287). Backend finds active employee, generates 6-digit OTP, stores `pw_otp_{email}` in ScriptProperties with **15-minute** expiry (line 298), emails OTP via `GmailApp.sendEmail` (HTML template). Returns `{ok:false,error:'No active account found for this email.'}` if not found.

**Button: Send Reset Code** — id `btn-send-code`, `onclick="sendResetCode()"`. On success reveals Step 2, status "Code sent! Check your email.", focuses OTP.

**Step 2 (`#fp-step2`)**

**Field: 6-digit code**
- element:     `<input type="text" maxlength="6">` id `fp-otp` class `fc` (letter-spacing styling)
- placeholder: `6-digit code`
- required:    yes (`resetPasswordSubmit` line 845)
- handlers:    `onkeydown` Enter → focus `fp-newpw`
- validation:  client requires non-empty; backend `resetPasswordWithOTP` compares `data.otp !== String(otp).trim()` → "Invalid reset code…"; expiry check deletes the property and returns "Reset code expired…".

**Field: New password**
- element:     `<input type="password">` id `fp-newpw` + eye `togglePw('fp-newpw',this)`
- placeholder: `New password`
- required:    yes; client checks `length < 6` → "Password must be at least 6 characters." (line 847)
- handlers:    `onkeydown` Enter → focus `fp-confirmpw`

**Field: Confirm new password**
- element:     `<input type="password">` id `fp-confirmpw` + eye
- placeholder: `Confirm new password`
- required:    yes; client checks `newpw !== confirm` → "Passwords do not match." (line 848)
- handlers:    `onkeydown` Enter → `resetPasswordSubmit()`

**Button: Reset Password** — id `btn-reset-pw`, `onclick="resetPasswordSubmit()"` → `resetPasswordWithOTP(email, otp, newpw)` (auth.gs 321). Backend re-validates, `dbUpdate('Employees',…,{Password_Hash: hashPassword(newPassword)})`, deletes OTP property, audits `PASSWORD_RESET`. On success: `hideForgotPw()`, status "Password reset! Please sign in.", pre-fills email, focuses password.

**Link: Resend code** (`onclick="sendResetCode()"`) and **← Back to Sign In** (`onclick="hideForgotPw()"`, resets steps + clears fields).

`hashPassword` (auth.gs 261) = `SHA256(password + 'tms_2025')` hex — confirmed legacy salt-concat scheme; same as documented in CLAUDE.md.

> ⚠ **OTP is single-attempt-friendly but not rate-limited**: `requestPasswordReset` has no throttle / lockout — repeated calls overwrite the stored OTP each time. Brute-force of the 6-digit code over 15 min is unmitigated (no attempt counter). Severity: medium (security).
> ⚠ **No client length check on reset OTP**: `fp-otp` allows any text; backend trims and compares. Non-numeric input simply fails the compare. Acceptable but no inline format hint. Severity: low.

### 2.2 Registration Form — Field-by-Field

**Modal:** `#register-modal`
- trigger:     `openRegisterModal()` (app.js.html 7723), invoked from the login screen "Register →" link.

- close:       `✕` button `onclick="closeModal('register-modal')"`; **Cancel** ghost button (footer); backdrop click. `closeModal` (8355) adds `hidden`.

> ❌ **Mismatch: division select default option text**: HTML static markup has `<option value="">— Select Team —</option>` (index.html 3344) but `openRegisterModal` rewrites it to `— Select Division —` (app.js.html 7735). Label above is "Team Division *". Cosmetic inconsistency in placeholder wording. Severity: low.

- element:     `<input class="fc">` (type text default)

- placeholder: `First name`

- handlers:    (none)

**Field: Date of Birth **\***
- element:     `<input type="date" class="fc">`
- id/class:    `id="reg-dob"`
- placeholder: (none — native date picker)
- required:    yes (7909 `if(!dob) ...'Date of birth is required.'`)
- default:     (none)
- handlers:    (none)
- wired:       yes → `record.dob`
- gaps:        ⚠ Label/validation treat DOB as **required**, but the HTML label "Date of Birth *" is the only enforcement of "required" beyond the JS check; no min/max age validation. Severity: low.

**Field: Team Division **\***
- element:     `<select class="fc">`
- id/class:    `id="reg-division"`
- placeholder: static markup `— Select Team —`; runtime-rewritten to `— Select Division —`
- required:    yes (7910 `if(!division) ...'Please select a division.'`)
- default:     placeholder
- options:     **dynamically populated** from `Object.keys(TEAM_HIERARCHY)` (client copy, app.js.html 705–714):
  ```
  1. Founder's Office
  2. Student Success
  3. Knowledge
  4. Growth (Marketing)
  5. Tech
  6. Consulting
  7. Operations - PP & Admin
  8. Operations - FP&A
  ```
- handlers:    `onchange="onRegDivisionChange()"`
- wired:       yes. `onRegDivisionChange` (7762) repopulates sub-dept select for the chosen division AND calls `_regAutoFillManager()`. → `record.team`.
- data source: client `TEAM_HIERARCHY` constant
- gaps:        none (matches `setupSheets.gs` TEAM_HIERARCHY per CLAUDE.md org list)

**Field: Sub-department (if applicable)** — FLAGSHIP GAP CHECK
- element:     **`<select class="fc">`** (NOT an `<input>`) — index.html line 3349
- id/class:    `id="reg-subdept"`; wrapper `<div class="fg" id="fg-reg-subdept">`
- placeholder: first option `<option value="">— Select Sub-department —</option>`
- required:    no (label says "(if applicable)"; no `submitRegistration` check)
- default:     placeholder
- options:     **dynamically populated AND cascaded** from the division select. `onRegDivisionChange` (7762–7771) reads `TEAM_HIERARCHY[div]` and rebuilds `#reg-subdept`'s options; if the division has no sub-depts the array is empty (e.g. "3. Knowledge" → `[]`) and the wrapper opacity drops to `0.4`. Example for "5. Tech": `5a. Product`, `5b. Development`, `5c. Maintenance`.
- handlers:    `onchange="onRegSubdeptChange()"` → `_regAutoFillManager()` (re-resolves manager when sub-dept narrows the lookup).
- wired:       **yes — it IS a `<select>` and it DOES cascade off the division select.** This contradicts any PRD/scaffold claim that `reg-subdept` is a free-text input or non-cascading. The cascade is implemented correctly. → `record.sub_department`.
- data source: client `TEAM_HIERARCHY[division]`
- gaps:        ✅ No gap on the cascade itself. ⚠ Minor: the wrapper is only dimmed (opacity 0.4), not disabled, when a no-subdept division is chosen — a user could still expand the (placeholder-only) select. Cosmetic. Severity: low.

**Field: Manager's / Reports-to Email **\* (dynamic label)**
- element:     `<input type="email" class="fc">`
- id/class:    `id="reg-manager-email"`; label `id="lbl-reg-manager"`; hint `id="reg-manager-hint"`
- placeholder: static `Your direct manager's email`; rewritten by `onRegRoleChange`/`_regAutoFillManager` (e.g. "Looking up manager…", "Email of who you report to (leave blank if none)", "No manager assigned for this team").
- required:    conditional — required for all roles EXCEPT Super Admin (7912 `if(!managerEmail && role!=='Super Admin')`).
- default:     (none) / auto-filled
- handlers:    no direct handler on the input — it is driven by `onRegRoleChange` (role) and `onRegDivisionChange`/`onRegSubdeptChange` (team/subdept).
- behaviour:   `_REG_MANUAL_ROLES = ['Super Admin','Admin','Team Captain']` (7778). For these roles the field is editable manual entry (label "Reports-to Email" / "Reports-to Email *"). For **Team Member / Team Facilitator / Intern** the field is **auto-filled and made `readOnly`** by `_regAutoFillManager` (7818) via a `getTeamCaptainByTeam(team, subDept)` lookup:
    - success → green border, value = manager email, hint "✓ Auto-filled: {name} (Manager)", submit enabled.
    - no manager → red border, value cleared, placeholder "No manager assigned for this team", **submit DISABLED**, hint "No manager is assigned for this team yet. Please contact the admin."
    - network/script failure → field unlocked (fail-open) so user can type manually, submit enabled.
- validation:  conditional-required (above).
- wired:       yes → `record.manager_email`
- gaps:        ⚠ The success path keeps `inp.readOnly = true` and disables editing — a TM/TF cannot override a wrongly-resolved manager unless the GAS call fails. Severity: low.

**Field: Message (optional)**
- element:     `<textarea class="fc" rows="3">`
- id/class:    `id="reg-message"`
- placeholder: `Tell your manager why you need access...`
- required:    no
- handlers:    (none)
- wired:       yes → `record.message`

**Error banner:** `#reg-error` (hidden div) — populated by `showRegError` inside `submitRegistration`.

**Buttons (footer):**
- **Cancel** — `btn btn-ghost`, `onclick="closeModal('register-modal')"`.
- **Submit Registration** — `id="btn-submit-reg"` `btn btn-primary`, `onclick="submitRegistration()"`. (Note: also the name of a backend GAS function — frontend wrapper is the global JS `submitRegistration()` at 7880; the GAS call inside it is `.submitRegistration(record)`.)

**`submitRegistration()` (frontend, 7880) → GAS `submitRegistration(record)` (auth.gs 1597):**
- params:      `record = {first_name,last_name,email,password,role,designation,dob,team,sub_department,manager_email,message}` (7917–7929)
- success:     `_btnReset(btn)`; if `!result.ok` → inline error; else `closeModal('register-modal')` + `toast('Registration submitted! Your manager will review your request.','success')`.
- failure:     `showRegError(err.message || 'Registration failed. Please try again.')`.
- return shape: `{ok:true, reqId}` or `{ok:false, error}`.
- backend behaviour: hashes password server-side (accepts pre-hashed `password_hash` OR raw `password`), requires `first_name,last_name,email,passwordHash,role,team` (line 1606), dedups against Employees (1612) and pending Registration_Requests (1621), inserts a `Registration_Requests` row with `Status:'Pending'` (1631–1650). **No email/notification is sent to the manager on submission.**

> ⚠ **PRD intent vs actual — "your manager will review"**: the success toast and the `manager_email`/`Manager_Email` plumbing imply the manager is notified, but `submitRegistration` (auth.gs) sends NO email and writes no notification. Discovery is pull-only (manager must open Team-Mgmt/Org page). Severity: medium.

---

### 2.3 Registration Manager Resolution

`getTeamCaptainByTeam(team, subDept)` (auth.gs 1525) is called from `_regAutoFillManager` to resolve the manager email for TM/TF/Intern registrations. **Actual fallback order (verified in source):**

```
function getTeamCaptainByTeam(team, subDept) {
  try {
    if (!team) return { ok: true, email: '', name: '' };
    ...
```

| Step | Condition | Returns |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | `!team` | `{ok:true, email:'', name:''}` (no-op, empty) |
| 1a | role `Team Captain` whose `Team` == team **AND** `Sub_Department` == subDept (only attempted if subDept provided) | that TC |
| 1b | role `Team Captain` whose `Team` == team (team-only widen) | that TC |
| 2 | Script Property `LGD_DEFAULT_MANAGER_EMAIL` set | `{email: LGD_DEFAULT_MANAGER_EMAIL, name: LGD_DEFAULT_MANAGER_NAME || email}` |
| 3a | any active `Super Admin` | that SA |
| 3b | any active `Admin` | that Admin |
| 4 | none of the above | `{ok:false, error:'No Team Captain, Admin, or Super Admin found…'}` |

So the canonical order is:
**sub-dept TC → team TC → `LGD_DEFAULT_MANAGER_EMAIL` → Super Admin → Admin → fail.** This matches the slice brief exactly.

> ❌ **Frontend comment claims a DIFFERENT order**: `_regAutoFillManager`'s header comment (app.js.html 7814) states *"priority: TC → Admin → Super Admin → TF"*. That is wrong on two counts vs the actual backend: (a) the real order is Super Admin **before** Admin (Step 3a before 3b), and (b) there is no Team-Facilitator fallback at all, and the configured default-manager Script Property step is omitted entirely from the comment. The comment is stale/misleading. Severity: low (doc/comment only — backend behaviour is correct).

**`setDefaultManager()` (auth.gs 1590)** — one-time GAS-editor helper that hardcodes the fallback:
```
props.setProperty('LGD_DEFAULT_MANAGER_EMAIL', 'priyankadugar.lg@gmail.com');
props.setProperty('LGD_DEFAULT_MANAGER_NAME',  'Priyanka Dugar');
```
> ⚠ A specific person's email is hardcoded in source as the default manager. Changing it requires editing source and re-running the function (documented in the function comment). Severity: low.

Note: `getTeamCaptainByTeam` only returns the TC sub-dept match for `Role === 'Team Captain'` exactly; Team Facilitators are **never** auto-resolved as managers despite the frontend label/comment implying a TF could be. The hint shown to the user always says "(Manager)" regardless of which fallback tier produced the email.

---

### 2.4 Admin Approval Flow

Pending registrations render in two places (containers in index.html):
- **Team Management view** (`#view-team-mgmt`, line 2351) → `<div id="team-pending-registrations">` (2353). Populated by `_loadPendingRegs('team-pending-registrations', true)` called from `renderTeamMgmt` (app.js.html line 6348). `myOnly=true`.
- **Organisation view** (`#view-org-page`, line 2365) → `<div id="org-pending-registrations">` (2367). Populated by `_loadPendingRegs('org-pending-registrations', false)` called from `renderOrgPage` (line 6387). `myOnly=false`.

**`_loadPendingRegs(elId, myOnly)` (6108)** → `getRegistrationRequests(APP.currentUser.email, myOnly)` (auth.gs 1659):
- Non-managers → `{ok:true, requests:[]}` (nothing shown).
- Admin/SA with `myOnly=false` → ALL pending requests.
- TC/TF (or admin with myOnly) → requests where `Manager_Email === email` OR `Team === user's team` (so a TF sees the TC's incoming requests for the same team).

Each request renders as a `.reg-card` (`_renderRegCards`, 6080) showing name, email, role pill, designation, team, manager email, requested date, optional message, and two buttons:

**Button: Approve** — `onclick="approveReg(this, '{Req_ID}')"`
- `approveReg` (6418): `confirm('Approve this registration request?')` → `_btnLoading` → `approveRegistration(reqId, APP.currentUser.email)`.
- backend `approveRegistration` (auth.gs 1683): requires `_isManager`; non-admins must be the designated manager OR a same-team manager; resolves manager Emp_ID via `getEmployeeByEmail(Manager_Email)`; generates `EMP-###`; **inserts an Employees row** (`Is_Active:'TRUE'`, copies First/Last/Email/Role/Designation/Team/Sub_Department/Password_Hash/DOB) (1710–1725); `invalidateOrgTreeCache()`; attempts `_tryAddToTeamSpace(email, team)` (Chat space add, wrapped in try/catch); marks the request `Approved` with reviewer/time; audits `APPROVE_REG`.
- return: `{ok:true, empId}`.
- frontend reaction: toast `'Registration approved! Employee ID: {empId}'`, `refresh()`, then re-render the current view.

**Button: Reject** — `onclick="rejectReg(this, '{Req_ID}')"`
- `rejectReg` (6474): `prompt('Reason for rejection (optional):')` (null = cancel) → `rejectRegistration(reqId, email, notes)`.
- backend `rejectRegistration` (auth.gs 1743): same authorization gate; marks request `Rejected` with notes/reviewer/time; audits `REJECT_REG`. Returns `{ok:true}`.
- frontend reaction: toast `'Registration rejected.'` ('warn'), re-render view (note: `rejectReg` does NOT call `refresh()`, only re-renders the page — acceptable since no employee was created).

**Account creation:** happens entirely inside `approveRegistration` — the registrant's password hash was captured at submission and carried into the new Employees row, so the user can sign in immediately after approval with the password they chose.

> ❌ **No email is sent on approval or rejection.** `approveRegistration` and `rejectRegistration` write the sheet + audit but never `GmailApp.sendEmail` the applicant. The applicant is not told they were approved (and given their Emp_ID) or rejected (with the reason). They must simply try to log in. PRD-style "your manager will review your request" workflow has no closing notification loop. Severity: medium. Status: present-in-intent / missing-in-code.
> ⚠ **Same-team-manager approval is broad**: any TC/TF in the same team (not just the designated `Manager_Email`) can approve/reject a request, including a request that self-selected `Role: Admin` or `Super Admin`. Combined with the role-self-select gap (2.2), a Team Captain could approve someone who registered as Admin. Severity: medium.


## PART 3: MY SPACE

The "My Space" group covers the two personal landing surfaces every user sees: the **Dashboard** (`#view-dashboard`, the default view loaded on login) and **Plan My Week** (`#view-plan-week`). Both are pure client-render views dispatched from `navigate()` (`app.js.html` lines 1143–1144): `if (view === 'dashboard') renderDashboard();` and `if (view === 'plan-week') renderPlanWeek();`. Neither view is role-gated at the navigate layer (any authenticated user can open both); individual *widgets* inside the dashboard are role-gated client-side.

Sidebar nav entries (`index.html` 1801–1805):
- Dashboard — `<div class="nav-item active" data-view="dashboard">`, icon `home` (Material Symbols).
- Plan My Week — `<div class="nav-item" data-view="plan-week">`, icon `calendar_view_week`, label "Plan My Week".

---

### 3.1 Dashboard

`renderDashboard()` is defined in `app.js.html` lines 1170–1268. It reads `APP.currentUser`, `APP.tasks`, `APP.projects` from in-memory global state (populated by `getInitialPayload`/`validateSession`). It renders a greeting, a stats grid, fires the clock widget, renders upcoming-task buckets and the My Projects panel synchronously, then makes **three async `google.script.run` calls** to fill the remaining widgets: `getDashboardExtras`, `getTeamClockStatus` (via `_teamClockLoad`), and `getMyWlWeekSummary` (via `_wlLoadWidgetWeek`). Lines read for this section: app.js.html 1170–1656, 9072–9145, 11135–11167, 16082–16184; index.html 1888–2032; dashboard.gs 1–375; work-duration.gs 552–626; auth.gs 1320–1331.

### 3.1.0 View header — greeting, date, action buttons

| Element | id | Source | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Greeting | `dash-greeting` | client: `new Date().getHours()` → "Good morning/afternoon/evening, " + `u.name` + "!" | Cutoffs: <12 morning, <17 afternoon, else evening (line 1175). |
| Date line | `dash-date` | `new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-IN', {weekday,day,month,year})` | Line 1177. |
| Forms button | `dash-btn-forms` | static, `class="btn btn-outline nav-mgr-only hidden"`, `onclick="navigate('forms')"` | Visibility toggled elsewhere by `nav-mgr-only` gating. Hidden on mobile (CLAUDE.md feature gating). |
| Clock-in header widget | wrapper `wd-hdr-wrap`; button `wd-hdr-btn` `onclick="_wdTogglePopup(event)"`; dot `wd-hdr-dot`; label `wd-hdr-label`; popup `wd-popup` containing `wd-widget` | Populated by `loadWorkDuration()` → `wdGetStatus` GAS call (work-duration.gs). | This is the Work-Duration clock; full field detail belongs to the Work Duration module slice. On the dashboard `renderDashboard` simply calls `loadWorkDuration()` (line 1213) which calls `.wdGetStatus(APP._verifiedEmail)` and on success runs `_wdRenderStatus()`, `_wdUpdateHeaderBtn()`, `_wdStartTimer()` (app.js.html 15290–15305). |
| "Log Today's Work" button | static `class="btn btn-accent"`, `onclick="navigate('work-log')"`, icon `edit_note` | n/a | Always shown. |
| WL week mini-widget container | `wl-widget-container` | filled by `_wlLoadWidgetWeek()` | See 3.1.7. |

### 3.1.1 Stats grid (`#dash-stats`)

Rendered synchronously (lines 1204–1210) via `statCard(label,val,sub)` (line 1649). Built from `myTasks` = `APP.tasks` filtered to those where current user's `empId` is in `Assignee_IDs`/`Assignee_ID` (`_parseIds`, line 1179).

| Card | Value computed from | Helper |
|---|---|---|
| My Tasks · "total assigned" | `myTasks.length` | — |
| Open · "pending" | `myTasks.filter(_isOpen)` | `_isOpen(s) = !_isClosed(s)` (line 703) — i.e. anything not Done/Completed/Cancelled/Implemented (includes WIP, Review, On Hold, Yet to Start). ⚠️ Label "Open / pending" is broad: it counts in-progress and on-hold tasks too, not only un-started ones. |
| In Progress · "active now" | `myTasks.filter(_isActive)` | `_isActive(s) = String(s).indexOf('WIP')===0 \|\| s==='WIP'` (line 702). Only WIP-prefixed statuses; excludes Review/Planning. |
| Under Review · "awaiting approval" | `myTasks.filter(x => x.Status === 'On Hold')` | ⚠️ Card is labelled "Under Review" but the filter matches **`On Hold`**, NOT the `Review` status (line 1182). The actual `Review` status is never counted here. Mislabel/mismatch. |
| Done · "finished" | `myTasks.filter(_isDone)` | `_isDone(s)` = Done/Completed/Implemented (line 700). |
| Projects · "involved in" | `myProjs.length` | project ids derived from my tasks + their parent projects (lines 1185–1210). |

Data source: `APP.tasks` / `APP.projects` (in-memory). No GAS call. Hidden on mobile (`#dash-stats` `display:none` in media query + IIFE bypass; CLAUDE.md feature gating).

### 3.1.2 Notice Board (`#dash-notice-board`)

- **Render:** placeholder "Loading..." set (line 1234), then filled by `_renderNoticeBoard(notices)` (lines 1357–1403) from `getDashboardExtras` success handler (line 1247).
- **GAS call:** `getDashboardExtras(email)` (dashboard.gs line 4). Signature confirmed:
  ```js
  function getDashboardExtras(email) {
    try {
      var user    = getCurrentUser(email);
      var allEmps = [];
  ```
  Returns `{ ok, notices, onLeave, scores, scoreScope }`. Notices assembled by `_getNotices(user, allEmps)` (dashboard.gs 29–227).
- **Notice sources (server-side, `_getNotices`):**
  1. Manual announcements from `Announcements` sheet — filtered by `Is_Active`, `Expires_At >= today`, `Target_Date <= today`, and `Visibility` role-gate (`TCs Only` → Admin+Team Captain; `TCs & TFs` → +Team Facilitator; `Organisation` → all). `manual:true`.
  2. Holidays from `Holidays` sheet — shown from 2 days before through the holiday date.
  3. Birthdays — `Employees.DOB` MM-DD == today.
  4. Upcoming meetings — Google Calendar via `UrlFetchApp.fetchAll` of two `privateExtendedProperty=tm_type=company`/`=team` queries, today→+30d.
  5. Active forms — `Forms` sheet where `Is_Active=TRUE`, `Status='Active'`, visibility `Team` matches user's team. Sets `formUrl: r['Responder_URL']`.
  - Sorted Urgent→High→Normal then by date.
- **Card rendering (`_renderNoticeBoard`):** icon via `_NB_TYPE_ICON` map `{ General:'info', Emergency:'warning', Holiday:'celebration', Birthday:'cake', Reminder:'notifications', Meeting:'video_call', Form:'description' }` (Material Symbols). URGENT badge when `priority==='Urgent'`. Visibility badge shown when `n.visibility && !== 'Organisation'`. Admins (`_isAdmin`) additionally see "Visible until {expiresAt}". Meeting cards are clickable (`onclick="navigate('meetings')"`) and render Join (`n.meetLink`) / View (`goToMeeting(n.meetId)`) buttons. Form cards render a "Fill Form" link to `n.formUrl`. Delete button (`_nbDelete`) shown only when `isAdmin && n.manual`.
- **Empty state:** Material Symbol `notifications_none` + "No announcements right now".
- **Failure handler:** all three async widgets show "Could not load data." (lines 1251–1256).

#### Post Announcement panel (`#nb-post-panel`) — admin only

Trigger: "Post" button `#btn-nb-post` (`class="btn btn-sm btn-primary nav-admin-only hidden"`, `onclick="_nbTogglePost()"`). `_nbTogglePost()` (lines 1405–1431) toggles `.hidden`, and on open pre-fills `nb-date`=today, `nb-visibility`='Organisation', `ann-start-date`=today, `ann-end-date`=today+7, focuses `nb-title`.

Fields:

Field: **Type**
- element: `<select class="fc">`
- id/class: `nb-type` / `fc`
- placeholder: (none)
- required: no
- default: `General` (first option)
- options (static, verbatim): `<option>General</option><option>Emergency</option><option>Reminder</option>`
- handlers: none (read in `_nbSave`)

- gaps: ⚠️ The `_NB_TYPE_ICON` map and server-side notice types include `Holiday`, `Birthday`, `Meeting`, `Form` — but the post dropdown only allows General/Emergency/Reminder. Admins cannot manually post `Holiday`/`Meeting`/etc types (those are auto-generated). Acceptable by design but worth noting.

- effect (`_annApplyTemplate`, lines 1472–1502): sets `nb-visibility`, `ann-start-date`=today, `ann-end-date`=today+7/+14/+30, `nb-priority`=Normal/High/High respectively.

`_nbSave(btn)` (lines 1433–1470): builds `record { type, priority, title, message, visibility, startDate, endDate }`, calls `_btnLoading(btn,'Posting…')`, then:
- GAS: `createAnnouncement(record, APP._verifiedEmail)` (dashboard.gs 229).
  ```js
  function createAnnouncement(record, email) {
    try {
      var user = getCurrentUser(email);
      if (!_isAdmin(user.role)) throw new Error('Only admins can post announcements.');
      if (!record.title) return { ok: false, error: 'Title is required.' };
  ```
  Returns `{ ok:true, annId }`.
- success: `_btnReset(btn)`, toast "Announcement posted!", `_nbTogglePost()`, clears `nb-title`/`nb-message`, resets type/priority/visibility/dates, then re-fetches `getDashboardExtras` and re-renders notice board.
- failure: `_btnReset(btn)`, toast error.

`_nbDelete(btn, annId)` (lines 1504–1517): `confirm('Remove this announcement?')`, then GAS `deleteAnnouncement(annId, email)` (dashboard.gs 272, admin-only, soft-deletes via `Is_Active:'FALSE'`). On success toast + re-fetch `getDashboardExtras` → re-render.

### 3.1.3 On Leave Today (`#dash-on-leave`)

- **Render:** `_renderOnLeave(onLeave)` (lines 1520–1544) from `getDashboardExtras` success (line 1248).
- **Data source:** `_getOnLeaveToday(allEmps)` (dashboard.gs 284–312) — reads `Leaves` sheet, filters `Status==='Approved'` and `Start_Date <= today <= End_Date`, maps to `{empId,name,team,leaveType,endDate}` sorted by name.
- **Card:** avatar initials, name, "{leaveType} · {team}", and a "Back {date+1}" chip computed client-side from `endDate`+1 day.
- **Empty state:** Material Symbol `groups` + "Everyone is in today!".
- **Role gating:** none — all roles see the full org's approved leaves for today.
- wired: yes.

First project auto-selected and its tree rendered via `_renderProjHierarchy(projList[0].Proj_ID)`.

### 3.1.5 My Upcoming Tasks buckets (`#dash-my-tasks`)

- **Render:** `_dashRenderUpcoming('dash-my-tasks')` (lines 9072–9145), invoked in a `try/catch` at line 1216.
- **Data:** `APP.tasks` filtered to non-closed (`!_isClosed`) and assigned to current user (`_parseIds(t.Assignee_IDs)` contains `empId`), sorted by due date. ⚠️ Filter checks **only `Assignee_IDs`** (not `Assigner_ID`), so tasks the user *created but is not assigned to* do NOT appear in the dashboard upcoming list — unlike Plan My Week (3.2) which includes assigner tasks. Inconsistency between the two surfaces.
- **Buckets:** `_tskBucketByDate(tasks)` (lines 8870–8904) → overdue / today / thisWeek / nextWeek / later / noDate (string compare on YYYY-MM-DD). Rendered via `_ubSection(...)` collapsible sections. Open/closed state tracked in `_dashUbOpen = { overdue:true, today:true, thisweek:true, nextweek:true, later:false }` (line 8769), toggled by `dashUbToggle(key)` (lines 9063–9070).
- **Section rows:** `_tskDateRowHtml(task, isOverdue)` (lines 8807–8867) — priority bar/badge, title, "fnLabel · by assignerName", progress bar (`_tskProgHtml`), status pill (dbl-click → `tskStatusDblClick`), due-date dot. Row click → `openTaskDetail(Task_ID)`.

- 🔴 **BROKEN ICONS:** This widget uses Tabler-style `ti ti-*` classes for ALL its icons, but no Tabler webfont is loaded (Material Symbols is the only icon font). These render as **blank/empty space**:
  - `ti ti-sort-descending-2` (header "My Upcoming Tasks", line 9123)
  - `ti ti-arrow-right` (×2, "View all"/"Open weekly plan" links, 9125 & 9141)
  - `ti ti-alert-triangle` (Overdue section icon, 9127)
  - `ti ti-calendar-event` (Today, 9129)
  - `ti ti-calendar-week` (×2, This week / Next week, 9131 & 9133)
  - `ti ti-calendar-time` (Later, 9135)
  - `ti ti-chevron-down` (collapse chevron `dash-ub-{key}-ico`, line 9106)
  - These are the stray "ti ti-*" gaps called out in the brief's ground truths.

### 3.1.6 Scoreboard (`#dash-scoreboard`, wrapper `#dash-scoreboard-wrap`)

- **Title:** `#dash-sb-title` set synchronously from client role (lines 1226–1231): `_isAdmin`→"Company Scoreboard", `_isManager`→"Team Scoreboard", else "Personal Scoreboard".
- **Render:** `_renderScoreboard(scores, scope)` (lines 1549–1611) from `getDashboardExtras` success (line 1249, `d.scores` + `d.scoreScope`).
- **Data:** `_getScores(user, allEmps)` (dashboard.gs 320–374). Scope: Admin→`company` (all active emps), Manager→`team` (`getSubordinateIds` + self), else→`self`. Per-emp computes done (`Done`/`Completed`), inProg (status starts "WIP"), overdue, and `score = max(0, done*10 + inProg*3 - overdue*5)`.
  ```js
  function _getScores(user, allEmps) {
    var today      = _todayStr();
    var monthStart = today.substring(0, 7) + '-01';
    var tasks    = []; try { tasks    = dbGetAll('Tasks');    } catch(e) {}
  ```
- **`self` scope:** single-card layout via `_sbStatTile` — Score / Tasks Done / Overdue / Logs This Month, plus formula caption.
- **`company`/`team` scope:** ranked table (`.sb-table`) columns: #, Employee, Team, Score, Done, Overdue, Logs (mo.). Top-3 medal emojis 🥇🥈🥉. Current user row highlighted (`sb-me`, "(you)"). "Show All N Members" toggle (`_sbToggleAll`, line 1620) when >10.
- 🔴 / ⚠️ **Stale "Logs" data + caption mismatch:**
  - Server-side `logs` is hard-coded to `0` (Work_Log read removed for performance, dashboard.gs 324–325, 365) and the score formula no longer includes a logs term. But:
    - The `Logs (mo.)` table column and the `self`-scope "Logs This Month" tile always display **0** for everyone (dead column).
    - The caption (line 1570) still reads: **"Score = done×10 + in-progress×3 + attendance logs×2 − overdue×5"** — the `+ attendance logs×2` term no longer exists in the actual formula (`done*10 + inProg*3 - overdue*5`). Misleading caption. Gap.
- **Empty state:** `emptyState('No data yet','')`.
- wired: partial (logs column/tile dead; caption inaccurate).
- Hidden on mobile (`#dash-scoreboard-wrap` `display:none`).

### 3.1.8 Team Clock Status widget (`#dash-team-clock`, body `#dash-team-clock-body`)

- **Trigger/render:** `_teamClockLoad()` (lines 16089–16115), invoked at end of `renderDashboard` (line 1260).
- **Role gating (client):** if `!_isManager && !_isAdmin` → `widget.classList.add('hidden')` and return (TM never sees it). Otherwise unhides and shows "Loading...".
- **GAS call:** `getTeamClockStatus(APP._verifiedEmail)` (work-duration.gs 552):
  ```js
  function getTeamClockStatus(email) {
    try {
      var user = getCurrentUser(email);
      if (!_isManager(user.role) && !_isAdmin(user.role)) {
        return { ok: false, error: 'Access denied' };
  ```
  Admins → all active emps; TC/TF → `_getTeamEmpIds(user)` set. Returns `{ ok, data:[{empId,name,avatar,status,clockInTs,totalBreakMins,netWorkMins}] }` sorted active→on_break→completed→not_clocked_in.
- **Render (`_teamClockRender`, lines 16122–16184):** per-member card with avatar, name, status dot (colour via `statusColor` map: active=ok, on_break=warn, completed=muted, not_clocked_in=border), status label ("Clocked In"/"On Break"/"Done"/"Not In"), and a live "Elapsed" ticker (`tck-{empId}`) updating every 1s = `Date.now() - clockInMs - breakMs` via `_wdFmtMs`. Tickers stored in `_TEAM_CLOCK_INTERVALS` (line 16082).
- **Refresh button:** `#dash-team-clock` header has refresh button `onclick="_teamClockRefresh()"` (stops tickers + reloads).
- **Cleanup:** `navigate()` calls `_teamClockStopTickers()` whenever leaving the dashboard (line 1121).
- **Empty state:** "No team members found."
- wired: yes.
