### 3.2 Plan My Week

View `#view-plan-week` (index.html 2029–2032) — two containers only: `#pw-header` and `#pw-body`. Entry: `navigate('plan-week')` → `renderPlanWeek()` (app.js.html 8915–8919). No role gating. Lines read: app.js.html 8768–9057. Module-level state: `var _PW_ANCHOR = null;` (line 8768) — the Monday of the currently displayed week.

### 3.2.1 Behaviour

`renderPlanWeek()`:
```js
function renderPlanWeek() {
  if (!_PW_ANCHOR) _PW_ANCHOR = _mlWeekMonday(new Date());
  _pwRenderHeader();
  _pwRenderBody();
}
```
On first open `_PW_ANCHOR` is normalised to the Monday of the current week (`_mlWeekMonday`). Subsequent navigation mutates `_PW_ANCHOR` and re-renders.

### 3.2.2 Header (`#pw-header`) — `_pwRenderHeader()` (lines 8921–8938)

- Title: "Plan my week"
- Subtitle: "Tasks organised by due date — click any task to open it"
- Week label: `{_tskDateLabel(Mon)} – {_tskDateLabel(Sun)} {year}` (e.g. "23 Jun – 29 Jun 2026").

Navigation controls (buttons, `class="btn btn-outline btn-sm"`):

Field: **Previous week**
- element: button "‹"
- onclick: `_pwPrevWeek()` (line 9055) → `_PW_ANCHOR -= 7 days`, re-render header+body.
- wired: yes

Field: **Next week**
- element: button "›"
- onclick: `_pwNextWeek()` (line 9056) → `_PW_ANCHOR += 7 days`, re-render.
- wired: yes

Field: **Today**
- element: button "Today"
- onclick: `_pwGoToday()` (line 9057) → `_PW_ANCHOR = _mlWeekMonday(new Date())`, re-render.
- wired: yes

### 3.2.3 Body (`#pw-body`) — `_pwRenderBody()` (lines 8940–8942 guard, 8943–9042)

- **Task source:** `APP.tasks` filtered (lines 8945–8950) to non-closed (`!_isClosed`) AND (`_parseIds(Assignee_IDs)` contains `empId` OR `Assigner_ID === empId`). ⚠️ Note divergence from dashboard upcoming (3.1.5) which is assignee-only — Plan My Week *also* includes tasks the user assigned (created). The two "my tasks" surfaces use different inclusion rules.
- **Buckets (computed against `anchorStr` = the displayed week's Monday, NOT today):**
  - **Overdue:** tasks with `Due_Date < anchorStr`, sorted ascending. ⚠️ "Overdue" is relative to the *displayed week's* Monday, not today. Navigating to a future week reclassifies all earlier-week tasks (including ones not yet due as of today) as "overdue". Navigating to a past week shows fewer/no overdue. This is week-relative, which may confuse users expecting "overdue = past today".
  - **Day rows Mon–Sun (`byDay[0..6]`):** tasks whose `Due_Date` equals each day in the anchored week; sorted by priority (Critical→High→Medium→Low via `priOrder`). Today's row highlighted blue (`#ddeaf5`) with a "Today" pill; weekend rows (i≥5) dimmed (`opacity:.85`) with "Weekend" label; non-empty non-today days show a red count badge; empty days show "Nothing due today" (today) / "No tasks due".
  - **No due date:** tasks with no parseable `Due_Date`, listed under a "No due date" header.
- **Row rendering:** each task via `_tskDateRowHtml(t, isOverdue)` (same component as dashboard buckets — priority bar/badge, title, "fn · by assigner", progress bar, status pill with dbl-click edit, due-date dot, click → `openTaskDetail`).
- **Overdue header:** `_pwBucketHeader('Overdue', count)` (lines 9044–9053).
- **Error handling:** whole body wrapped in try/catch → on error renders "Error loading weekly plan. Check console." (line 9040). `pw-body` missing → console error + return.

### 3.2.4 Gaps in Plan My Week

- 🔴 **BROKEN ICONS (Tabler `ti ti-*`, no font loaded → blank):**
  - `ti ti-alert-triangle` — Overdue header (`_pwBucketHeader`, line 9049).
  - `ti ti-calendar-off` — "No due date" header (line 9029).
  - Both render as empty space.
- ⚠️ "Overdue" is week-relative (vs `_PW_ANCHOR` Monday) not today-relative — see 3.2.3.
- ⚠️ No empty-state for a completely empty week other than per-day "No tasks due"; if the user has zero tasks the view still renders 7 empty day rows + empty overdue — acceptable but bare.
- ⚠️ No way to add/create/reschedule a task from Plan My Week (read-only planning view despite the "Plan" name; the only interactions are open-detail and dbl-click status edit). PRD intent of "plan" implies drag/reschedule which is absent — feature is a read-only weekly digest.

### 3.2.5 google.script.run calls in this slice

| Function | Called from | Params | Success handler | Failure handler | DOM side-effects |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `getDashboardExtras` | renderDashboard (1237), `_nbSave` (1464), `_nbDelete` (1511) | `APP._verifiedEmail` | `_renderNoticeBoard` + `_renderOnLeave` + `_renderScoreboard` (or notice-only on re-fetch) | sets "Could not load data." on 3 containers | fills notice/on-leave/scoreboard |
| `getTeamClockStatus` | `_teamClockLoad` (16114) | `APP._verifiedEmail` | `_teamClockRender(r.data)` | "Failed to load team status." | fills #dash-team-clock-body + starts tickers |
| `getMyWlWeekSummary` | `_wlLoadWidgetWeek` (1350) | `email, isoStart, isoEnd` | rebuild `#wl-widget-container` via `_buildWlWeekWidget` | console.error, restore opacity | week mini-widget |
| `wdGetStatus` | `loadWorkDuration` (15304) | `APP._verifiedEmail` | `_wdRenderStatus/_wdUpdateHeaderBtn/_wdStartTimer` | toast | clock header widget |
| `createAnnouncement` | `_nbSave` (1469) | `record, APP._verifiedEmail` | toast + close panel + re-fetch | `_btnReset` + toast | notice board |
| `deleteAnnouncement` | `_nbDelete` (1516) | `annId, APP._verifiedEmail` | toast + re-fetch | `_btnReset` + toast | notice board |
| (none) | renderPlanWeek | — | — | — | all client-side from `APP.tasks` |

> Note: **Plan My Week makes NO server calls** — it is a pure client render over the already-loaded `APP.tasks`. It will not reflect task changes made by others until the next full `getInitialPayload`/refresh.


### 3.3 My Tasks (Filter Bar, Task Table, Inline Status Edit, Task Detail Modal)

> Source read: `src/app.js.html` lines 335-340 (`TASK_STATUSES`), 473-708 (`_ssm*` multi-select + `_TSK_COL_SPEC` + status helpers), 1659-1753 (`renderMyTasks`/`renderMyProjects`), 3222-3330 (`_ASGN_FILTER`/`setAsgnFilter`/`_applyAsgnProjFilter`), 5263-5447 (inline status edit + `_renderGroupedRow`), 5451-5599 (search/sort/rerender), 5601-5745 (colgroup/filter-bar build/inject), 5950-5989 (clear + select-all), 6494-6705 (`openTaskDetail` + progress timeline), 8576-8760 (col-filter helpers + cascade `_populateTskFilterSelects`), 8770-8773 + 9147-9217 (`_TSK_GRP_MODE`/`lgd_grp_*` + group toggle + date-grouped render), 9220-9258 (week-grouped render), 10964-11106 (project cards/action btns/group block), 11109-11131 (`_renderProjGrouped`). `src/index.html` lines 2035-2111 (My Tasks + My Projects view shells), 2711-2744 (task-detail-modal). `src/auth.gs` lines 898-909 (`updateTask` signature).

The **My Tasks** view (`#view-my-tasks`, index.html 2035-2095) is rendered by `renderMyTasks()` (app.js.html 1659-1715). It hosts: an assignment-relation tab strip (All / To Me / By Me), a group-by toggle (Function / Date / Week), an injected aligned filter bar, the grouped task table, and an alternate (date/week) body container.

#### 3.3.1 Assignment-relation tab strip (`.asgn-tab`)

Three buttons in `.ph-actions > .tl-tabs` (index.html 2040-2042):

| Label | element | onclick | data-vid | data-val | effect |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| All | `<button class="tl-tab asgn-tab active">` | `setAsgnFilter('my-tsk','all')` | `my-tsk` | `all` | rows = `toMe \|\| byMe` |
| To Me | `<button class="tl-tab asgn-tab">` | `setAsgnFilter('my-tsk','to-me')` | `my-tsk` | `to-me` | rows = `Assignee_IDs` contains my Emp_ID |
| By Me | `<button class="tl-tab asgn-tab">` | `setAsgnFilter('my-tsk','by-me')` | `my-tsk` | `by-me` | rows = `Assigner_ID === my Emp_ID` |

- **wired:** yes. `setAsgnFilter(vid,val)` (3230-3240) sets `_ASGN_FILTER['my-tsk']`, toggles `.active` on the matching tab, then re-invokes `renderMyTasks`. Filtering logic in `renderMyTasks` 1671-1675 reads `_ASGN_FILTER['my-tsk']`: `toMe = _parseIds(t.Assignee_IDs || t.Assignee_ID).indexOf(u.empId) !== -1`; `byMe = t.Assigner_ID === u.empId`; default `all` = `toMe || byMe`.
- **note:** there is also an `af === 'team'` branch in `renderMyTasks` (1666-1669) but **no UI tab emits `'team'`** for `my-tsk` — only `all`/`to-me`/`by-me` exist in this strip. ⚠️ Dead branch (matches CLAUDE.md "Recent Changes #12" which documents only the three tabs).
- **badge:** `#badge-my-tasks` set to `openCount` (count of `_isOpen(t.Status)`); hidden when 0 (1678-1680).

#### 3.3.2 Group-by toggle (`#my-tasks-grp-toggle`)

Built by `_tskGrpToggleHtml('my')` (9159-9179), injected into `#my-tasks-grp-toggle` at 1689-1690. Three segmented buttons:

```
modes = [ {key:'function',label:'Function'}, {key:'date',label:'Date'}, {key:'week',label:'Week'} ]
```

Field: Group by (segmented control)
- element: three `<button>`s inside an inline-styled wrapper "Group by:"
- id/class: container `#my-tasks-grp-toggle` (static in index.html 2044); buttons have no id
- options: `Function` / `Date` / `Week` (static, hardcoded in `_tskGrpToggleHtml`)
- default: `_TSK_GRP_MODE['my']` (8770-8773) = `localStorage.getItem('lgd_grp_my') || 'function'`
- handlers: each button `onclick="_tskSetGrp('my','<key>')"`
- validation: none
- wired: yes. `_tskSetGrp(scope,mode)` (9151-9157) sets `_TSK_GRP_MODE[scope]=mode`, persists `localStorage.setItem('lgd_grp_'+scope, mode)` (wrapped in try/catch), then re-renders the scope's view.
- data source: `localStorage` key `lgd_grp_my` / `lgd_grp_team` / `lgd_grp_all`
- gaps: active-button styling (navy `#2D3E51` bg) is correct. Mode dispatch in `renderMyTasks` (1696-1714): `function` → grouped table in `#my-tasks-tbl-wrap`/`#my-tasks-body`; `date`/`week` → hides table, renders into `#my-tasks-alt-body` via `_renderDateGroupedTasksHtml` / `_renderWeekGroupedTasksHtml`.

#### 3.3.3 Filter bar — injected aligned bar (`_tskBuildFilterBar` / `_tskInjectFilterBarForVid` / `_tskInitFilterWidgets`)

`renderMyTasks` calls `_tskInjectFilterBarForVid('my-tasks','my')` (1683, 1702). `_tskInjectFilterBarForVid` (5731-5743):
1. returns early if `#tsk-filter-bar-wrap-my` already exists (injected once);
2. removes the legacy static `.ts-filter-row` (index.html 2063-2075) and hides the first static `thead` row;
3. inserts `_tskBuildFilterBar('my')` HTML *before* `.task-sheet-wrap`;
4. `setTimeout(0)` → `_tskInitFilterWidgets('my')` which upgrades 8 selects to multi-select portals.

🔴 **Duplicate-ID hazard:** the static HTML filter row (index.html 2065-2073) defines `tf-my-fn`, `tf-my-parent`, `tf-my-sub`, `tf-my-asgr`, `tf-my-proj`, `tf-my-status`, `tf-my-pri`, `tf-my-due` (note: **no** `tf-my-asgn` in the static row — the Assigned-To column `<th></th>` is empty at line 2068). The injected bar (`_tskBuildFilterBar`) re-creates the same IDs **plus** `tf-my-asgn`. `_tskInjectFilterBarForVid` does `.remove()` on the whole `.ts-filter-row`, so the static duplicates are deleted before init — but only if the static row is found. If `renderMyTasks` runs after the row is already removed this is fine; the design relies on the one-shot removal. Documented as a fragile-but-functional design, not a live bug. ⚠️

**Filter bar layout** (`_tskBuildFilterBar('my')`, 5638-5726): a "secondary" row (Function + Project selects) above a `<table>` whose columns match `_TSK_COL_SPEC` (677-688). Each column gets a filter control:

`_TSK_COL_SPEC` (single source of truth for column order/width/filter type):

| order | key | label | width | ftype |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `Created_At` | Assigned date | 100 | `adate` |
| 2 | `subFnName` | Sub-function | 140 | `subfn` |
| 3 | `Title` | Task | 205 | `task` |
| 4 | `Assignee_IDs` | Assigned to | 155 | `assignee` |
| 5 | `assignerName` | Assigned by | 130 | `assigner` |
| 6 | `Recurring_Task` | Recurring | 95 | `recurring` |
| 7 | `Status` | Status | 125 | `status` |
| 8 | `Priority` | Priority | 95 | `priority` |
| 9 | `Due_Date` | Due date | 100 | `date2` |
| 10 | (null) | Actions | 100 | (null) |

**Filter controls (each documented in field format):**

Field: Filter — Function (secondary row)
- element: `<select class="ts-cf-inp">` → upgraded to multi-select portal
- id/class: `tf-my-fn` / `.ts-cf-inp`
- placeholder: trigger shows "All Functions" (portal placeholder = `'All Functions'`)
- options: built dynamically by `_populateTskFilterSelects` (8709-8717): top-level functions (`!Parent_Fn_ID`) narrowed to selected projects; verbatim base `<option value="">All Functions</option>` then one `<option value="{Function_ID}">{Name}</option>` per function
- handlers: static `onchange="renderMyTasks()"` (5646); portal re-dispatches `change` on selection via `_persist()`
- wired: yes — but see auto-apply note below
- data source: `APP.functions`

Field: Filter — Project (secondary row)
- element: `<select class="ts-cf-inp">` → multi-select portal
- id/class: `tf-my-proj` / `.ts-cf-inp`
- placeholder: "All Projects"
- options: `_populateTskFilterSelects` 8700-8703 — `<option value="">All Projects</option>` + one per `APP.projects` entry (`{Proj_ID}` → `{Name}`)
- handlers: `onchange="renderMyTasks()"` (5647)
- wired: yes; data source `APP.projects`

Field: Filter — Assigned date (from / to)
- element: two `<input type="date">`
- id/class: `flt-adate-from` (data-filter `adate-from`) and `flt-adate-to` (data-filter `adate-to`)
- placeholder: (none — native date)
- handlers: `onchange="_tskGrpRerender()"`
- validation: none; "to" adds +86400000 ms so the end day is inclusive (8635)
- wired: yes — filters `t.Created_At` range in `_tskApplyColFilters` (8631-8636)
- data source: typed
- gaps: 🔴 these are **fixed ids** (`flt-adate-from`/`flt-adate-to`, not `vid`-prefixed). With three task views (my/team/all) each injecting a bar, multiple elements can share `flt-adate-from`. `_tskApplyColFilters` reads them scoped via `document.getElementById('tsk-filter-bar-wrap-'+vid).querySelector('[data-filter="adate-from"]')` (8629-8633) so the scoping works, but the literal `id` is duplicated across views. Severity low (querySelector-by-data-filter masks it).

Field: Filter — Sub-function
- element: `<select class="ts-cf-inp" data-filter="subfn" data-ss="1">` → multi-select portal
- id/class: `tf-my-parent`
- placeholder: "All Sub-Fns" (static option text) → portal placeholder "All Sub-Functions"
- options: `_populateTskFilterSelects` 8720-8733 — sub-functions (`Parent_Fn_ID` set) narrowed to selected projects + selected functions; `<option value="">All Sub-Functions</option>` + `{Function_ID}`→`{Name}`
- handlers: `onchange="_tskGrpRerender()"`
- wired: yes; data source `APP.functions`

Field: Filter — Task
- element: `<select data-filter="task" data-ss="1">` → portal
- id/class: `tf-my-sub`
- options: `_populateTskFilterSelects` 8736-8749 — tasks narrowed to selected projects + sub-functions; `<option value="">All Tasks</option>` + `{Task_ID}`→`{Title}`
- handlers: `onchange="_tskGrpRerender()"`; wired yes; data source `APP.tasks`

Field: Filter — Assigned to (assignee)
- element: `<select data-filter="assignee" data-ss="1">` → portal
- id/class: `tf-my-asgn`
- options: `_populateTskFilterSelects` 8686-8690 — **full** employee list (no cascade), A→Z sorted copy of `APP.employees`; `<option value="">All Assignees</option>` + `{Emp_ID}`→`{Name}`
- handlers: `onchange="_tskGrpRerender()"`; wired yes; data source `APP.employees`

Field: Filter — Assigned by (assigner)
- element: `<select data-filter="assigner" data-ss="1">` → portal
- id/class: `tf-my-asgr`
- options: `_populateTskFilterSelects` 8693-8697 — full A→Z employee list; `<option value="">All Assigners</option>` + `{Emp_ID}`→`{Name}`
- handlers: `onchange="_tskGrpRerender()"`; wired yes; data source `APP.employees`

Field: Filter — Recurring
- element: `<select id="flt-recurring-my" data-filter="recurring">` (plain select, NOT multi)
- placeholder: (none)
- options (verbatim, static, 5686-5690):
  ```
  <option value="">All</option><option value="none">—</option>
  <option value="Daily">Daily</option><option value="Weekly">Weekly</option>
  <option value="Monthly">Monthly</option><option value="Quarterly">Quarterly</option>
  ```
- handlers: `onchange="_tskGrpRerender()"`
- validation: `none` value matches empty / 'none' / 'one time' / '—' (8638-8642)
- wired: yes; data source: typed/static

Field: Filter — Status
- element: `<select id="tf-my-status" data-filter="status">` (plain single select — **not** `data-ss`, NOT a multi-portal; nevertheless `_tskInitFilterWidgets` calls `_ssInitMulti(el('tf-my-status'))` upgrading it)
- options (verbatim, static, 5693-5697):
  ```
  <option value="">All Statuses</option><option>Yet to Start</option><option>Planning</option>
  <option>WIP (0%-25%)</option><option>WIP (25%-50%)</option><option>WIP (50%-75%)</option>
  <option>WIP (75%-100%)</option><option>Review</option><option>On Hold</option>
  <option>Cancelled</option><option>Done</option>
  ```
- handlers: `onchange="_tskGrpRerender()"`
- wired: yes; data source: static
- gaps: ⚠️ static option list omits legacy statuses (`WIP`, `Shared`, `Implemented`, `Stuck`) that exist in `TASK_STATUSES` and in imported data — tasks with those legacy statuses cannot be filtered to here.

Field: Filter — Priority
- element: `<select id="tf-my-pri" data-filter="priority">` → upgraded to portal by `_tskInitFilterWidgets`
- options (verbatim, static, 5700-5702):
  ```
  <option value="">All</option><option>Critical</option><option>High</option>
  <option>Medium</option><option>Low</option>
  ```
- handlers: `onchange="_tskGrpRerender()"`; wired yes; data source static

Field: Filter — Due date
- element: `<input type="date" class="ts-cf-inp" id="tf-my-due" data-filter="due">`
- placeholder: (none); title "Due by"
- handlers: `onchange="_tskGrpRerender()"`
- validation: "due by" semantics — keeps tasks with `Due_Date <= value` (8607-8610)
- wired: yes; data source typed

Field: Select-all checkbox (Actions column)
- element: `<input type="checkbox" id="tsk-chk-all">`
- handlers: `onchange="_tskSelectAll(this.checked)"`, `onclick="event.stopPropagation()"`
- wired: yes — `_tskSelectAll` (5970-5977) checks every `.tsk-row-chk`, sets `_tskSelectedIds[taskId]`, calls `_tskUpdateSelectionBar()`

Field: Clear filters button (Actions column)
- element: `<button>&#10005; Clear</button>` (in injected bar)
- handlers: `onclick="event.stopPropagation();_tskClearFilters()"`
- wired: yes — `_tskClearFilters()` (5950-5968): clears all 8 portals via `_ssmClear`, clears date inputs + recurring select in `#tsk-filter-bar-wrap-{vid}`, clears `#tsk-search-input` + `_tskSearchQuery`, then `_tskGrpRerender()`.
- gaps: a **second** clear path exists — `_clearTskFilters(vid)` (8752-8760) is wired to the *legacy* static-row clear button `<button class="ts-cf-clear" onclick="_clearTskFilters('my')">` (index.html 2074). That sets `inp.value = ''` on `tf-my-*` (which no longer works for portal selects since they store state in `dataset.ssmVals`, not `.value`). ⚠️ Since the legacy row is removed on inject, this button normally never shows; if it ever does, Clear would not reset the multi-selects. Two near-identical clear functions (`_tskClearFilters` vs `_clearTskFilters`) is confusing dead-code risk.

**Multi-select widget `_ssInitMulti(sel,label)` (489-659):**
- Idempotent guard `sel._ssmDone` (490); sets `sel._ss=true` so the single-select `_ssInit` IIFE never touches it (492).
- Hides the native `<select>`, wraps it, inserts a `.ssm-trigger` div. Trigger shows: placeholder "All {label}" when 0 selected; the single label when 1; a navy count pill + "selected" when ≥2 (528-543). When ≥1 selected it renders a red (`#E64D3D`) `×` clear glyph; otherwise a Material Symbols `expand_more` chevron.
- **Portal dropdown** is `position:fixed; z-index:2147483647` appended to `document.body` (584-639); opens up or down based on viewport space (`openUp` 589-590). Contains a "Search…" text input (`.ssm-srch`) and a checkbox list (`.ssm-list`). Options come from the underlying `<select>`'s `<option>`s (skipping value `''`).
- **Outside-click guard** `_onDocDown` (629-633): `mousedown` capture listener; bails (keeps open) when `portal.contains(e.target) || wrap.contains(e.target)` — i.e. clicks inside the portal or the trigger wrapper do NOT close it; otherwise `_closePortal()`. ✅ matches brief's `contains()` guard.
- **Scroll guard** `_onScroll` (635-639): capture `scroll` listener; bails (keeps open) when the scroll target is an element inside the portal (`portal.contains(e.target)`) — so scrolling the option list doesn't close it; any other scroll closes it (the fixed portal would otherwise detach from its trigger). ✅
- Only one portal open at a time: `_ssmActivePortal` (473) tracked; opening a new one calls the previous portal's `_ssmClose` (585).
- **Auto-apply wiring:** selecting an option (613-620) toggles membership in `selected`, re-renders the trigger + list, then `_persist()` (510-513) which writes `dataset.ssmVals` and dispatches a synthetic `change` event with `{bubbles:true}` on the original `<select>` → triggers the select's static `onchange` (`renderMyTasks()` or `_tskGrpRerender()`). So filters apply immediately on each tick (no explicit Apply button). ✅
- `_ssmGet(idOrEl)` (476-480) reads `JSON.parse(dataset.ssmVals||'[]')`, never throws.
- `_tskInitFilterWidgets(vid)` (662-671) upgrades exactly the 8 selects: `tf-{vid}-fn`, `-proj`, `-parent`, `-sub`, `-asgn`, `-asgr`, `-status`, `-pri`.

**Cascade (`_populateTskFilterSelects`, 8677-8750):** Project → narrows Function options; Project+Function → narrows Sub-Function options; Project+Sub-Function → narrows Task options. Stale selections that fall out of the narrowed set are pruned via `_ssmSet` (8716, 8732, 8748). Assignee/Assigner are the full list (no cascade). Comment at 8672-8676 documents intended cascade behaviour. ✅

#### 3.3.4 Debounced search

Search input is injected by `_tskGrpInjectHeaderControls(viewId)` (5521-5546) into the view's `.ph-actions`:

Field: Search tasks
- element: `<input id="tsk-search-input" type="text">`
- placeholder: "Search tasks…"
- default: `esc(_tskSearchQuery)` (preserved across re-renders)
- handlers: `oninput="_tskGrpSearchDebounced(this.value)"`; `onfocus`/`onblur` toggle border colour
- validation: none
- wired: yes — `_tskGrpSearchDebounced(val)` (5457-5461) sets `_tskSearchQuery = val.trim().toLowerCase()`, clears `_tskSearchTimer`, schedules `_tskGrpRerender` after **220 ms**. Matching in `_tskApplyColFilters` (8619-8625) / `_renderGroupedTasksHtml` (5749-5755): case-insensitive substring over `Title`, `Description`, `Task_ID`.
- data source: `APP.tasks` (in-memory)
- gaps: `_tskGrpInjectHeaderControls` also injects an "A-Z" group-sort button (`_tskGrpToggleGroupSortFromBar`, 5510-5518). `tsk-search-count` element referenced in `_tskClearFilters` (5965) is set to '' but is never created — harmless no-op. ⚠️

#### 3.3.5 Task table (Function group mode) — `_renderGroupedTasksHtml` + `_renderGroupedRow`

In `function` mode `renderMyTasks` writes `'<tr><td colspan="11">' + _renderGroupedTasksHtml(rows) + '</td></tr>'` into `#my-tasks-body` (1704-1705). `_renderGroupedTasksHtml` applies the search filter and groups by function/sub-function (per-group theads built from `_TSK_COL_SPEC` via `_tskGrpTheadHtml`, 5610-5634). Each row is `_renderGroupedRow(t)` (5361-5447).

**Row columns (in `_renderGroupedRow`, matching `_TSK_COL_SPEC` order):**

| Col | Source field | Render |
|---|---|---|
| Assigned date | `t.Created_At` | `_tskGrpFmtDate(t.Created_At)` |
| Sub-function | `t.SubFn_ID` → `APP.functions` lookup | name + Function_ID below, else `—` |
| Task | `t.Title` + `t.Task_ID` | title (+ `+N` link badge if `t.Links` lines) + ID line |
| Assigned to | `t.Assignee_IDs` | `_tskGrpAvatarHtml(..., 3)` (up to 3 avatars) |
| Assigned by | `t.Assigner_ID` | colored initials disc + name (falls back to raw ID or `—`) |
| Recurring | `t.Recurring_Task` | badge if value not blank/'none'/'One Time', else `—` |
| Status | `t.Status` | `_tskGrpStatusHtml(t.Status)`; cell has `ondblclick=tskStatusDblClick(...,'grp')` + `onclick=event.stopPropagation()` |
| Priority | `t.Priority` | `_tskGrpPriorityHtml(t.Priority)` |
| Due date | `t.Due_Date` | `_tskGrpDueHtml(t.Due_Date, t.Status)` (overdue-aware) |
| Actions | — | row checkbox + 📎 att-badge (if `ATT_COUNTS`) + open_in_new + edit + delete buttons |

- Row click: `onclick="openTaskDetail(t.Task_ID)"`. Closed tasks get class `tsk-row-closed`.
- Actions buttons: `openTaskDetail`, `openEditTaskModal`, `confirmDeleteTask` (each `event.stopPropagation()`-guarded). Edit/Delete are shown to **all** users in the grouped row (no per-row permission gate here; the gate is applied inside `openTaskDetail`/edit modal). ⚠️ Means a TM sees Edit/Delete icons on every row even where they can't act (backend `updateTask`/`deleteTask` enforce auth → error toast).
- Per-group column sort: clicking a per-group `<th>` calls `_tskGrpSetSort(grpId,col)` (5464-5507) — re-sorts just that group's tbody, toggles ↑/↓/↕ arrows.

#### 3.3.6 Date & Week group modes

`date` mode → `_renderDateGroupedTasksHtml(rows,'my')` (9181-9218): buckets non-closed tasks via `_tskBucketByDate` into Overdue / Today / This week / Next week / Later / No due date; each row via `_tskDateRowHtml(task,isOverdue)` (8807-8867). The status pill in date mode is double-click-editable (`tskStatusDblClick(...,'date')`, 8860-8863).

`week` mode → `_renderWeekGroupedTasksHtml(rows,'my')` (9220-9258+): Overdue bucket + one bucket per ISO week of `Due_Date`; rows reuse `_tskDateRowHtml`.

- 🔴 **Blank icons (Tabler `ti ti-*`):** the date/week bucket headers use `<i class="ti ti-...">` which render BLANK (no Tabler font loaded). Confirmed occurrences: `_renderDateGroupedTasksHtml` `_bucket` calls pass `ti-alert-triangle`, `ti-calendar-event`, `ti-calendar-week`, `ti-calendar-time`, `ti-calendar-off` (9211-9216, rendered at 9196); `_renderWeekGroupedTasksHtml` uses `ti ti-alert-triangle` (9251), `ti ti-calendar-week` (9270), `ti ti-calendar-off` (9287). Each is a visible gap (empty icon slot in the section header).

#### 3.3.7 Inline status edit (`tskStatusDblClick` / `tskStatusSave`)

- **Trigger:** double-click on a status cell (grouped row 5414-5417; date row 8860-8863). Single click is `stopPropagation`-guarded so the row's open-detail click does not fire.
- `tskStatusDblClick(event,taskId,currentStatus,kind)` (5263-5294): stores restore state in `_TSK_INLINE_RESTORE[taskId]`, replaces the cell with a `<select>` whose options are built from `TASK_STATUSES`:

  **`TASK_STATUSES` (335-340, verbatim):**
  ```
  'Yet to Start','Planning',
  'WIP (0%-25%)','WIP (25%-50%)','WIP (50%-75%)','WIP (75%-100%)',
  'Review','On Hold','Cancelled','Done',
  'WIP','Shared','Implemented','Stuck'
  ```
  (So the inline editor DOES expose the 4 legacy statuses, unlike the filter-bar status select. ⚠️ inconsistency.)
- The `<select>` auto-focuses; `change` → `tskStatusSave`; `Escape` → `_tskStatusRevert`; `blur` (200 ms later) → revert if no save in flight.

Field: Inline status `<select>`
- element: `<select>` injected into the cell
- id/class: none (queried via `host.querySelector('select')`)
- options: dynamic from `TASK_STATUSES`, with `selected` on `currentStatus`
- handlers: `onchange`→`tskStatusSave(taskId, sel.value, currentStatus)`; keydown Escape; blur revert
- validation: no-op if `newStatus === previousStatus` (reverts)
- wired: yes
- data source: `TASK_STATUSES` (static array)

- **`tskStatusSave(taskId,newStatus,previousStatus)` (5298-5333):** sets `_saving=true`, dims cell, calls:
  ```
  google.script.run
    .withSuccessHandler(...)
    .withFailureHandler(...)
    .updateTask(taskId, { Status: newStatus }, APP._verifiedEmail);
  ```
  - **GAS call:** `updateTask(taskId, updates, email)` — backend `auth.gs` 898-909: `var user = getCurrentUser(email); ... if (!canModifyTask(task, user)) throw new Error('Not authorized to edit this task.');`. Throws on error (caught by `withFailureHandler`).
  - **Success:** updates `APP.tasks[].Status` in place (5318-5319), toast 'Status updated', rebuilds the cell with `_tskRebuildStatusEl` (5337-5351), re-binds dblclick with the new status. Defensive `res.ok === false` branch reverts + toasts (5312-5316).
  - **Failure:** restores original cell HTML, toast error.
- **Permission:** there is **no client-side role check** before opening the inline editor — any user who can see the row can double-click and attempt a status change. The authorization gate is **server-side only** (`canModifyTask` in `updateTask`). ⚠️ A non-authorized user (e.g. a TM on a task they neither own nor are assigned) sees the editor, picks a value, and gets an error toast — the cell reverts. Functionally safe but UX-wise the control is presented to users who can't use it.

#### 3.3.8 Task Detail modal (`#task-detail-modal`)

- **id:** `task-detail-modal` (index.html 2711). **trigger:** `openTaskDetail(taskId)` (app.js.html 6494-6683) from any task row / action button.
- **Header (`.modal-hd`):** title `#tdm-title` (set to `t.Title`), subtitle `#tdm-subtitle` = `'Task · ' + t.Task_ID` (6507-6509). Header buttons:
  - `#tdm-edit-btn` (`<button class="modal-icon-btn hidden">`, edit icon) — `onclick="openEditTaskModal(APP._taskId)"`. Visibility 6663-6665: shown when `isTaskManager = !t._contextOnly && (_isManager(role) || isTmAssignee)`; `isTmAssignee` = role is Team Member/Intern AND in `Assignee_IDs`.
  - `#tdm-delete-btn` (`modal-icon-btn-danger hidden`, delete icon) — `onclick="confirmDeleteTask(APP._taskId)"`. Shown when `_isManager(role) && !t._contextOnly` (6666).
  - `.modal-x` (✕) — `onclick="closeModal('task-detail-modal')"`.
- **close mechanism:** ✕ button; backdrop click `onclick="closeModal('task-detail-modal',event)"` (closes only when click target is the backdrop); `closeModal` also called by delete (6781) and by `closeAllModals` path (11907).
- **data-in source:** `APP.tasks` (in-memory) for the body; progress + attachments via GAS (below).
- **Body (`#task-detail-body`)** built as `strip + ctxHtml + peopleHtml + hoursHtml + descHtml + linksHtml + chainHtml` (6650). Sections (all field-format-relevant):

  1. **Info strip** (6522-6547): Status (`pill(t.Status)`), Priority (`badge(t.Priority)`), Due Date (`deadlineHtml(t.Due_Date,t.Status)`), Recurring (only if `t.Recurring_Task && !== 'One Time'`).
     - **Request Change button** (6533-6539): shown only when NOT admin AND `t.Assigner_ID !== currentUser.empId` → `openDueDateRequestModal('Task', taskId, title, currentDueISO)`. Admins / the assigner edit due date directly (via edit modal). ✅ matches Due Date Change Approval flow (CLAUDE.md #13/#37 Recent Changes).
  2. **Context cards** (`tdm-grid2`/`tdm-card`, 6549-6583): Project (name), Function (name + truncated 120-char description), Sub-Function (name + truncated description). Each only shown if present.
  3. **People** (6585-6601): "Assigned To" → assignee chips `<span class="ms-chip">` (name or raw id), `—` if none. "Assigned By" → avatar + name + meta (`Designation · Role · Team`).
  4. **Hours** (always shown, 6603-6620): actual / est / pct progress bar; "Not tracked" when both 0.
  5. **Description** (always, 6622-6630): pre-wrapped text or italic "No description added.".
  6. **Related Links** (always, 6632-6637): `_renderLinksHtml(t.Links)` (renders clickable chips; placeholder when empty).
  7. **Assignment Chain** (6639-6648): collapsible `<details>` only if `_renderTaskAssignChain(t)` returns content.
- **Progress section** (static in index.html 2727-2731): `#progress-timeline`, "+ Add Progress" button `#btn-add-progress` → `openProgressModal()`. Button visibility gated by `canProgress` (6653-6659). Timeline loaded via GAS:
  - **GAS call:** `getTaskProgressUpdates(taskId, APP.currentUser.email)` (6674-6679) → success `renderProgressTimeline` (6685-6705); failure shows error text in `#progress-timeline`.
- **Attachments section** (index.html 2733-2741): `#task-attachments-list`, "File"/"Audio" buttons (`_attPickFile`/`_attRecordAudio`), hidden `#att-file-input-Task`. Loaded via `loadAttachments('Task', taskId, 'task-attachments-list')` (6682).
- **return shape + reaction:** `getTaskProgressUpdates` returns an array of update rows; `renderProgressTimeline` renders avatar/author/date/percentage bar/desc/blocker/hours per item, or an empty-state. `updateTask` (inline status) returns void/throws.

---

### 3.4 My Projects

The **My Projects** view (`#view-my-projects`, index.html 2098-2111) is rendered by `renderMyProjects()` (app.js.html 1718-1753).

#### 3.4.1 View header controls

Tab strip (`.tl-tabs`, index.html 2103-2105):

| Label | element | onclick | data-vid | data-val |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| All | `<button class="tl-tab asgn-tab active">` | `setAsgnFilter('my-prj','all')` | `my-prj` | `all` |
| To Me | `<button class="tl-tab asgn-tab">` | `setAsgnFilter('my-prj','to-me')` | `my-prj` | `to-me` |
| By Me | `<button class="tl-tab asgn-tab">` | `setAsgnFilter('my-prj','by-me')` | `my-prj` | `by-me` |

- wired: yes — `setAsgnFilter('my-prj', …)` re-invokes `renderMyProjects`; filter applied by `_applyAsgnProjFilter(myProjs,'my-prj')` (1744). `_applyAsgnProjFilter` (3305-3330): `to-me` = in `Assignee_IDs` or `Owner_IDs`; `by-me` = `Assigner_ID === me`. (`team`/`by-team` branches exist but no UI tab emits them for `my-prj`. ⚠️ dead branches.)

Field: + New Project button
- element: `<button class="btn btn-primary nav-mgr-only hidden">`
- id/class: `.btn-primary.nav-mgr-only`
- handlers: `onclick="openNewProjectModal()"`
- wired: yes
- gaps: visibility toggled twice — static class `nav-mgr-only hidden` (index.html 2107) AND in `renderMyProjects` (1748-1750): `btnProj.classList.toggle('hidden', managerRoles.indexOf(u.role) === -1)` where `managerRoles = ['Super Admin','Admin','Team Captain','Team Facilitator']`. So shown only to managers. ✅

#### 3.4.2 Project collection logic (`renderMyProjects`)

- `myProjIds` = projects referenced by tasks where I'm assignee OR assigner (1721-1726).
- `myProjs` = projects where `myProjIds.has(p.Proj_ID)` OR I'm in `Owner_IDs` OR `Assigner_ID === me` OR in `Assignee_IDs` (1728-1733).
- Adds parent projects of any qualifying sub-project (1736-1742).
- Then `_applyAsgnProjFilter` (tab) → `_sortProjHierarchy` (1744-1745) → `_renderProjGrouped` into `#my-projects-grid` (1752).
- wired: yes; data source `APP.projects` + `APP.tasks`.

#### 3.4.3 Project cards (`_renderProjGrouped` / `projCard` / `_projGroupBlock` / `_projSubCard`)

`_renderProjGrouped(projs)` (11109-11131): empty-state if none; top-level projects with sub-projects → `_projGroupBlock`, else `projCard`; orphan sub-projects → `projCard`.

**`projCard(p)` (10992-11032)** — standalone card. Shows:
- parent label (if sub-project) with `subdirectory_arrow_right` icon
- `.proj-name` + `pill(p.Status)`
- meta: `badge(p.Priority)`, deadline (`event` icon + `fmtDate`) or "No deadline", task counts (`{done}/{total} done` + `{open} open`) or "No tasks"
- owner row: assigner name (`person` icon) → assignee names
- progress bar (`{pct}%`) when `total > 0`
- actions: `_projActionBtns(p, 'sm')`

**`_projGroupBlock(parent, subProjs)` (11065-11106)** — parent header (`folder_open` icon, aggregate stats across parent+subs, sub-project count) + a `.proj-sub-grid` of `_projSubCard` mini-cards. Parent actions via `_projActionBtns(parent, '')`.

**`_projActionBtns(p, size)` (10964-10989)** — per-card action buttons:

| Button | shown when | onclick | effect |
|---|---|---|---|
| Project Chat | `p.Chat_Space_URI \|\| p.Chat_Link` | `openChatWindow(uri, projId)` | open chat |
| Attachments | always | `openAttPanel('Project', projId, name)` | attachment panel (+ count badge) |
| Edit | `canEdit` = admin OR owner OR in Assignee/Owner IDs | `openEditProjectModal(projId)` | edit modal |
| Delete | `canDelete` = admin OR (manager AND owner) | `confirmDeleteProject(projId, name)` | delete w/ confirm |

- All `event.stopPropagation()`-guarded.
- wired: yes. `confirmDeleteProject` (6789-6794) → `confirm()` → `deleteProject(...)` GAS → toast + `refresh()`.

🔴 **No project detail modal exists.** Unlike tasks (`openTaskDetail` → `#task-detail-modal`), there is **no `openProjectDetail` function and the project cards have no click-to-open-detail handler** — clicking a card body does nothing (only the action buttons respond). Project structure is only viewable via the **dashboard** hierarchy panel (`_dashSelectProj` → `_renderProjHierarchy`, 11162-11308), not from the My Projects grid. This is a gap vs. the task-detail pattern: a user on My Projects cannot open a read-only project detail view; they can only Edit (if permitted), open Chat, open Attachments, or Delete. ⚠️/❌

#### 3.4.4 Project detail (dashboard-only `_renderProjHierarchy`)

For completeness: the project structure tree (`_renderProjSectionHtml`, 11171-11248; `_renderProjHierarchy`, 11250-11308) lives on the **Dashboard** ("My Projects" panel), not in `#view-my-projects`. It renders overall progress bar + function/sub-function/task tree + sub-project blocks. Functions/sub-functions open `openFunctionDetail(...)`; tasks via `_hierTaskRow`. Not reachable from the My Projects grid. (Documented here only to flag the missing detail link from the My Projects view.)

---

#### Gap summary (3.3 / 3.4)

| # | Location | Issue | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|
| G1 | date/week group headers (`_renderDateGroupedTasksHtml` 9211-9216, `_renderWeekGroupedTasksHtml` 9251/9270/9287) | Tabler `ti ti-*` icons render blank (no Tabler font) | medium |
| G2 | filter status select (`_tskBuildFilterBar` 5693-5697) | omits legacy statuses WIP/Shared/Implemented/Stuck → those tasks unfilterable | low |
| G3 | grouped row actions (`_renderGroupedRow` 5439-5441) | Edit/Delete icons shown to all viewers regardless of permission; only server enforces auth | low |
| G4 | inline status edit (`tskStatusDblClick` 5263) | no client-side permission gate before opening editor; unauthorized users get error toast on save | low |
| G5 | My Projects grid (`projCard`/`_projGroupBlock`) | no project detail modal / no card-body click handler; cannot view project structure from this view | medium |
| G6 | `renderMyTasks` 1666-1669 / `_applyAsgnProjFilter` 3309-3322 | dead `team`/`by-team` branches with no UI tab to trigger them | low |
| G7 | static vs injected filter row (index.html 2063-2075 vs `_tskBuildFilterBar`) | duplicate `tf-my-*` ids resolved only by one-shot `.ts-filter-row` removal; fragile | low |
| G8 | `flt-adate-from`/`flt-adate-to` (5669-5670) | fixed (non-vid-prefixed) ids duplicated across 3 task views; masked by scoped querySelector | low |
| G9 | `_clearTskFilters(vid)` (8752-8760) wired to legacy clear btn (index.html 2074) | sets `.value=''` which does NOT clear multi-select portals (state in dataset.ssmVals) | low |
| G10 | `tsk-search-count` (referenced 5965) | element never created; `_tskClearFilters` write is a no-op | low |


### 3.5 Work Log (personal) — CORE

> Source read for this slice (verified, not from PRD/scaffold):
> - `src/app.js.html` — WL state vars + `_attEffHours` + WL constants/styles + OT helpers (1748–1905); `loadMyWorkLogs` + `_wlLoadMeetingsAsync` (1907–2012); `renderWeekLog` (2013–2247); `_wlUpdateOtBadge` / `wlOnAttendanceChange` (2249–2304); nav + `_wlCommitAllTextareas` (2306–2357); `_wlSaveStatus` / `_wlSaveComment` / `wlMarkDirty` (2359–2403); duration display helpers `_wlDurHtml` / `_wlApplyDurations` / `_wlLoadDurationsAsync` (2405–2456); auto-save helpers `_wlAsStatus` / `_wlToggleOffDayWork` / `_wlAutoSave` / `_mlAutoSave` / `_wlOnBlur` / `wlAutoResize` (2537–2684); hours calculator `_wlCalcFromHrs` / `wlHrsPreview` / `wlApplyHrs` (2686–2767); chip builders `wlAutoAddMeetings` / `_wlBuildCell` / `_wlChipHtml` / `wlRenderChips` / picker (2769–2983); `wlAddItem` / `wlRemoveItem` / `wlEditChip` / `wlAddCustom` / `wlGetWorkText` / `_wlUpdateSaveBtn` / `saveWeekEntry` (2985–3215); `_isAltWeekSat` (3575–3581); intern `INTERN_OFF_PATTERNS` / `_internIsOffDayText` / `_internWlOnAttendanceChange` (3721–3812); work-duration widget `loadWorkDuration` → `_wdClockOutAt` (15290–15438), `wdStartBreak`/`wdEndBreak` (15441–15468), `_wdRenderStatus` (15471–15516+), `_wdStartTimer` (15626–15660), `_wdEditBreak` (15794–15817).
> - `src/index.html` — Work Log view shell (2114–2133); dashboard header clock popup (1896–1904).
> - `src/auth.gs` — `MANAGER_ROLES`/`_canEditWlStatus`/`_isManager` (189–208), `_wlNextId`/`submitWorkLog`/`updateWorkLogStatus`/`updateWorkLogComment`/`updateWorkLog`/`getMyWorkLogs`/`getMyWlWeekSummary` (1201–1370).
> - `src/work-duration.gs` — `wdClockIn`/`wdClockOut`/`wdStartBreak`/`wdEndBreak` (35–211), `wdAutoClockOut` (367–435), `getWorkDurationsForDates`/`getTeamWorkDurationsRange` (693–737).
> - `src/intern-work-log.gs` — `saveInternWorkLog`/`getInternWorkLogs` (64–133).
> - `src/triggers.gs` — `installTriggers` (5–36).

The personal Work Log is a weekly (or monthly) editable grid where the logged-in user records attendance, half-by-half work updates, extra hours, a remark, and (managers only) status + comments. It auto-saves with a 500 ms debounce per row. It is reachable via sidebar → Work Log (`view-work-log`) or the dashboard "Log Today's Work" accent button (`navigate('work-log')`).

---

#### 3.5.0 View Shell (`index.html` 2114–2133)

| Element | id / class | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| View container | `view-work-log` (`div.view hidden`) | Rendered into `#my-work-log-history` |
| Title | `ph-title` static "Work Log" | |
| Subtitle | `wl-week-sub` | Set by `loadMyWorkLogs`/`wlNav` to `_wlRangeLabel()` (NOT "Weekly view" after first load) |
| Week tab | `wl-mode-week` (`tl-tab active`) | `onclick="setWLMode('week')"` |
| Month tab | `wl-mode-month` (`tl-tab`) | `onclick="setWLMode('month')"` |
| Prev | `tl-nav-btn` | `onclick="wlNav(-1)"` — ‹ |
| Today | `btn btn-ghost btn-sm` | `onclick="wlNavToday()"` |
| Range label | `wl-week-label` (`span.wl-week-label`) | default `—`; set to `_wlRangeLabel()` |
| Next | `tl-nav-btn` | `onclick="wlNav(1)"` — › |
| Weekly Summary | `btn btn-ghost btn-sm` | `onclick="openWeeklySummaryModal()"` — **separate slice** |
| Refresh | `btn btn-ghost btn-sm` | `onclick="loadMyWorkLogs()"` — ↻ |

`setWLMode(mode)` toggles `.active` on the two mode buttons and re-calls `loadMyWorkLogs()`.

**Load pipeline** (`loadMyWorkLogs`, 1907–1985): resets `WL_ITEMS/WL_MTGS/WL_AUTO_HRS/WL_DURATIONS`, sets labels, paints a "Loading…" empty state, registers a one-time document click listener that closes any open picker (`WL_DOC_LISTENER_DONE`), then calls **`getInternWorkLogs` for role `Intern`, else `getMyWorkLogs`** with the **±8-week window** from `_wlDateRange()` (`start = anchor − 8 wk`, `end = anchor + 2 wk` via `_localIso`). On success it stores rows into `WL_DATA` keyed by `_dateIso(l.Date)`, **dedups duplicate dates by keeping the richer `work1+work2`** record, seeds empty `WL_ITEMS` arrays per day, calls `renderWeekLog()`, then async Phase 2 `_wlLoadMeetingsAsync` (meetings) and Phase 3 `_wlLoadDurationsAsync` (work durations). Failure paints an error empty-state.

`google.script.run` calls used here:

| Function | Called from | Params | Success | Failure |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `getMyWorkLogs` / `getInternWorkLogs` | `loadMyWorkLogs` | `(email, wlRange.start, wlRange.end)` | builds `WL_DATA`, renders | error empty-state |
| `getMeetingsForRange` | `_wlLoadMeetingsAsync` | `(startDate, endDate, email)` | fills `WL_MTGS`, `wlAutoAddMeetings`, re-renders chips + HRS | silent |
| `getWorkDurationsForDates` | `_wlLoadDurationsAsync` | `(email, start, end)` | `Object.assign(WL_DURATIONS,…)`, `_wlApplyDurations` | silent |

`getMyWorkLogs(email, startDate, endDate)` backend (auth.gs 1306): `var user = getCurrentUser(email); var logs = dbGetWorkLogs(user.empId);` then optional date filter — returns the raw rows array (no `{ok}` wrapper).

---

#### 3.5.1 Week View (table — `renderWeekLog`, 2013–2247)

Table id is implicit (`table.wl-week-table` inside `.wl-week-wrap` inside `#my-work-log-history`). Each row is `<tr class="wl-row …" id="wl-tr-{iso}">`. 9 columns:

| # | Header (verbatim) | th class | Cell content |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `Day` | `wl-day-cell` | `DN[dow] + ' ' + iso.substring(8)`; "Today"/"Upcoming" badges; `wl-ot-badge-{iso}` |
| 2 | `Attendance` | `wl-att-cell` | attendance input/select + Hrs calc + off-day toggle |
| 3 | `Work Update — 1st Half` | `wl-h1-cell` | `wl-h1-work-{iso}` (chip cell) + `wl-h1-leave-{iso}` (leave type) |
| 4 | `Work Update — 2nd Half` | `wl-h2-cell` | `wl-h2-work-{iso}` (chip cell) + `wl-h2-leave-{iso}` (leave-requested) |
| 5 | `Extra Hrs` | `wl-extra-cell` | `wl-eh-{iso}` |
| 6 | `Remark` | `wl-remark-cell` | `wl-rm-{iso}` |
| 7 | `Status` | `wl-status-cell` | `wl-st-{iso}` (manager) or `.wl-status-badge` (others) |
| 8 | `Comments` | `wl-comments-cell` | `wl-ac-{iso}` (manager) or `.wl-admin-note`; + work-duration row |
| 9 | (empty) | `wl-save-cell` | `wl-save-btn-{iso}` + `wl-as-{iso}` status span |

Per-row computed flags: `isTdy` (today), `isWknd` (Sat/Sun), `isOff` (off-type / intern off-text), `isLeave` (Leave Full/Half), `isFuture = !isAdmin && iso > today` (future locked for non-managers), `hasOffDayWork` (off-type + saved logId + work content or ≥2 extra hrs), `disWork` (off-day-without-work → disable). Row CSS classes: `wl-row-today / wl-row-wknd / wl-row-off / wl-row-leave / wl-row-future`.

**Default attendance pre-fill** when no saved log: Sun → `Week Off`; Sat that is a 1st/3rd/5th Saturday (`_isAltWeekSat`) → `Alternate Week Off`; date in `TL_HOLIDAYS` → `Holiday`; else blank. ⚠️ Mon–Fri non-holiday default is empty string (`'—'` option), so a brand-new day shows no attendance until the user picks one — `saveWeekEntry` then no-ops on a fully empty row.

##### Field: Attendance (regular employee)
- element: `<select>`
- id/class: `id="wl-att-{iso}"`, class `wl-inp wl-att-sel`
- placeholder: (none)
- required: no (blank allowed)
- default: saved `data.attendance` or computed pre-fill (see above) or `''`
- options: static, from `WL_ATTENDANCE_OPTIONS` via `selOpts()` (blank value renders label `—`). See §3.5.2 for verbatim list.
- handlers: `onchange="wlOnAttendanceChange('{iso}')"`
- validation: none in the select itself; downstream off-day ≥2 hr gate in `saveWeekEntry`
- wired: yes
- data source: hardcoded `WL_ATTENDANCE_OPTIONS`
- gaps: none

##### Field: Attendance (Intern — free text)
- element: `<input type="text">`
- id/class: `id="wl-att-{iso}"`, class `wl-inp wl-att-sel`
- placeholder: `e.g. 9, 8.5, Holiday, Leave`
- required: no
- default: saved value or `''`
- handlers: `oninput="_internWlOnAttendanceChange(this,'{iso}')"`, `onblur="wlMarkDirty('{iso}')"`
- validation: free text; `_internIsOffDayText` regex distinguishes off-day keywords (see §3.5.6)
- wired: yes — numeric/text enables work fields, off-keyword disables them
- data source: free text, persisted to `Intern_Work_Log`
- gaps: none

##### Field: Hrs (hours calculator — regular employee only)
- element: `<input type="number">`
- id/class: `id="wl-hc-{iso}"`, class `wl-hc-inp` (inside `.wl-hc-wrap`); label span `.wl-hc-lbl` "Hrs"; preview span `wl-hc-pre-{iso}` class `wl-hc-pre`
- placeholder: `—`
- required: no
- default: pre-fill — if saved log, `_attEffHours(att, extraHrs)` (when >0); else meeting-derived `WL_AUTO_HRS[iso]` **only if `iso <= today`** (role-independent guard)
- min/max/step: `min="0" max="16" step="0.5"`; title `"Total hours worked (0.5 steps) → auto-calculates attendance & extra hours"`
- handlers: `oninput="wlHrsPreview('{iso}')"`, `onblur="wlApplyHrs('{iso}')"`, Enter → `wlApplyHrs`
- validation: `wlHrsPreview` flags `invalid` outside 0–16; non-0.5 multiples preview "→ round to Xh"
- wired: yes — `wlApplyHrs` snaps to 0.5 and sets attendance + extra hrs via `_wlCalcFromHrs` then `wlOnAttendanceChange` + `wlMarkDirty`
- data source: derived; writes into `wl-att-{iso}` and `wl-eh-{iso}`
- gaps: hidden entirely for interns (intern Hrs is just a read-only `wl-intern-hrs-{iso}` text)

**Hours → attendance mapping (`_wlCalcFromHrs`, 2693–2720):**
```
OFF_BASE = Week Off | Holiday | Alternate Week Off ; EXT_OFF = Extra Full Day | Extra Half Day
isOffCtx = att in OFF_BASE/EXT_OFF, OR (Sunday with no att)
Off-day context:  hrs<4 → keep base off-day, extra=hrs ; 4≤hrs<9 → Extra Half Day, extra=hrs-4 ; hrs≥9 → Extra Full Day, extra=hrs-9
Regular day:      hrs<4 → Leave Full Day, extra=hrs ; 4≤hrs<9 → Leave Half Day, extra=hrs-4 ; hrs≥9 → Present, extra=hrs-9
xtra(v) = max(0, round(v*2)/2)
```

##### Field: Off-day "Worked today" toggle
- element: `<input type="checkbox">` inside `wl-odt-{iso}` (class `wl-offday-toggle`, hidden unless off-type)
- id/class: `id="wl-odc-{iso}"`; warning span `wl-odc-warn-{iso}` ("Min 2 hrs")
- default: checked when `hasOffDayWork`
- handlers: `onchange="_wlToggleOffDayWork(this,'{iso}')"`
- validation: manual save requires `extraHrs ≥ 2` when checked (off-type) — else shows "Min 2 hrs" warning 3 s and aborts save
- wired: yes — reveals/enables work fields and overrides the `.wl-row-off` `pointer-events:none;opacity:.45` CSS via inline styles; unchecking clears chips + extra hrs and auto-saves empty
- data source: UI state
- gaps: the ≥2 hr gate is bypassed on **auto-save** (only enforced on manual save) — by design comment

##### Field: Work Update — 1st / 2nd Half (chip cell)
See §3.5.3. Built by `_wlBuildCell(iso, half, data, disWork)`. Wrapper `wl-upd-{half}-{iso}`.

##### Field: Leave Type (1st-half slot when Leave Full/Half)
- element: `<select>` id `wl-lt-{iso}` class `wl-inp`
- options: static `WL_LEAVE_OPTIONS = ['','Planned Leave','Sick Leave','Casual Leave','Emergency Leave','Other']` (blank → `—`)
- handlers: `onchange="wlMarkDirty('{iso}')"`
- wired: yes — saved as `Purpose` field
- gaps: visible only when `isLeave`; ⚠️ **no auto-save on change** (only marks dirty; relies on blur of another field or nav-commit to persist)

##### Field: Leave Requested (2nd-half slot when Leave Full/Half)
- element: `<select>` id `wl-lr-{iso}` class `wl-inp`
- options: static **inline literal** `['','Same Day','One Day Before','Within Same Week','One Week Before']` (NOT a named const)
- handlers: `onchange="wlMarkDirty('{iso}')"`
- wired: yes — saved as `Leave Requested`
- gaps: ⚠️ same no-auto-save-on-change issue as Leave Type

##### Field: Extra Hrs
- element: `<input type="number">` id `wl-eh-{iso}` class `wl-inp wl-hrs-inp`
- placeholder: `0`; `min="0" max="12" step="0.5"`
- default: `data.extraHrs || ''`
- handlers: `oninput="wlMarkDirty('{iso}');_wlUpdateOtBadge('{iso}')"`, `onblur="_wlOnBlur('{iso}')"`; disabled for off-day-without-work
- wired: yes
- gaps: none

##### Field: Remark
- element: `<input type="text">` id `wl-rm-{iso}` class `wl-inp`
- placeholder: `Remark…`
- handlers: `oninput="wlMarkDirty('{iso}')"`, `onblur="_wlOnBlur('{iso}')"`
- wired: yes — saved as `Remark`
- gaps: none

##### Field: Status (manager-only editable, others read-only) — RBAC gate
- isAdmin gate: `_canEditWlStatus(APP.currentUser.role)` = `MANAGER_ROLES.indexOf(role)!==-1` (Super Admin, Admin, Team Captain, Team Facilitator)
- Manager element: `<select>` id `wl-st-{iso}` class `wl-inp wl-status-sel`, `data-date`, `data-emp`, `onchange="_wlSaveStatus(this)"`
- options: static `WL_STATUS_OPTIONS = ['','Tentative','On Time','Late','Absent','Half Day']`
- Non-manager element: read-only `<span class="wl-status-badge {cls}">` — badge class by value: On Time→`wl-badge-ok`, Late→`wl-badge-warn`, Absent→`wl-badge-danger`, Tentative→`wl-badge-tentative`, else `wl-badge-none`
- wired: yes — `_wlSaveStatus` calls `updateWorkLogStatus(empId, dateKey, newStatus, APP._verifiedEmail)`; on "not found" error, marks the row dirty and toasts that status saves with the row
- backend gate: `updateWorkLogStatus` throws `Permission denied` if caller not `_isManager`
- gaps: none — gating consistent FE+BE

##### Field: Comments (manager-only editable)
- Manager element: `<input type="text">` id `wl-ac-{iso}` class `wl-inp wl-admin-inp`, placeholder `Admin notes…`, `onchange="_wlSaveComment(this)"`
- Non-manager: read-only `<span class="wl-admin-note">`
- wired: yes — `_wlSaveComment` → `updateWorkLogComment(empId, dateKey, newComment, APP._verifiedEmail)`; backend `_isManager` gated
- gaps: none

##### Field: Save button + auto-save status
- element: `<button class="wl-save-btn …" id="wl-save-btn-{iso}" onclick="saveWeekEntry('{iso}')">`; icon = `sync` (saving) / `save` (dirty) / `check` (saved) / `—` (empty). Hidden for interns (intern saves via auto-save only).
- status span: `wl-as-{iso}` — "Saving…" / "Saved ✓" / "Save failed"
- wired: yes
- gaps: none

##### Work-duration row inside Comments cell (`_wlDurHtml`, 2410–2426)
Rendered under every Comments cell: wrap `wl-dur-wrap-{iso}`, value span `wl-dur-{iso}` ("—" default, "HH:MM:SS" when populated by Phase 3). 🔴 **Uses `<i class="ti ti-clock-hour-4">`** — a stray Tabler icon class; no Tabler font is loaded, so this clock glyph renders BLANK. Same blank-icon bug in `_mlDurHtml` (member modal) at line 2499. Mark as a real gap per ground-truth.

#### 3.5.2 Attendance Dropdown — verbatim options + colours + hours

`WL_ATTENDANCE_OPTIONS` (app.js.html 1845) — **all 8, verbatim, in order:**
```js
['Present','Leave Full Day','Leave Half Day','Alternate Week Off','Week Off','Holiday','Extra Full Day','Extra Half Day']
```
(`selOpts` also prepends/keeps a blank `''` value only when the saved/pre-filled att is blank — the blank option is not in the const; it appears as the currently-selected `—` because `o === val` matching needs the value to exist. ⚠️ A blank att renders with no matching `<option>` selected unless the value is empty — in practice the select shows "Present" first since `''` is not in the list. Confirmed: the list has NO empty option, so a row defaulting to `att=''` shows `Present` highlighted only if browser selects index 0; the stored value remains `''` until changed. Minor UX ambiguity.)

**`WL_ATTENDANCE_STYLES` (1856–1865) — all 8 verbatim (abbr / bg / text):**

| Attendance | abbr | bg | text |
|---|---|---|---|
| Present | `P` | `#dcfce7` | `#15803d` |
| Leave Full Day | `LF` | `#fecdd3` | `#be123c` |
| Leave Half Day | `LH` | `#fed7aa` | `#c2410c` |
| Alternate Week Off | `AW` | `#fde68a` | `#92400e` |
| Week Off | `W` | `#fce7f3` | `#9d174d` |
| Holiday | `H` | `#bfdbfe` | `#1d4ed8` |
| Extra Full Day | `EF` | `#14532d` | `#ffffff` |
| Extra Half Day | `EH` | `#065f46` | `#ffffff` |

(`_wlAttendanceColor`/`_wlAttendanceTextColor` override an off-type that has logged work to green `#d1fae5`/`#065f46`.)

**Effective-hours mapping (`_attEffHours`, 1773–1780) — verbatim:**
```js
function _attEffHours(att, extra) {
  var ex = parseFloat(extra) || 0;
  var base = { 'Present': 9, 'Extra Full Day': 9, 'Extra Half Day': 4, 'Leave Half Day': 4 };
  if (base[att] !== undefined) return base[att] + ex;
  var numericAtt = parseFloat(att);
  if (att && !isNaN(numericAtt) && numericAtt > 0) return numericAtt + ex;  // intern free-text numeric
  return ex;
}
```
So: Present = 9 + extra; Extra Full Day = 9 + extra; Extra Half Day = 4 + extra; Leave Half Day = 4 + extra; off-types/Leave Full Day = extra only; intern numeric att = att + extra. The backend `getMyWlWeekSummary` (auth.gs 1344) re-declares the identical `BASE_HRS` map — kept in sync manually. ⚠️ Duplication risk: two independent copies of the base-hours table (FE `_attEffHours` and BE `BASE_HRS`).

**Type groupings:**
```js
WL_LEAVE_TYPES    = ['Leave Full Day','Leave Half Day'];                 // show Leave Type + Leave Requested
WL_OFF_TYPES      = ['Week Off','Holiday','Alternate Week Off'];          // fully off → disable work fields
WL_OFF_DAY_TYPES  = WL_OFF_TYPES;                                         // alias
WL_ALWAYS_OT_TYPES= ['Extra Full Day','Extra Half Day'];                 // always show +OT badge
```
`_wlShowsOtBadge(att, extraHrs, isOffDayWorked)` (1883): true if att in `WL_ALWAYS_OT_TYPES`, or off-day-worked, or `extraHrs > 0`.

**`wlOnAttendanceChange` (2262–2304):** toggles off-day container, leave/work div visibility (`showWork = !isOff || offWorked`), disables work/extra inputs for off-without-work, updates row colour class + OT badge, and (when `!WL_RENDERING`) marks dirty + auto-saves.

---

#### 3.5.3 Task-Chip Area + Picker

`_wlBuildCell(iso, half, data, disabled)` (2808–2838) builds, per half, in this vertical order:
1. **Task/Project picker button** — `<button class="wl-add-item-btn" onclick="wlTogglePicker('{iso}','{half}')">` with Material `add` icon + label "Task / Project"; dropdown div `wl-picker-{half}-{iso}` (class `wl-picker-drop`, hidden).
2. **Custom note textarea** — `<textarea class="wl-cust-inp wl-inp" id="wl-cust-{half}-{iso}" data-iso data-half rows="2" placeholder="Custom note — 1st/2nd half…">`; handlers `oninput="wlMarkDirty(iso);wlAutoResize(this)"`, `onblur="_wlOnBlur(iso, half)"`, Enter-without-Shift → `wlAddCustom(iso, half)`; sibling add button `.wl-cust-add-btn` (label `↵`) → `wlAddCustom`.
3. **Chips area** — `<div class="wl-upd-chips" id="wl-chips-{half}-{iso}">`.

**`WL_ITEMS` structure:** `WL_ITEMS['{iso}:h1' | '{iso}:h2'] = [{ type, id, text }]`. `type ∈ meeting|project|task|subtask|subfn|custom`. Chip icons (`_wlChipHtml`, 2842): meeting→`video_call`, project→`folder_open`, task→`task_alt`, subtask→`subdirectory_arrow_right`, subfn→`check_box`, custom→`edit_note`.

**Picker modal (dropdown, not a true modal)** — `wlTogglePicker` (2868) opens `wl-picker-{half}-{iso}` and renders `_wlPickerHtml`:
- Search input `.wl-picker-search` placeholder `Search projects, tasks…`, `oninput="wlPickerFilter(iso,half,this.value)"` (auto-focused on open)
- List `wl-picker-list-{half}-{iso}` from `_wlPickerItemsHtml` — sections **Projects** then **Tasks**:
  - my tasks = `APP.tasks` where `_parseIds(Assignee_IDs).indexOf(uid)!==-1` and `!_contextOnly`
  - my projects = projects of my tasks ∪ projects where I'm Owner
  - each item `onclick="wlAddItemFromEl(this,iso,half)"`; already-added items get `.wl-pi-added` + `✓` and no onclick
- Empty: `No items found`
- Close: outside-click document listener (registered once in `loadMyWorkLogs`) or toggling again. ⚠️ Picker only surfaces **tasks/projects** — no sub-function or sub-task source despite the `subtask`/`subfn` chip icon types existing.

**Chip operations:**
- `wlAddItem(iso,half,type,id,text)` (2991) — dedups by `id`; pushes; re-renders chips; refreshes picker checkmarks; if `!WL_RENDERING` → mark dirty + auto-save.
- `wlAddCustom(iso,half)` (3038) — normalises CRLF, trims each line, adds a `custom` chip, clears textarea.
- `wlRemoveItem` (3012) — splices, re-renders, dirty + auto-save.
- `wlEditChip` (3021) — puts chip text back into the textarea and removes the chip (no auto-save until re-committed).
- `wlGetWorkText(iso,half)` (3051) — serialises chips + current textarea content; newlines stored as literal `\n`; joins with `'; '` (canonical delimiter). `renderWeekLog` reverses: splits stored `work1/work2` by `'; '`, restores `\\n`→newline, and re-creates chips during render (guarded by `WL_RENDERING`).

`wlAutoAddMeetings(days)` (2774) auto-adds meeting chips: <1pm → h1, ≥1pm → h2, skipping halves with saved content; computes per-day meeting hours → `WL_AUTO_HRS[iso]` (work days floor 9h).

---

#### 3.5.4 Auto-Save Mechanism (state machine)

State vars (1756–1768): `WL_DATA{}`, `WL_DIRTY{}`, `WL_SAVING{}`, `WL_DEBOUNCE{}` (iso→timeout id, 500 ms), `WL_RENDERING` (bool), `WL_ITEMS{}`.

Flow:
1. Any edit → `wlMarkDirty(iso)` sets `WL_DIRTY[iso]=true` and repaints the save button.
2. `_wlAutoSave(iso)` (2613): clears any pending debounce, sets a **500 ms** `setTimeout`; on fire, bails if `!WL_DIRTY[iso] || WL_SAVING[iso]`, else shows "Saving…" and calls `saveWeekEntry(iso, true)`.
3. `saveWeekEntry(iso, autoSave)` (3075): guards `if (WL_SAVING[iso]) return;` (no concurrent saves per row); during **autoSave** it persists only committed chips (not in-progress textarea text) to avoid duplicate chips on later Enter; sets `WL_SAVING=true` and **immediately deletes `WL_DIRTY`** (edits arriving mid-flight re-set it). On success it merges the patch into `WL_DATA[iso]`, stores returned `logId`, and — if `WL_DIRTY[iso]` was re-set mid-flight — schedules one more `_wlAutoSave`; otherwise shows "Saved ✓" (auto) or toast (manual). On error: "Save failed" / toast, then retries once if still dirty.

**The bug `WL_RENDERING` fixes:** `renderWeekLog` re-creates rows and pre-populates chips from saved data; the `onchange`/`oninput` handlers (`wlOnAttendanceChange`, `wlAddItem` during pre-population, intern handler) would mark every freshly-rendered row dirty and fire auto-saves, causing spurious/duplicate `submitWorkLog` writes on every load. `WL_RENDERING=true` is set at the top of `renderWeekLog` and cleared at the end; `wlOnAttendanceChange` and `wlAddItem` skip `wlMarkDirty`/`_wlAutoSave` while it is true. (Member-modal counterpart is `ML_RENDERING`.)

**Triggers that auto-save:** attendance change (regular + intern), Hrs apply (via `wlOnAttendanceChange`), chip add/remove, off-day toggle uncheck, Extra Hrs blur, Remark blur, textarea blur (commits chip then debounces). **Nav commit:** `wlNav`/`wlNavToday` call `_wlCommitAllTextareas()` which commits all open textareas as chips and fire-and-forget saves all dirty rows before navigating. ⚠️ Leave Type / Leave Requested selects only `wlMarkDirty` (no `_wlAutoSave`), so a lone leave-detail change without a subsequent blur/nav may not persist until another field triggers a save.

**Backend save routing (`saveWeekEntry`):**
| Role / state | Backend call | Signature confirmation |
|---|---|---|
| Intern | `saveInternWorkLog(record, email)` | `function saveInternWorkLog(record, email) {` (intern-work-log.gs 64) — upsert by Emp_ID+Date, LockService on insert via `_ilNextId()` |
| Existing `data.logId` | `updateWorkLog(logId, updates, email)` | `function updateWorkLog(logId, updates, email) {` (auth.gs 1294) — owner or admin only |
| New entry | `submitWorkLog(record, email)` | `function submitWorkLog(record, email) {` (auth.gs 1216) — LockService 20 s, `_wlNextId()`, Status/Comments persisted only if `_isManager` |

`submitWorkLog` first 5 lines:
```js
function submitWorkLog(record, email) {
  var lock = LockService.getScriptLock();
  if (!lock.tryLock(20000)) {
    Logger.log('submitWorkLog: could not acquire lock for ' + email);
    return { ok: false, error: 'Server busy — please try again in a moment.' };
```
⚠️ `submitWorkLog` returns a bare `newId` string on success (not `{ok}`); the FE success handler treats the raw return as `logId`. But the lock-fail path returns `{ ok:false, error }` — an object, not an id — which the FE `function(newId){ onSuccess({logId:newId}) }` would treat as a truthy logId object (no `r.ok` check on the non-intern path). Severity: medium — a busy-lock failure could be silently mis-handled as success on a new entry.

**Off-day ≥2 hr gate** (manual save only, 3102): if `att in WL_OFF_TYPES`, `wl-odc` checked, and `extraHrs < 2` → show "Min 2 hrs" warning and abort. **Empty-row no-op:** if no att/work/leave/remark/extra, deletes dirty and returns without a server call.

---

#### 3.5.5 Work Duration (clock widget + display)

The clock widget lives in the dashboard header popup (`#wd-widget` inside `#wd-popup`, toggled by `#wd-hdr-btn` → `_wdTogglePopup`). The work-log grid only **displays** net duration per day (Phase 3). State `_WD = { status, session, breaks }`.

`loadWorkDuration()` (15290): `wdGetStatus(APP._verifiedEmail)` → sets `_WD.*`, renders widget, updates header button, starts ticking timer. `wdGetStatus` is the canonical status read (work-duration.gs 212).

| Action | FE function | Backend call | Signature (work-duration.gs) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Clock In | `wdClockIn()` (15308) | `wdClockIn(email)` | `function wdClockIn(email) {` (35) — resumes today's COMPLETED session or creates new ACTIVE |
| Clock Out (now) | `wdClockOut()` (15322) → `_wdClockOutNow` | `wdClockOut(email, sessionId, '')` | `function wdClockOut(email, sessionId, customTime, reason) {` (89) |
| Clock Out (custom HH:MM) | `_wdClockOutAt()` (15409) | `wdClockOut(email, sessionId, 'HH:MM', reason)` | same; validates `clockOut > clockIn` |
| Start break | `wdStartBreak()` (15441) | `wdStartBreak(email, sessionId)` | (152) — rejects if break already open |
| End break | `wdEndBreak()` (15456) | `wdEndBreak(email, sessionId)` | (178) |
| Edit break mins | `_wdEditBreak()` (15794) → `_wdSaveBreakEdit` | `wdEditBreak(...)` | inline form `wd-break-edit-area`, input `wd-break-edit-mins` |

State machine: `IDLE → ACTIVE → ON_BREAK → ACTIVE → COMPLETED`; clock-in on a COMPLETED today reopens it (resumes, preserving Clock_In). `_wdRenderStatus` (15471) renders 4 states with timer `#wd-timer` and break row `#wd-break-time` + edit pencil. `_wdStartTimer` (15626) ticks net = elapsed − break (uses `Math.max(closedBreaks, storedMins)`).

**Auto clock-out = daily midnight-UTC reset, NOT an 18-hour cap.** `wdAutoClockOut()` (work-duration.gs 367): computes `todayMidnightUTC` (= 05:30 IST), closes any open session whose `Clock_In` is **before** that boundary, setting `Clock_Out = midnight-UTC-as-IST-ISO`, `Status = 'AUTO_CLOSED'`, net = clockIn→midnight − break. Registered hourly: `ScriptApp.newTrigger('wdAutoClockOut').timeBased().everyHours(1).create()` (triggers.gs 26–27). ✓ Ground-truth confirmed — there is no `LIMIT_MS`/18-hour cap.

**Duration display in WL grid:** `getWorkDurationsForDates(email, startDate, endDate)` (693) returns `{ ok:true, data: { 'YYYY-MM-DD':'HH:MM:SS' } }` from `Net_Work_Mins` (or Clock_In→Clock_Out − break). `_wlApplyDurations` patches `wl-dur-{iso}` spans. 🔴 The label icon is the blank Tabler `ti ti-clock-hour-4` (see §3.5.1).

---

#### 3.5.6 Intern free-text attendance branch

- `INTERN_OFF_PATTERNS = /^\s*(holiday|leave|week.?off|off|absent|half.?day|sick|vacation|alt.?week)\s*$/i` (3721).
- `_internIsOffDayText(att)` → true if blank or matches the regex.
- `_internWlOnAttendanceChange(input, dateKey)` (3741): numeric (>0) or non-off text → **enable** work textareas/text/number/buttons (skip the att field + save button); off-keyword or empty → **disable** (opacity .4). Shows the "Worked today" toggle for off text. Updates `+OT` badge from extra hrs and the `wl-intern-hrs-{iso}` display. Then `wlMarkDirty` + `_wlAutoSave`.
- Interns: status/comments columns are non-editable (intern role ∉ MANAGER_ROLES), Hrs calculator replaced by read-only display, save button hidden (auto-save only), data persisted to `Intern_Work_Log` via `saveInternWorkLog` (upsert by Emp_ID+Date). Load uses `getInternWorkLogs` (intern-work-log.gs 126).

---

#### 3.5.7 Manager-only gating summary (Status / Comments)

`_canEditWlStatus(role)` (app.js.html 208) === `_isManager(role)` (auth.gs 189) === `MANAGER_ROLES = ['Super Admin','Admin','Team Captain','Team Facilitator']`. Both Status select and Comments input render as editable only for these roles; everyone else gets read-only spans. Backend `updateWorkLogStatus`/`updateWorkLogComment` throw on non-manager callers, and `submitWorkLog` zeroes Status/Comments for non-managers. ✓ FE and BE gating are consistent.

| Marker | Location | Issue |
|---|---|---|
| 🔴 | `_wlDurHtml` (2421) & `_mlDurHtml` (2499) | `ti ti-clock-hour-4` Tabler icon renders blank (no Tabler font) |
| ⚠️ | Leave Type `wl-lt-{iso}` (2124) / Leave Requested `wl-lr-{iso}` (2134) | `onchange` only marks dirty — no `_wlAutoSave`; lone leave-detail change may not persist without another save trigger |
| ⚠️ | `_wlPickerItemsHtml` (2925) | Picker only offers Projects + Tasks; no Sub-Function/Sub-Task source though those chip types exist |
| ⚠️ | `saveWeekEntry` new-entry path (3210) + `submitWorkLog` (1218) | lock-fail returns `{ok:false}` object but FE treats raw return as `logId` (no `r.ok` check) — busy-lock could be mis-read as success |
| ⚠️ | `_attEffHours` (1773) vs `getMyWlWeekSummary` `BASE_HRS` (1344) | base-hours table duplicated FE/BE, must be kept in sync manually |
| ⚠️ | `WL_ATTENDANCE_OPTIONS` (1845) | no blank option in list; a row defaulting to `att=''` has no matching `<option>` to select |

### 3.5 Weekly Summary modal (AI-generated, editable)

### 3.5.1 Entry point + modal shell

**Trigger button** (Work Log view header, `index.html:2128`):

```html
<button class="btn btn-ghost btn-sm" onclick="openWeeklySummaryModal()"
  title="View AI-generated weekly summary for MIS">
  <span class="material-symbols-outlined" style="font-size:16px">summarize</span>Weekly Summary</button>
```
- The button is shown to **all roles** in the Work Log header (no role gating on the button itself). The MIS Report nav item is the access-gated surface; this per-user summary modal is open to everyone. ⚠️ **Gap (low):** brief implies "for MIS" but any employee can open/generate/edit their own weekly summary regardless of MIS access.

**Modal element** (`index.html:3585–3599`):
- id: `weekly-summary-modal`, classes `modal-bg hidden`
- header title: `Weekly Summary`; subtitle container `#ws-modal-sub` (date range + an `editable` pill, injected by JS)
- close X button: `<button class="modal-x" onclick="closeModal('weekly-summary-modal')">✕</button>`
- body container: `#ws-modal-body`
- footer container: `#ws-modal-footer` (flex, right-aligned, wraps)
- inner width: `max-width:620px`

**Close mechanism:** backdrop click `onclick="closeModal('weekly-summary-modal',event)"` (only closes when `e.target === el(id)`), the X button, and the footer "Close" buttons — all call `closeModal('weekly-summary-modal')`.

### 3.5.2 State + open flow

Module-level state (`app.js.html:16772–16774`):
- `_WS_BULLETS = []` — bullet text array currently shown
- `_WS_EDIT_IDX = null` — index of bullet being edited (null = none)
- `_WS_CUR_START = ''` — weekStart key (`YYYY-MM-DD`) of the open summary

**`openWeeklySummaryModal()`** (`16798`):
1. Computes `weekStart` = local Monday of `WL_ANCHOR` (or today) via `_wsLocalMonday`/`_wsIso`; `weekEnd` = Monday+6.
2. Sets `_WS_CUR_START`, `_WS_EDIT_IDX=null`.
3. `#ws-modal-sub` = `<span>{Mon 8 Jun – Sun 14 Jun 2026}</span>` + an `editable` uppercase pill (via `_wsFmtRangeLabel`).
4. Body = loading state `⏳ Loading summary…`; footer cleared.
5. `openModal('weekly-summary-modal')`.
6. **`google.script.run` call:** `getWeeklySummary(APP._verifiedEmail, weekStart)`
   - success → `_wsRenderModalContent(r, weekStart)`
   - failure → `_wsRenderModalError('Failed to load summary. Please try again.')`

> Note: the week shown follows `WL_ANCHOR` (the Work Log view's current week). Open the modal from a different Work Log week → that week's summary loads. ✅ wired.

### 3.5.3 `_wsRenderModalContent(r, weekStart)` (16823)

| Branch | Condition | Render |
|---|---|---|
| Error | `!r || !r.ok` | `_wsRenderModalError(r.error || 'Failed to load summary.')` |
| Not found | `r.found === false` | "No summary for this week yet" empty state + footer **Close** + **Generate Now** (navy `btn-accent`, icon `auto_awesome`) → `_wsGenerateNow(weekStart)` |
| Found | `r.found` | parses `r.content` into `_WS_BULLETS` (split `\n`, strip leading `• ` / `- `, filter empty), renders bullets, footer = "Generated {ts}{ · edited}" + **Copy** + **Close** |

`generatedAt` formatted by `_wsFormatTs` → e.g. `8 Jun 2026, 5:30 AM`.

### 3.5.4 Bullet UI (crimson-dot list)

**`_wsRenderBullets(body, weekStart)`** (16885): header "Click any point to edit" + `#ws-bullet-list` (each bullet via `_wsBulletRowHtml`) + an **Add a point** dashed-border row at the bottom (`onclick="_wsAddBullet(weekStart)"`, `+ Add a point`, hover turns indigo `var(--p)`).

**`_wsBulletRowHtml(text, idx, qs)`** (16912): returns `''` for empty/placeholder/zero-width-space bullets (skips floating dot). Otherwise a clickable row with:
- a **crimson dot** `7×7px border-radius:50% background:var(--danger)` (LG crimson) ✅
- the bullet text (`esc`-escaped), hover background `#eef1f4`
- `onclick="_wsStartEditBullet(idx, qs)"`

**`_wsStartEditBullet(idx, weekStart)`** (16927): replaces the row with an inline editor:
| Element | id/class | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Textarea | `ws-ta-{idx}`, `rows="2"` | pre-filled with `_WS_BULLETS[idx]` (zero-width space stripped); auto-focus + select |
| Save btn | navy `#2D3E51` filled, icon `check` | `onclick="_wsConfirmBullet(idx, qs)"`, title "Save" |
| Cancel btn | ghost bordered, icon `undo` | `onclick="_wsCancelBullet(qs)"`, title "Cancel" |
| Delete btn | red bordered `#fca5a5`/`#dc2626`, icon `delete` | `onclick="_wsDeleteBullet(idx, qs)"`, title "Delete this point" |

> Icons are Material Symbols (`check`/`undo`/`delete`) — render correctly. ✅

**Handlers:**

| Function | line | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `_wsConfirmBullet(idx, weekStart)` | 16982 | reads textarea; empty → splice out the bullet (no save); else set `_WS_BULLETS[idx]`, re-render, **`_wsPersist`** (auto-save) |
| `_wsCancelBullet(weekStart)` | 16999 | if the edited bullet is empty/placeholder → splice it out; re-render; **no save** |
| `_wsDeleteBullet(idx, weekStart)` | 17009 | splice out bullet, re-render, **`_wsPersist`** (auto-save) |
| `_wsAddBullet(weekStart)` | 17016 | push zero-width-space placeholder, re-render, then `setTimeout(30ms)` → `_wsStartEditBullet(newIdx)` (clean textarea) |

### 3.5.5 Auto-save per bullet — `_wsPersist(weekStart)` (17028)

- Joins `_WS_BULLETS` as `• {b}\n…` → `content`.
- **`google.script.run.saveWeeklySummary(APP._verifiedEmail, weekStart, content)`**
  - success: if `!r.ok` → `toast(r.error || 'Auto-save failed', 'error')`; else inserts/updates a `.ws-saved-hint` span at the front of the footer showing **`Saved ✓`** in `var(--ok)`, cleared after 2.5 s.
  - failure: `toast('Auto-save failed', 'error')`.
- ✅ wired: every confirm/delete persists immediately (no manual Save button). Cancel and add-placeholder do not persist.

### 3.5.6 Footer actions

| Button | shown when | onclick | effect |
|---|---|---|---|
| Generate Now | summary not found | `_wsGenerateNow(weekStart)` | regenerate via AI then reload |
| Copy | summary found | `_wsCopyAll()` | copies `• …\n…` to clipboard |
| Close | always | `closeModal('weekly-summary-modal')` | hide modal |

**`_wsCopyAll()`** (17050): builds `• {b}\n…`; empty → `toast('Nothing to copy','error')`; uses `navigator.clipboard.writeText` (fallback `_wsCopyFallback` via `document.execCommand('copy')`); success → `toast('Copied to clipboard','success')`. ✅
- `_wsCopyToClipboard()` and `_wsCopyText(text)` are aliases/wrappers (17090–17098); `_wsCopyToClipboard` just calls `_wsCopyAll()`.

**`_wsGenerateNow(weekStart)`** (17074):
1. Body → `⏳ Generating summary with AI… this may take 10–20 seconds`; footer cleared.
2. **`generateMyWeeklySummary(APP._verifiedEmail, weekStart)`**
   - success `r.ok` → chained **`getWeeklySummary(APP._verifiedEmail, weekStart)`** → `_wsRenderModalContent(r2)`
   - success `!r.ok` → `_wsRenderModalError(r.error || 'Generation failed.')`
   - failure → `_wsRenderModalError('Generation failed: …')`

### 3.5.7 Backend (`weekly-summary.gs`)

Sheet constants: `_WS_SHEET = 'Weekly_Summary'`, `_MIS_SHEET = 'MIS_Access'`.

**`getWeeklySummary(callerEmail, weekStart)`** (348):
```js
function getWeeklySummary(callerEmail, weekStart) {
  try {
    var user = getCurrentUser(callerEmail);   // validate identity (throws for unknown/inactive)
    var ce   = String(user.email || '').toLowerCase();
    var row  = _wsGetAllSafe(_WS_SHEET).filter(function(r) {
```
- Matches own row by lowercased `Email` + normalised `Week_Start`. Returns `{ok,found:false,weekStart}` if none; else `{ok,found:true,summaryId,content,isEdited,generatedAt,editedAt,weekStart,weekEnd}`.

**`saveWeeklySummary(callerEmail, weekStart, newContent)`** (374):
```js
function saveWeeklySummary(callerEmail, weekStart, newContent) {
  try {
    var user = getCurrentUser(callerEmail);   // validate identity (throws for unknown/inactive)
    var ce   = String(user.email || '').toLowerCase();
    var row  = _wsGetAllSafe(_WS_SHEET).filter(function(r) {
```
- Own-row only; `dbUpdate` sets `Content`, `Is_Edited:'TRUE'`, `Edited_At`, `Edited_By:user.email`; returns `{ok:true}` or `{ok:false,error:'Summary not found'}`.

**`generateMyWeeklySummary(callerEmail, weekStart)`** (400):
```js
function generateMyWeeklySummary(callerEmail, weekStart) {
  try {
    var user = getCurrentUser(callerEmail);   // validate identity (throws for unknown/inactive)
    var emp  = user.emp;
    var monDate = new Date(weekStart + 'T00:00:00Z');
```
- Builds 7 UTC week dates; indexes Work_Log + Intern_Work_Log rows by `Emp_ID`; if no logs → `{ok:false,error:'No work logs found for this week — fill in your work log first.'}`; else calls `_wsGenerateWithAI`, upserts row, returns `{ok:true,content}`.

**`_wsGenerateWithAI(empName, weekStart, weekEnd, dayEntries)`** (126):
- Requires Script Property `GEMINI_API_KEY` (else `{ok:false,error:'GEMINI_API_KEY not set in Script Properties'}`).
- Endpoint: `https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-2.5-flash:generateContent?key=…` ✅ model id exact.
- `generationConfig: { temperature: 0.4, maxOutputTokens: 2048 }` ✅.
- Prompt asks for **5–10 past-tense bullets**, each prefixed `•`, no headers/numbers; deduplicates 1st/2nd-half chips; skips empty days; includes attendance + OT-hours per day.
- HTTP non-200 → `{ok:false,error:'AI request failed (HTTP …): …'}`; success parses `candidates[0].content.parts[0].text`.

**`generateWeeklySummaries()`** (229) — Monday trigger target:
- Cleans any leftover one-shot continuation trigger (`WS_CONT_TRIGGER_ID`).
- Computes previous UTC week (Mon–Sun ending the day before run).
- Iterates active employees; skips those already with a row for the week (`existing` map) and those with no log data.
- **Time-budget guard:** if elapsed > 300000 ms (5 min), schedules a one-shot `generateWeeklySummaries` continuation trigger `.after(60s)`, saves its id to `WS_CONT_TRIGGER_ID`, breaks. ✅ continuation logic present.
- Per employee: `_wsGenerateWithAI` → `_wsUpsertSummary`; `Utilities.sleep(500)` pacing.

**`_wsUpsertSummary(record)`** (98): deterministic `Summary_ID = 'WS-' + empId + '-' + weekStartNoDashes`; re-reads sheet, updates if exists else inserts (prevents trigger-vs-on-demand duplicates).

**`getMisSummaries(callerEmail, weekStart)`** (456):
```js
function getMisSummaries(callerEmail, weekStart) {
  try {
    var user = getCurrentUser(callerEmail);   // validate identity (throws for unknown/inactive)
    if (!_wsHasMisAccess(user.email)) return { ok: false, error: 'Access denied' };
```
- MIS-gated; dedups by `Summary_ID` keeping latest `Generated_At`; returns `{ok,weekStart,summaries:[{empId,empName,email,content,isEdited,weekEnd}]}` sorted by `Emp_Name`.

**`_wsHasMisAccess(email)`** (496) / **`wsCheckMisAccess(email)`** (507): checks lowercased `Email` against `MIS_Access` sheet; `wsCheckMisAccess` is the public wrapper called from `getInitialPayload` (`hasMisAccess`).

**Trigger registration** (`triggers.gs:32`):
```js
ScriptApp.newTrigger('generateWeeklySummaries')
  .timeBased().onWeekDay(ScriptApp.WeekDay.MONDAY).atHour(0).inTimezone('Etc/UTC').create();
```
✅ Monday 12 AM UTC (05:30 IST).

**Gaps (3.5):**
- ⚠️ `_wsGenerateNow` failure handler reads `err.message` but a GAS thrown error object structure may differ — minor cosmetic. (low)
- ⚠️ The WS button has no role gate; the "for MIS" framing is misleading — every employee can generate/edit their own summary. (low — by design but undocumented in brief)
- `_wsCopyText` (17090) is called by `exportMisReport()` (17194) to copy the MIS report text to clipboard; `_wsCopyToClipboard()` (17098) is a thin wrapper that delegates to `_wsCopyAll()`.
