### 3.6 Calendar

### 3.6.1 View shell (`index.html:2379–2413`)

- View id: `view-calendar`; header title "Calendar", sub "Tasks, deadlines, leaves & holidays across the team".
- Header action: **+ Add Holiday** button `#btn-add-holiday-cal` `class="btn btn-primary nav-admin-only hidden"` → `openHolidayModal()` (admin-only, gated by `nav-admin-only`).
- Toolbar: prev `‹` (`calNav(-1)`), **Today** (`calToday()`), next `›` (`calNav(1)`), title `#cal-title`.
- Five filter chips (all start `active`):

| Chip | id | onclick | dot colour |
|---|---|---|---|
| Task Due Dates | `cal-f-task` | `_calToggleFilter('task')` | `#1565c0` |
| Events & Meetings | `cal-f-meeting` | `_calToggleFilter('meeting')` | `#00897b` |
| Project Deadlines | `cal-f-project` | `_calToggleFilter('project')` | `#c62828` |
| Holidays | `cal-f-holiday` | `_calToggleFilter('holiday')` | `#2e7d32` |
| Leaves | `cal-f-leave` | `_calToggleFilter('leave')` | `#6a1b9a` |

- Manager-only selects (`nav-mgr-only hidden`):

**Field: Team filter (`cal-team-sel`)**
- element: `<select>` · id `cal-team-sel` (class `cal-member-sel nav-mgr-only hidden`)
- placeholder: (none)
- required: no · default: `Organisation`
- options: static first `<option value="">Organisation</option>`; rest **dynamically populated** by `_calPopulateMemberSel` — admins get `Object.keys(TEAM_HIERARCHY).sort()` (all 8 teams), non-admin managers get only their own `u.team`.
- handlers: `onchange="_calTeamChange()"`
- wired: yes · data source: `TEAM_HIERARCHY` + `APP.currentUser`
- gaps: hidden entirely for non-managers (`_calPopulateMemberSel` early-return + `.hidden`).

**Field: Member filter (`cal-member-sel`)**
- element: `<select>` · id `cal-member-sel`
- default: `All Members` (`value=""`); after team chosen → `All in {team}`
- options: dynamically rebuilt by `_calTeamChange` from `APP.employees` filtered to the selected team, sorted by `Name`; each `<option value="{Emp_ID}">{Name}</option>`.
- handlers: `onchange="_calMemberChange()"`
- wired: yes · data source: `APP.employees`

- Day-of-week header row: Sunday … Saturday (static).
- Grid body: `#cal-body` (initial "Loading calendar…").

### 3.6.2 Data load — `loadCalendarData()` (12049)

Called from `navigate('calendar')`. Steps:
1. Body → loading state; title set; `_calPopulateMemberSel()`.
2. **`google.script.run.getCalendarData(APP.currentUser.email)`**
   - success: reset `CAL.evMap`; `_calAddTasks()` + `_calAddProjects()` (from in-memory `APP.tasks`/`APP.projects`); if `r.ok` → `_calAddLeaves(r.leaves)`, `_calAddHolidays(r.holidays)`, `_calAddMeetings(r.meetings)`; `renderCalendar()`.
   - failure: still adds tasks + projects from local data, `renderCalendar()` (degrades gracefully). ✅

> Note: `r.workLogs` is returned by the backend but **never added to the calendar** — `_calAddWorkLogs` exists (12131) but is never called from `loadCalendarData`. ⚠️ **Gap (low):** work logs are fetched server-side (cost) but unused in the calendar render.

### 3.6.3 Event sources

| Source | function | filter | colour key |
|---|---|---|---|
| Tasks | `_calAddTasks` | `Due_Date` set & not `_isDone` | `task` |
| Projects | `_calAddProjects` | `Deadline` set & not `_isDone` | `project` |
| Leaves | `_calAddLeaves` | per-day span Start→End | `leave` |
| Holidays | `_calAddHolidays` | `unshift` (first) | `holiday` |
| Meetings | `_calAddMeetings` | by `m.start` date | `meeting` |

`CAL` state (11914): `{year, month, popupDate, evMap, filters{task,meeting,project,holiday,leave:true}, memberFilter:'', empTeamMap:{}}`.

### 3.6.4 `renderCalendar()` (12148)

- Builds a 6-week × 7-col grid (42 cells, Sunday-start), out-of-month cells flagged `out`.
- Per week: collects visible events (`_calGetVisibleEvs` honours filter toggles + member filter), spans multi-day leaves across columns, lays out up to `MAX_EV_ROWS=4` rows, overflow → "+N more".
- Event chips: tasks → `openTaskDetail(id)`; meetings → `goToMeeting(id)`; others → `calDayClick`.
- `TYPE_ORDER`: holiday 0, meeting 1, task 2, project 3, leave 4.
- ✅ wired.

`calNav(dir)` (12289) shifts month; `calToday()` (12296) resets to current month — both call `renderCalendar()` (no re-fetch; uses cached `CAL.evMap`). ⚠️ **Gap (low):** navigating months never re-fetches; meetings/leaves outside the originally-loaded set are not range-aware (backend `getCalendarData` returns whatever it returns — meetings come from `getMeetings` upcoming only). Past months show no meetings.

### 3.6.5 Day popup — `_calOpenPopup(e, ds)` (12328)

- Popup element `#cal-popup` (`index.html:3467`): date label `#cal-popup-date`, events `#cal-popup-events`, admin actions `#cal-popup-admin-actions` (hidden unless admin) with **+ Add Holiday for this day** → `openHolidayModal(CAL.popupDate)`.
- Sections rendered by `CAL_SECTIONS` order (Task Due Dates, Events & Meetings, Project Deadlines, Holidays, Leaves).
- Holiday rows get an admin **Delete** button → `deleteHoliday(id)`.
- Task rows clickable → `openTaskDetail`.
- Closes on outside click (`document.addEventListener('click', calPopupClose, {once:true})`) or `calPopupClose()` / X button.

### 3.6.6 Backend — `getCalendarData(email)` (`leaves.gs:195`)

```js
function getCalendarData(email) {
  try {
    var user = getCurrentUser(email);
    var empMap = {};
    dbGetAll('Employees').forEach(function(e) {
```
- Approved leaves scoped: admin → all; manager → subordinates + self (`getSubordinateIds`); member → own.
- Holidays mapped (`Date` → first 10 chars).
- Work logs (own for members; all for managers/admins) capped at 90 — **returned but unused by frontend** (see 3.6.2 gap).
- Meetings via `getMeetings(email)` (RBAC-filtered, upcoming).
- Returns `{ok,leaves,holidays,workLogs,meetings}`.

**Gaps (3.6):**
- ⚠️ Server fetches 90 work logs that the frontend never renders. (low)
- ⚠️ Month navigation does not re-fetch; past/future months beyond loaded meetings/leaves appear empty for those types. (low)
- NOTE: `calEvClick` (12302) is defined but `renderCalendar` chips wire `onclick` inline (not via `calEvClick`); only used as a legacy helper. (low)

### 3.7 Meetings

### 3.7.1 View shell (`index.html:2462–2616`)

- View id `view-meetings`; header "Meetings" / "Start, join or schedule Google Meet calls"; refresh button → `loadMeetings()`.
- **Quick action cards:**
  - **Start Instant Meeting** → `startInstantMeeting()` (opens `https://meet.google.com/new`).
  - **Join a Meeting** — input `#meet-join-inp` placeholder `abc-defg-hij or full link`; **Join** → `joinMeeting()`.
  - **Custom Meeting** → `openScheduleForm('custom')`.
- **Meeting Templates** section `#meet-templates-section` (display gated by `_meetShowTemplates`): **Company Meeting** card `#meet-tpl-company` (admins only) → `openScheduleForm('company')`; **Team Meeting** card `#meet-tpl-team` (managers only) → `openScheduleForm('team')`.
- **Created banner** `#meet-created-banner`.
- **Upcoming Meetings** list `#meet-list`.

**Field: Join input**
- element: `<input class="fc">` · id `meet-join-inp`
- placeholder: `abc-defg-hij or full link` · required: no · default: (none)
- handlers: button `onclick="joinMeeting()"`
- validation: empty → `toast('Enter a meeting code or link','error')`; normalises code to `https://meet.google.com/{code}`.
- wired: yes (opens new tab) · data source: hardcoded URL.

### 3.7.2 Schedule form (`#meet-schedule-form`, hidden) — fields

**Field: type (hidden)**
- element: `<input type="hidden">` · id `mf-type` · default `custom` (set per `openScheduleForm(type)`).

**Field: Meeting Title \***
- element: `<input class="fc">` · id `mf-title`
- placeholder: static `e.g. Weekly Sync`; overwritten by `openScheduleForm` per type (`e.g. All-Hands Company Meeting` / `e.g. Weekly Team Standup` / `e.g. Project Kickoff`)
- required: yes (client: empty → `toast('Meeting title is required','error')`)
- default: (none)

**Field: Date & Time \***
- element: `<input type="datetime-local" class="fc">` · id `mf-datetime`
- required: yes (empty → `toast('Date and time are required','error')`)
- default: set by `openScheduleForm` to next rounded 30 min.

**Field: Duration**
- element: `<select class="fc">` · id `mf-duration`
- required: no · default: `30 minutes` (`selected`)
- options (verbatim, static):
```html
<option value="15">15 minutes</option>
<option value="30" selected>30 minutes</option>
<option value="45">45 minutes</option>
<option value="60">1 hour</option>
<option value="90">1.5 hours</option>
<option value="120">2 hours</option>
```

**Field: Team \*** (`#mf-team-row`, shown only for `type==='team'`)
- element: `<select class="fc">` · id `mf-team`
- options: dynamically populated by `_meetPopulateTeamSel()` → `Object.keys(TEAM_HIERARCHY).sort()`, current user's team pre-`selected`.
- data source: `TEAM_HIERARCHY`.

**Field: Description (optional)**
- element: `<textarea class="fc" rows="2">` · id `mf-desc` · placeholder `Agenda or notes…` · required: no.

**INVITE ATTENDEES** (`#mf-explicit-attendees`, shown only for `type==='custom'`):

*Assign to Teams multiselect* (`#mtg-asgn-teams`):
- ms-widget; placeholder `Select teams to invite…` (`#mtg-asgn-teams-ph`); chips `#mtg-asgn-teams-chips`; dropdown `#mtg-asgn-teams-dd`; hidden input `#f-mtg-teams`.
- populated by `_buildTeamsMultiSelect('mtg-asgn-teams','')` (within `_mtgInitAssignFilters`).
- toggle `onclick="_msToggle('mtg-asgn-teams')"`.

*Team filter* (`#mtg-asgn-filter-team`):
- element `<select>`; default `<option value="">All Teams</option>`; populated by `_mtgInitAssignFilters` as optgroups per division with sub-departments indented.
- handler `onchange="_mtgBuildPeoplePicker()"` · data source `TEAM_HIERARCHY`.

*Role filter* (`#mtg-asgn-filter-role`):
- element `<select>`; default `<option value="">All Roles</option>`; populated from `ROLES` constant.
- handler `onchange="_mtgBuildPeoplePicker()"`.

*Select people multiselect* (`#mtg-asgn-people`):
- ms-widget; placeholder `Select people to invite…`; hidden input `#f-mtg-people`; built by `_mtgBuildPeoplePicker` from `APP.employees` (excludes self, respects team+role filters), each item `{value:Emp_ID, label:Name, sub:'Role · Team'}`.
- toggle `onclick="_msToggle('mtg-asgn-people')"`.

**Auto-invite notice** (`#mf-auto-invite-note`, shown for `type!=='custom'`): text `#mf-auto-invite-text` set to "All members of the selected team will be auto-invited." (team) / "All active employees in the company will be auto-invited." (company).

**Footer buttons:**
- **Cancel** → `closeScheduleForm()` (clears title + desc, hides form).
- **Create Meeting** (label `#mf-submit-label`, id `#mf-submit-btn`) → `submitScheduleMeeting()`; label varies per type (`Schedule Company Meeting` / `Schedule Team Meeting` / `Create Meeting`).

### 3.7.3 `openScheduleForm(type)` (8082)

Sets `mf-type`, the title-text/type-bar/icons, shows/hides `mf-team-row` (team), `mf-auto-invite-note` (non-custom), `mf-explicit-attendees` (custom); for team → `_meetPopulateTeamSel`; sets default datetime; for custom → `_mtgInitAssignFilters()`; scrolls form into view.

### 3.7.4 `submitScheduleMeeting()` (8140)

- Reads title, datetime, duration, desc, type.
- Validates title + datetime (toasts).
- `startIso = new Date(dtVal).toISOString()`; disables button, label "Creating…".
- **custom path:** reads `_msGetIds('mtg-asgn-people')` + `_msGetIds('mtg-asgn-teams')`; builds `record = {title, startIso, durationMins, description, type:'personal', attendeeIds, attendeeTeams}` → **`scheduleMeeting(record, APP._verifiedEmail)`**.
- **template path (company/team):** reads `mf-team` for team; → **`scheduleMeetingWithTemplate({type,title,startIso,durationMins,description,team}, APP.currentUser.email)`**.
- success `onSuccess(r)`: re-enable button; if `!r.ok` → error toast; else `closeScheduleForm()`, `_showMeetCreatedBanner(r)`, `loadMeetings()`, `toast('Meeting scheduled!','success')`.
- failure `onError(err)`: re-enable, `toast('Error: '+err.message,'error')`.

> ⚠️ **Note:** custom meetings pass `type:'personal'` to `scheduleMeeting` (not `'custom'`). Backend stores it as `tm_type:'personal'`; calendar render in `_calAddMeetings` treats anything not company/team as label-less custom. Internally consistent.

### 3.7.5 Meeting list render — `_renderMeetingsList(meetings)` (7991)

- Updates `#badge-meetings`; empty → "No upcoming meetings." empty state.
- Each card `#mtg-card-{id}`: type badge (Company/Team/none), title, date/time, attendees (first 3 + "+N more"), description, attendees line (first 5 + "+N more").
- Actions: **Join** (`m.meetLink`, opens tab), Calendar link (`m.htmlLink`), **Cancel** (icon `cancel`) shown when `canCancel` = admin OR manager OR `m.creatorEmail===me` OR `m.organizerEmail===me` → `cancelMeetingById(this, m.id)`.

**`cancelMeetingById(btn, calEventId)`** (8201): `confirm('Cancel this meeting for all attendees?')`; loading btn; **`google.script.run.cancelMeetingById(calEventId, APP.currentUser.email)`**; success → `toast('Meeting cancelled','success')` + `loadMeetings()`.

**`goToMeeting(calEventId)`** (8217): `navigate('meetings')` then after 900 ms scrolls + outlines `#mtg-card-{id}` (used by calendar meeting chips).

### 3.7.6 Backend (`meet.gs`)

**`getMeetings(email)`** (499):
```js
function getMeetings(email) {
  try {
    var user    = getCurrentUser(email);
    var userTeam = user.emp ? (user.emp['Team'] || '') : '';
    var userObj = {
```
- Calls `getUpcomingMeetings()`, filters via `_userCanSeeMeeting`; returns `{ok,meetings,isAdmin,isManager,userTeam}`.

**`_userCanSeeMeeting(m, userObj)`** (464): admins all; new-model (has `tmCreatorEmail`) → creator/organizer/explicit-attendee-by-Emp_ID/explicit-team/direct-calendar-attendee else hidden; legacy → team/company/creator/direct-attendee.

**`scheduleMeeting(recordOrTitle, emailOrStartIso, …)`** (32):
```js
function scheduleMeeting(recordOrTitle, emailOrStartIso, durationMins, attendeeEmails, description, extProps) {
  try {
    var title, startIso, durMs, descText, allEmails, newStyleProps;
    if (recordOrTitle && typeof recordOrTitle === 'object') {
```
- Dual signature (record-object new style / positional legacy); resolves attendee emails from Emp_IDs + team names; stores shared extended props `tm_type/tm_team/tm_attendee_ids/tm_attendee_teams/tm_creator_email`.

**`scheduleMeetingWithTemplate(record, email)`** (320):
```js
function scheduleMeetingWithTemplate(record, email) {
  try {
    var user = getCurrentUser(email);
    var type = record.type || 'custom';
    if (type === 'company' && !_isAdmin(user.role))
```
- Role-gates company (admin) and team (manager); resolves attendee emails (all active for company, team members for team); calls `scheduleMeeting` legacy + Gmail notice + per-user calendar sync + reminder trigger.

**`cancelMeetingById(calEventId, email)`** (530): fetches event, allows cancel only if organizer/creator or admin, audits `DELETE_MEETING`, calls `deleteMeeting`.

**Gaps (3.7):**
- ⚠️ `_renderMeetAttendees()` (7953/8046) builds chips into `#mf-attendees` with `toggleAttendee`, but the **custom schedule form uses the ms-widget pickers (`mtg-asgn-people`/`mtg-asgn-teams`) instead** — `mf-attendees`/`toggleAttendee` appear to be **dead/legacy** (no `#mf-attendees` element in the meetings view HTML I read). `submitScheduleMeeting` never reads them. (medium — orphaned code path)
- ⚠️ `cancelMeetingById` frontend success shows the meeting cancelled, but `canCancel` lets any manager/admin see the Cancel button while backend only permits organizer/creator/admin — a non-admin manager who is not the organizer will get a backend "Not authorized" error after clicking. (low — UX mismatch)

### 3.8 Org Chart

### 3.8.1 View shell (`index.html:2618–2643`)

- View id `view-org-chart`; header "Org Chart" / "Leveraged Growth — Company Structure".
- Header actions: **Reset** → `renderOrgChart()`, **Expand All** → `orgExpandAll()`, **Collapse** → `orgCollapseAll()`.
- Legend `#oc-legend`; pan/zoom wrapper `#org-chart-wrap` containing `#oc-viewport` > `#org-chart-container`.
- **Zoom bar** (`.oc-zoom-bar`): **Find me** → `ocFindMe()`; **zoom out** → `ocZoom(-1)`; level label `#oc-zoom-label` (`100 %`); **zoom in** → `ocZoom(1)`; **Reset view** (fit) → `ocResetView()`.

### 3.8.2 Render flow — `renderOrgChart()` (13840)

- Resets `_ocExpanded/_ocParentOf/_ocSiblingExpanded`, zoom/pan to 1/0/0.
- Loading state; **`google.script.run.getOrgChartData()`** (no email param — public) → `_buildOrgChart(r.employees)`; failure/error → inline message.

**`_buildOrgChart(employees)`** (13864):
- Groups employees team → subDept → []; team order = all `TEAM_HIERARCHY` keys + extras from data (never drops empty teams/subdepts).
- Team colour from `_OC_TEAM_COLORS`:
```
1. Founder's Office  #6a1b9a   2. Student Success  #0277bd
3. Knowledge         #00695c   4. Growth (Marketing) #e65100
5. Tech              #1565c0   6. Consulting       #880e4f
7. Operations - PP & Admin #2e7d32   8. Operations - FP&A #6a4c93
```
- Role priority (`_OC_ROLE_PRIORITY`): Super Admin 1, Admin 2, Team Captain 3, Team Facilitator 4, Team Member 5 (else 99). Used to pick sub-dept head/deputy and team head.
- Builds root card "Leveraged Growth" (`#1a237e`), pre-builds all flat rows, renders legend, attaches pan/zoom listeners, auto-expands root, auto-fits via `ocResetView`.

### 3.8.3 Cards + expansion

- `_ocRenderCard` (14005): avatar (initials or icon), name/head/role, employee count, deputy section, expand button. Label per level: root → "X teams"; team → "X departments"/"no departments"; dept leaf → "X members"/"no members". Toggle icon Material Symbol `expand_more`/`expand_less`.
- `_ocRenderMemPanel` (14079): leaf member list (`Name` + `Role`), or "No members assigned yet".
- `_ocToggle(id)` (14132): flat accordion expand/collapse, repositions child row under parent (`_ocPositionRow`), collapses sibling-expanded branches.
- `orgExpandAll` (14193): switches to `oc-nested-mode`, nests all child ULs and shows all member panels.
- `orgCollapseAll` (14247): restores flat layout, collapses all, re-expands root.

### 3.8.4 Zoom / Pan

- State: `_ocZoom=1, _ocPanX=0, _ocPanY=0`; drag state vars.
- `_ocApplyTransform(animate)` (13702): sets `#oc-viewport` transform `translate(panX,panY) scale(zoom)`, updates `#oc-zoom-label`.
- `ocZoom(dir)` (13711): ±0.15 step, clamped 0.2–3, zoom about wrap centre.
- `ocResetView()` (13724): fit-to-wrap zoom (clamped ≤1, ≥0.2), centres content.
- `ocFindMe()` (13744): expands root, centres + highlights the current user's team card (`#oc-card-root_{idx}`); falls back to `ocResetView` if team unknown/not found.
- `_ocAttachListeners()` (13815): wheel = pan, Ctrl/⌘+wheel = zoom-at-cursor; mouse drag pan; touch (1-finger) pan. Listeners attached once (`_ocListenersAttached`).

### 3.8.5 Backend — `getOrgChartData()` (`auth.gs:817`)

```js
function getOrgChartData() {
  try {
    var emps = dbGetAll('Employees')
      .filter(function(e) { return String(e['Is_Active'] || '').toUpperCase() === 'TRUE'; })
      .map(function(e) {
```
- Returns `{ok, employees:[{empId,name,role,designation,team,subDept,managerId}]}`, active employees only.
- ⚠️ **Note:** takes **no `email` param** and applies **no RBAC** — any logged-in user sees the full active-employee org chart. (low — likely intentional for a directory/org view, but undocumented)

**Gaps (3.8):**
- ⚠️ `getOrgChartData()` is unauthenticated (no caller validation, no role scoping). (low)
- NOTE: `_ocRenderCard` supports a `node.deputies[]` array (multiple) but `_buildOrgChart` only ever sets a single `node.deputy` (string); the `deputies` branch is unreachable for generated nodes. (low)

### 3.9 My Leaves

### 3.9.1 View shell (`index.html:2416–2424`)

- View id `view-my-leaves`; header "My Leaves" / "Your leave requests and their status".
- Header action: **+ Request Leave** → `openLeaveModal()`.
- Container `#my-leaves-container`.

### 3.9.2 Load + render

**`loadMyLeaves()`** (12800): loading state; **`google.script.run.getMyLeaves(APP.currentUser.email)`** → `renderMyLeaves(r.leaves || [])`; error/failure → inline message. Called from `navigate('my-leaves')`.

**`renderMyLeaves(leaves)`** (12814): empty → empty-state "No leave requests yet"; else a table with columns: **ID · Type · Start · End · Days · Reason · Status · Reviewed By · Notes**. Type and Status rendered as `pill()`; dates via `fmtDate`; `Notes` reads `l.Review_Notes`.

### 3.9.3 Leave request modal (`#leave-modal`, `index.html:3477–3509`)

- Trigger: `openLeaveModal()`; header "Request Leave"; close X / Cancel / backdrop (`closeModal('leave-modal',event)`).
- `openLeaveModal()` (12905) resets fields: type `''`, start/end = today (`_localIso`), reason `''`, days info "1 day", enables submit.

**Field: Leave Type \***
- element: `<select class="fc">` · id `lv-type`
- placeholder: `— Select Type —` (`value=""`)
- required: yes (client: empty → `toast('Please select a leave type.','error')`)
- default: `''`
- options (verbatim, static):
```html
<option value="">— Select Type —</option>
<option>Annual Leave</option>
<option>Sick Leave</option>
<option>Casual Leave</option>
<option>Maternity Leave</option>
<option>Paternity Leave</option>
<option>Unpaid Leave</option>
<option value="Half Day">Half Day</option>
```
- data source: hardcoded.

**Field: Start Date \***
- element: `<input type="date" class="fc">` · id `lv-start` · default today · handler `onchange="calcLeaveDays()"` · required yes.

**Field: End Date \***
- element: `<input type="date" class="fc">` · id `lv-end` · default today · handler `onchange="calcLeaveDays()"` · required yes.

**Field: Days info (computed)**
- element: `<div>` · id `lv-days-info`; set by `calcLeaveDays()` (12915): `Math.round((end-start)/86400000)+1` days; `<1` → "⚠ End date must be on or after start date".
- ⚠️ **Gap (medium):** `calcLeaveDays` does **not** special-case Half Day — selecting "Half Day" still shows "1 day" in the info line (and never 0.5). The 0.5 value is only enforced server-side. The day-count UI is misleading for Half Day. The leave-type `onchange` does not recompute days either (no handler on `lv-type`).

**Field: Reason (optional)**
- element: `<textarea class="fc" rows="3">` · id `lv-reason` · placeholder `Briefly describe the reason for your leave...` · required no.

**Buttons:**
- **Cancel** → `closeModal('leave-modal')`.
- **Submit Request** (id `btn-submit-leave`) → `saveLeaveRequest()`.

### 3.9.4 `saveLeaveRequest()` (12923)

- Validates type/start/end present and `start <= end` (toasts on failure).
- ⚠️ No client-side Half-Day single-day check (relies on backend). (low)
- Loading btn; **`google.script.run.submitLeaveRequest({leave_type,start_date,end_date,reason}, APP.currentUser.email)`**.
- success: if `!r.ok` → error toast; else `closeModal('leave-modal')`, `toast('Leave request submitted! Your manager will review it.','success')`, reload if on my-leaves.
- failure: error toast.

### 3.9.5 Backend — `submitLeaveRequest(record, email)` (`leaves.gs:7`)

```js
function submitLeaveRequest(record, email) {
  try {
    var user = getCurrentUser(email);
    if (!record.leave_type) return { ok: false, error: 'Leave type is required.' };
    if (!record.start_date) return { ok: false, error: 'Start date is required.' };
```
- Validates type/start/end + `start <= end`.
- **Half Day rule** (15–17): `leave_type==='Half Day'` requires `start_date === end_date` (else `'Half Day leave must be a single day.'`); `Days` fixed at `0.5`; otherwise `Days = round((end-start)/86400000)+1`. ✅ matches Recent Change #4.
- Inserts `Leaves` row (`Status:'Pending'`), audits CREATE, returns `{ok:true,leaveId}`.

**`getMyLeaves(email)`** (92): own leaves sorted by `Created_At` desc.

**Related (not in My Leaves view but adjacent):** `getPendingLeaves`/`reviewLeaveRequest` power the Leave Approvals view (manager+); `reviewLeave` (12879) uses `prompt()` for rejection reason and `confirm()` for approval, calls `reviewLeaveRequest(leaveId, status, notes, email)`, then refreshes the leave badge via `getInitialPayload`.

**Gaps (3.9):**
- ⚠️ Half Day not reflected in the `lv-days-info` UI (shows "1 day"); 0.5 only applied server-side. (medium)
- ⚠️ No `lv-type` onchange to recompute/lock dates for Half Day; user can pick a date range with "Half Day" and only learn it's invalid after submit. (low)
- NOTE: `renderMyLeaves` "Notes" column reads `l.Review_Notes`, but the sheet schema column is `Review_Notes` (✅ correct); the `Reviewed_By` column shows the reviewer's **email** (backend stores `email`, not display name). (low — cosmetic)


## PART 4: TEAM

The **Team** section of the sidebar (`<div class="nav-sec nav-mgr-only hidden">Team</div>`, `index.html:1833`) groups the manager-and-above views: **Team Tasks**, **Team Projects**, **Team Work Logs**, **Leave Approvals**, **Team Management**. Every Team nav item carries the `nav-mgr-only hidden` classes and is revealed only when the current user's role is in `managerRoles = ['Super Admin','Admin','Team Captain','Team Facilitator']` (`app.js.html:1045–1049`, `onPayloadLoaded`). Team Members (TM) never see these nav items at all.

This part documents **only the differences** between Team Tasks / Team Projects and their personal counterparts (My Tasks `### 3.3`, My Projects `### 3.4`). The task-sheet rendering pipeline, the column-filter bar, the multi-select column filters, the group-by toggle (Function / Date / Week), the inline status-edit dropdown, batch "Add Tasks", and the task-detail / project-detail modals are **byte-for-byte identical** to the personal views and are documented in full in Part 3.3 / 3.4. They share the exact same helper functions; only the `vid` namespace (`'team'` / `'team-tsk'` / `'team-prj'`) and the data-scoping front-gate differ.

**Shared functions reused verbatim (cited, not re-documented here):**

| Concern | Shared function | Part |
|---|---|---|
| Task sheet body render (Function-grouped) | `_renderGroupedTasksHtml(tasks)` | 3.3 |
| Date / Week grouped render | `_renderDateGroupedTasksHtml(tasks,vid)` / `_renderWeekGroupedTasksHtml(tasks,vid)` | 3.3 |
| Group-by toggle HTML + state | `_tskGrpToggleHtml(vid)`, `_TSK_GRP_MODE[vid]` | 3.3 |
| Column-filter bar inject | `_tskInjectFilterBarForVid(viewId,vid)` | 3.3 |
| Populate filter `<select>`s (cascade) | `_populateTskFilterSelects(vid)` (`app.js.html:8677`) | 3.3 |
| Apply column / search / date / recurring filters | `_tskApplyColFilters(tasks,vid)` (`app.js.html:8617`) | 3.3 |
| Sort header click | `_tskSortClick(vid,col)` | 3.3 |
| Clear filters | `_clearTskFilters(vid)` | 3.3 |
| Batch add open / close / save | `_tskBatchOpen(vid)`, `_tskBatchClose(vid)`, `_atmAddRow()`, `_atmSaveAll()` | 3.3 |
| Inline status edit | (status `<select>` rendered inside `_renderGroupedTasksHtml`) | 3.3 |
| Task detail modal | `openTaskDetail(taskId)` | 3.3 |
| Project grid render | `_renderProjGrouped(projs)` (`app.js.html:11109`), `_sortProjHierarchy` (`app.js.html:10939`) | 3.4 |
| Project detail modal | `openProjectDetail(projId)` | 3.4 |
| New project modal | `openNewProjectModal()` | 3.4 |
| Assignment filter state + tab switch | `_ASGN_FILTER`, `setAsgnFilter(vid,val)` (`app.js.html:3222,3230`) | 3.3 |

### 4.1 Team Tasks

**View:** `<div class="view hidden" id="view-team-tasks">` (`index.html:2145`)
**Nav trigger:** `<div class="nav-item nav-mgr-only hidden" data-view="team-tasks" onclick="navigate('team-tasks')">` (`index.html:1834`)
**Render dispatch:** `navigate('team-tasks')` → `renderTeamTasks()` (`app.js.html:1148`, function at `3352`)
**Header:** title `Team Tasks`, sub `Tasks across your team` (`index.html:2147`)

#### 4.1.1 What is identical to My Tasks

The entire `<table class="task-sheet">` block (`index.html:2157–2202`) is structurally a clone of My Tasks (`index.html:2047–2092`) with the `id` prefix changed from `my-` to `team-`:

- Same 11 columns / sort headers: `_tskSortClick('team',…)` with `ts-si-team-*` indicators.
- Same group-by toggle host `<div id="team-tasks-grp-toggle">`.
- Same table-wrap / alt-body pattern: `team-tasks-tbl-wrap`, `team-tasks-body`, `team-tasks-alt-body`.
- Same batch-add trigger row `ts-trig-team` → `_tskBatchOpen('team')`, save button `atm-save-team` → `_atmSaveAll()`.
- Same status column-filter option list verbatim (`index.html:2181`):
  `All Statuses / Yet to Start / Planning / WIP (0%-25%) / WIP (25%-50%) / WIP (50%-75%) / WIP (75%-100%) / Review / On Hold / Cancelled / Done`
- Same priority filter (`index.html:2182`): `All / Critical / High / Medium / Low`.

`renderTeamTasks()` (`app.js.html:3352–3416`) mirrors `renderMyTasks()` (`app.js.html:1659–1715`) line-for-line for the filter-bar injection (`_tskInjectFilterBarForVid('team-tasks','team')`), `_populateTskFilterSelects('team')`, `_tskApplyColFilters(tasks,'team')`, group-toggle HTML, and the `function | date | week` mode dispatch (`_TSK_GRP_MODE['team']`).

#### 4.1.2 DIFFERENCE 1 — Assignment-filter tabs (To Team / By Team vs To Me / By Me)

The assignment-filter tab strip (`index.html:2149–2153`) uses a **different value set** than My Tasks:

| View | tab values (`data-val`) | labels |
|---|---|---|
| My Tasks (`my-tsk`) | `all` / `to-me` / `by-me` | All / To Me / By Me |
| **Team Tasks (`team-tsk`)** | `all` / `team` / `by-team` | **All / To Team / By Team** |

Verbatim Team Tasks tabs (`index.html:2150–2152`):
```html
<button class="tl-tab asgn-tab active" data-vid="team-tsk" data-val="all"     onclick="setAsgnFilter('team-tsk','all')">All</button>
<button class="tl-tab asgn-tab"        data-vid="team-tsk" data-val="team"    onclick="setAsgnFilter('team-tsk','team')" title="Assigned to team members">To Team</button>
<button class="tl-tab asgn-tab"        data-vid="team-tsk" data-val="by-team" onclick="setAsgnFilter('team-tsk','by-team')" title="Assigned by team members">By Team</button>
```

Field-level:

- **Tab: All**
  - element: `button.tl-tab.asgn-tab.active`
  - id/class: `data-vid="team-tsk" data-val="all"`
  - handler: `onclick="setAsgnFilter('team-tsk','all')"` (`app.js.html:3230`)
  - wired: yes — `_applyAsgnTaskFilter` returns tasks unchanged for `'all'` (`app.js.html:3277`)
- **Tab: To Team**
  - element: `button.tl-tab.asgn-tab`
  - id/class: `data-vid="team-tsk" data-val="team"`, `title="Assigned to team members"`
  - handler: `onclick="setAsgnFilter('team-tsk','team')"`
  - wired: yes — `_applyAsgnTaskFilter(tasks,'team-tsk')` branch `af==='team'` keeps tasks where `_belongsToMyTeam(t,myIds,myTeam)` (`app.js.html:3280–3286`). `myIds = _myTeamEmpIds()`; if `null` (team-name mismatch) it returns **all** tasks unfiltered (`app.js.html:3283`). ⚠️
- **Tab: By Team**
  - element: `button.tl-tab.asgn-tab`
  - id/class: `data-vid="team-tsk" data-val="by-team"`, `title="Assigned by team members"`
  - handler: `onclick="setAsgnFilter('team-tsk','by-team')"`
  - wired: yes — branch `af==='by-team'` keeps tasks whose `t.Assigner_ID` is in `myIds2 = _myTeamEmpIds()` (`app.js.html:3287–3292`); again falls back to all tasks if `myIds2` is `null`. ⚠️

`setAsgnFilter(vid,val)` (`app.js.html:3230–3240`) toggles `.active` on the matching `.asgn-tab[data-vid="team-tsk"]`, stores `_ASGN_FILTER['team-tsk']=val`, and dispatches `renderTeamTasks()` via the lookup map (`'team-tsk': renderTeamTasks`, `app.js.html:3237`).

**⚠️ GAP — no `to-me`/`by-me` analogue:** `_applyAsgnTaskFilter`'s `else` branch (`to-me`/`by-me`, `app.js.html:3293–3300`) is unreachable from the Team Tasks UI because the tab strip never emits those values. This is by design (Team view is team-scoped) but means a manager cannot quickly narrow Team Tasks to "tasks I personally own/created" from the tab bar — they must use the Assignee/Assigner column filters instead. Documented as a UX note, not a bug.

#### 4.1.3 DIFFERENCE 2 — Admin client-side team filter (the only data-scope difference)

For TC/TF the backend (`getAuthorizedTasks`, `auth.gs:663–693`) already returns **only team-scoped tasks** (uses `_getTeamEmpIds(user)` + `Assigned_Teams` + unassigned-in-team-project), so `renderTeamTasks` does **no** extra scoping for them — it shows the same `APP.tasks` payload as the dashboard.

For **Super Admin / Admin** the backend returns **ALL tasks** (`auth.gs:661 — if (_isAdmin(user.role)) return all;`). To keep "Team Tasks" meaning *the admin's own team* rather than the whole org, `renderTeamTasks` applies a client-side filter (`app.js.html:3360–3379`):

```js
if (_isAdmin(APP.currentUser.role)) {
  var myTeam = (APP.currentUser.team || '').toLowerCase();
  var myIds  = _myTeamEmpIds() || new Set();
  myIds.add(APP.currentUser.empId);                 // always include the admin themselves
  var myProjIds = new Set();
  APP.projects.forEach(function(p) {
    if (_parseIds(p.Owner_IDs||'').some(id => myIds.has(id)) || _belongsToMyTeam(p,myIds,myTeam))
      myProjIds.add(p.Proj_ID);
  });
  var includedIds = new Set();
  tasks.forEach(function(t) {
    if (_belongsToMyTeam(t,myIds,myTeam)) { includedIds.add(t.Task_ID); return; }
    var assignees = _parseIds(t.Assignee_IDs||t.Assignee_ID||'');
    if (assignees.length===0 && myProjIds.has(t.Proj_ID)) includedIds.add(t.Task_ID);  // unassigned-in-team-project
  });
  tasks = tasks.filter(function(t){ return includedIds.has(t.Task_ID); });
}
```

- This block runs **before** `_applyAsgnTaskFilter` and the column filters.
- `_myTeamEmpIds()` (`app.js.html:3332–3340`) returns a `Set` of `Emp_ID` whose `Team` (case-insensitive) matches the admin's `Team`, or `null` if the admin's team has no matching employees; the `|| new Set()` fallback plus `myIds.add(empId)` guarantees the admin at least sees their own tasks rather than the whole org. ⚠️ Consequence: an admin whose `Team` field is blank/renamed sees **only their own** tasks under Team Tasks (mirrors the documented fallback intent).
- `_belongsToMyTeam(rec,myIds,myTeam)` (`app.js.html:3343–3350`) matches on `Assigner_ID ∈ myIds`, any `Assignee_IDs ∈ myIds`, or `myTeam` present in `Assigned_Teams`.
- Mirrors the backend TC/TF rule (`auth.gs:678–685`), including the "unassigned task in a team-associated project" clause.

**⚠️ GAP — client-side admin scoping is bypassable / divergent:** Because the admin client filter is computed from `APP.projects` and `APP.employees` already loaded in the browser, it (a) can silently diverge from the backend TC/TF logic if those caches are stale, and (b) is purely cosmetic — the full org task list is already in `APP.tasks` in the browser. Not a security hole (admins are authorized to see all tasks anyway) but a correctness/consistency note: the two scoping implementations (`getAuthorizedTasks` for TC/TF vs this JS block for admins) must be kept in sync manually.

#### 4.1.4 Column filter bar — one extra select vs My Tasks

The Team Tasks inline column-filter row (`index.html:2173–2185`) includes an **Assignee filter `tf-team-asgn`** that My Tasks omits:

| filter id | My Tasks | Team Tasks |
|---|---|---|
| Function | `tf-my-fn` | `tf-team-fn` |
| Sub-Function | `tf-my-parent` | `tf-team-parent` |
| Task | `tf-my-sub` | `tf-team-sub` |
| **Assignee** | *(empty `<th></th>`, `index.html:2068)*  ❌ absent | **`tf-team-asgn`** ✅ present (`index.html:2178`) |
| Assigner | `tf-my-asgr` | `tf-team-asgr` |
| Project | `tf-my-proj` | `tf-team-proj` |
| Status | `tf-my-status` | `tf-team-status` |
| Priority | `tf-my-pri` | `tf-team-pri` |
| Due | `tf-my-due` | `tf-team-due` |

This is intentional: in My Tasks every task is assigned to the current user, so an Assignee filter is meaningless; in Team Tasks a manager filters across many assignees. Both are populated by the same `_populateTskFilterSelects(vid)` (`app.js.html:8686–8690`, builds `tf-{vid}-asgn` from a sorted copy of `APP.employees`). All `onchange` handlers call `renderTeamTasks()`.

Verbatim Team Tasks Assignee select (`index.html:2178`):
```html
<th><select class="ts-cf-inp" id="tf-team-asgn" onchange="renderTeamTasks()"><option value="">All Assignees</option></select></th>
```
options: dynamically populated by `_populateTskFilterSelects('team')` from `APP.employees` (sorted A→Z, `app.js.html:8683`); option label = `e.Name`, value = `e.Emp_ID`.

#### 4.1.5 google.script.run calls

`renderTeamTasks` itself makes **no** `google.script.run` call — it renders from the in-memory `APP.tasks` / `APP.projects` / `APP.employees` / `APP.functions` already loaded by `getInitialPayload` (Part 2). Server round-trips happen only through the shared machinery (inline status edit → `updateTaskStatus`, batch add → `createTask`, task detail edits) documented in Part 3.3.

---

### 4.2 Team Projects

**View:** `<div class="view hidden" id="view-team-projects">` (`index.html:2208`)
**Nav trigger:** `<div class="nav-item nav-mgr-only hidden" data-view="team-projects" onclick="navigate('team-projects')">` (`index.html:1837`)
**Render dispatch:** `navigate('team-projects')` → `renderTeamProjects()` (`app.js.html:1149`, function at `3419`)
**Header:** title `Team Projects`, sub `All active projects across the team` (`index.html:2210`)

#### 4.2.1 What is identical to My Projects

The card grid `<div class="cards-grid" id="team-projects-grid">` (`index.html:2225`) is rendered by the same `_renderProjGrouped(_sortProjHierarchy(rows))` (`app.js.html:3444`) used by My Projects (`app.js.html:1752`). Project cards, the parent/sub-project hierarchy grouping, status badges, and `openProjectDetail` are documented in Part 3.4.

The assignment-filter tab strip is the same `.tl-tabs / .asgn-tab` widget and uses the same value set as Team Tasks (`all` / `team` / `by-team`).

#### 4.2.2 DIFFERENCE 1 — Search box + status select instead of group-toggle

Unlike My Projects (which has no search/status controls), Team Projects exposes a **text search** and a **status `<select>`** directly in the page header (`index.html:2217–2221`):

**Field: Search projects**
- element: `<input class="fc tp-filter-inp">`
- id/class: `id="tp-search"`, classes `fc tp-filter-inp`
- placeholder: `Search projects…`
- required: no
- default: (none)
- handler: `oninput="renderTeamProjects()"` (`index.html:2217`)
- validation: none; query is `.toLowerCase()`'d (`app.js.html:3421`)
- wired: yes — `renderTeamProjects` reads `q = elVal('tp-search').toLowerCase()` and matches against `p.Name` OR `p.Description` (`app.js.html:3438`)
- data source: filters in-memory `APP.projects`

**Field: Status filter**
- element: `<select class="fc tp-filter-inp">`
- id/class: `id="tp-status"`
- required: no
- default: `All Statuses` (`value=""`)
- handler: `onchange="renderTeamProjects()"`
- options (verbatim, `index.html:2219–2220`):
  ```html
  <option value="">All Statuses</option>
  <option>Yet to Start</option><option>Planning</option><option>WIP (0%-25%)</option><option>WIP (25%-50%)</option><option>WIP (50%-75%)</option><option>WIP (75%-100%)</option><option>Review</option><option>On Hold</option><option>Cancelled</option><option>Done</option>
  ```
  static, hardcoded; matched exactly via `p.Status !== sf` (`app.js.html:3437`).
- wired: yes
- data source: hardcoded

#### 4.2.3 DIFFERENCE 2 — "+ New Project" button always visible (no role gate on the button)

Team Projects header has a **"+ New Project"** button (`index.html:2222`):
```html
<button class="btn btn-primary" onclick="openNewProjectModal()">+ New Project</button>
```
- element: button.btn.btn-primary
- id/class: (no id; class `btn btn-primary`)
- handler: `onclick="openNewProjectModal()"` (shared modal — Part 3.4)
- wired: yes
- gaps: **DIFFERENCE** — this button has **no `nav-mgr-only` class**, whereas the My Projects "+ New Project" button is `class="btn btn-primary nav-mgr-only hidden"` (`index.html:2107`). On Team Projects the button is unconditionally shown. This is safe in practice because the whole Team Projects **view** is manager-gated (`nav-mgr-only` on the nav item, `app.js.html:1834`), so only managers can reach it. ⚠️ Note: `renderMyProjects` additionally toggles its button via JS (`btnProj.classList.toggle('hidden', managerRoles.indexOf(u.role)===-1)`, `app.js.html:1748–1750`); `renderTeamProjects` has **no** equivalent button-toggling logic — it relies entirely on view-level gating.

#### 4.2.4 DIFFERENCE 3 — Admin client-side team filter (data scope)

Same pattern as Team Tasks. For TC/TF, `getAuthorizedProjects` (`auth.gs:706+`) already returns team-scoped projects, so no extra client scoping. For Admin/SA, backend returns ALL projects (`auth.gs:708 — if (_isAdmin(user.role)) return all;`), so `renderTeamProjects` applies the client-side admin filter (`app.js.html:3426–3434`):

```js
if (_isAdmin(APP.currentUser.role)) {
  var myTeam = (APP.currentUser.team || '').toLowerCase();
  var myIds  = _myTeamEmpIds() || new Set();
  myIds.add(APP.currentUser.empId);
  projs = projs.filter(function(p) {
    if (_parseIds(p.Owner_IDs||'').some(id => myIds.has(id))) return true;
    return _belongsToMyTeam(p,myIds,myTeam);
  });
}
```
Same `_myTeamEmpIds()`-null fallback caveat as 4.1.3 applies. ⚠️

#### 4.2.5 Render order summary

`renderTeamProjects()` (`app.js.html:3419–3445`):
1. read `sf = elVal('tp-status')`, `q = elVal('tp-search').toLowerCase()` (`app.js.html:3420–3421`)
2. start from `APP.projects`
3. admin → client-side team filter (4.2.4)
4. apply status + search filter (`app.js.html:3436–3440`)
5. apply assignment-tab filter `_applyAsgnProjFilter(rows,'team-prj')` (`app.js.html:3441`)
6. `grid.innerHTML = _renderProjGrouped(_sortProjHierarchy(rows))` (`app.js.html:3444`)

Returns early with no render if `el('team-projects-grid')` is missing (`app.js.html:3443`).

#### 4.2.6 `_applyAsgnProjFilter('team-prj')` semantics

(`app.js.html:3305–3330`) — Team Projects uses the `all` / `team` / `by-team` tabs:
- `all` → unchanged
- `team` → keep projects where any `Owner_IDs ∈ myIds` OR `_belongsToMyTeam(p,…)` (`app.js.html:3309–3316`); returns all if `myIds` is `null` ⚠️
- `by-team` → keep projects whose `p.Assigner_ID ∈ myIds2` (`app.js.html:3318–3321`); returns all if `null` ⚠️
- `to-me` / `by-me` branch (`app.js.html:3323–3329`) is present but unreachable from Team Projects (no such tabs).

#### 4.2.7 google.script.run calls

`renderTeamProjects` makes **no** server call — it renders from in-memory `APP.projects`. Project creation/edit round-trips go through the shared `openNewProjectModal` / `openProjectDetail` machinery (Part 3.4).

---

### Cross-cutting gaps for Part 4

1. ⚠️ **`_myTeamEmpIds()` null → show-all fallback** in both `_applyAsgnTaskFilter` (`app.js.html:3283,3289`) and `_applyAsgnProjFilter` (`app.js.html:3312,3320`): when a team name is inconsistent/blank, the "To Team"/"By Team" tabs silently fall back to showing the **unfiltered** list instead of an empty/own-only list — the opposite of the admin `renderTeam*` fallback (which narrows to own). The two fallbacks are inconsistent with each other.
2. ⚠️ **Duplicated scoping logic**: TC/TF scoping lives in the backend (`getAuthorizedTasks`/`getAuthorizedProjects`) while Admin scoping lives in the frontend (`renderTeamTasks`/`renderTeamProjects`). Drift risk; the "unassigned-task-in-team-project" rule is implemented twice.
3. ⚠️ Team Projects **"+ New Project" button lacks `nav-mgr-only`** and has no JS visibility toggle (unlike My Projects). Currently harmless due to view-level gating only.
4. No Tabler `ti ti-*` stray icons found in the Team Tasks / Team Projects view markup or render functions (icons here are Material Symbols `filter_alt_off`, `add`) — clean.
5. No `google.script.run` calls are made by `renderTeamTasks` / `renderTeamProjects` themselves; all server I/O is delegated to shared Part 3.3 / 3.4 machinery.


### 4.3 Team Work Logs

Manager-and-above drill-down view that aggregates every team member's daily Work Log into per-member cards (or per-date entries) and lets managers open a full editable per-member week/month/custom grid (the Member Work Log modal).

**Source read for this slice**
- `src/app.js.html` lines **3447–3701** (TL state vars, `_tlRange`, `tlNav`, `tlNavToday`, `setTLMode`, `tlApplyCustomRange`, `loadTeamLogs`, `_populateMemberFilter`, `switchTeamLogTab`, `renderTeamLogs`, work-day helpers, `_tlByMember`, `_tlMemberDayCards`)
- `src/app.js.html` lines **3703–4028** (`_attDot`, intern helpers, `_tlMemberWeekCards`, `_TLM_ATT_*` constants, `_tlMemberMonthCards`)
- `src/app.js.html` lines **4030–5036** (`_tlByDate`, ML state vars, `_mlNeedsReload`, `openMemberLogDetail`, `loadMemberLogDetail`, `mlNav`/`mlNavToday`, `_mlDays`, `_mlRangeLabel`, `renderMemberLogDetail`, `mlOnAttChange`, `saveMemberEntry`, `_renderMemberLogModal`, `_mlToggleOffDayWork`, `_mlOnAttChange`, `_mlAddItem`, `_mlSaveEntry`, `_mlChipHtml`, `_mlWeekMonday`, `_mlPrevWeek`/`_mlNextWeek`/`_mlGoToday`)
- `src/app.js.html` lines **1773–1888** (`_attEffHours`, `WL_ATTENDANCE_OPTIONS`/`WL_LEAVE_OPTIONS`/`WL_STATUS_OPTIONS`/`WL_LEAVE_TYPES`/`WL_OFF_TYPES`, `_wlShowsOtBadge`), line **208** (`_canEditWlStatus`)
- `src/app.js.html` lines **2410–2535** (`_wlDurHtml`, `_tlApplyDurations`, `_tlLoadDurationsAsync`, `_mlDurHtml`, `_mlApplyDurations`, `_mlLoadDurationsAsync`), lines **2542–2679** (`_wlAsStatus`, `_mlAutoSave`, `_mlOnBlur`)
- `src/index.html` lines **2229–2267** (`#view-team-logs` markup), lines **3563–3583** (`#member-log-modal` markup), line **1840** (nav item)
- `src/auth.gs` lines **1372–1519** (`getMemberWorkLogs`, `adminSubmitWorkLog`, `adminUpdateWorkLog`, `getTeamWorkLogs`)
- `src/work-duration.gs` lines **693–759** (`getWorkDurationsForDates`, `getTeamWorkDurationsRange`)

**Access / nav**
- Sidebar entry: `<div class="nav-item nav-mgr-only hidden" data-view="team-logs" onclick="navigate('team-logs')">` — icon `monitoring` (Material Symbols), label "Team Work Logs". Class `nav-mgr-only` is what reveals it for managers; backend `getTeamWorkLogs`/`getMemberWorkLogs`/`getTeamWorkDurationsRange` all hard-gate on `_isManager(user.role)` and throw/return error otherwise.
- `navigate('team-logs')` calls `loadTeamLogs()` (`app.js.html:1150`). Quick-search path also calls it (`app.js.html:8271`).

---

### 4.3.1 Navigation Controls (`#view-team-logs` header)

The header `.ph-actions` contains four control clusters: range navigation, period mode, custom date bar, and view-type tabs, plus a member filter and refresh button.

#### Period-mode tabs (`.tl-tabs`)
Four buttons; each calls `setTLMode(mode)`. Default active = **Day** (`TL_MODE = 'day'`, `app.js.html:3453`).

| Button | id | onclick | label |
|---|---|---|---|
| Day | `tl-mode-day` | `setTLMode('day')` | Day |
| Week | `tl-mode-week` | `setTLMode('week')` | Week |
| Month | `tl-mode-month` | `setTLMode('month')` | Month |
| Custom | `tl-mode-custom` | `setTLMode('custom')` | Custom |

`setTLMode(mode)` (`app.js.html:3509`): sets `TL_MODE`; toggles `.active` across the four buttons; toggles `#tl-custom-bar` `.hidden` (shown only for `custom`); hides the `.tl-range-bar` via `visibility:hidden` in custom mode; pre-fills `tl-cust-from`/`tl-cust-to` with the current range on first switch to custom; updates the range label; calls `loadTeamLogs()` for every mode **except** custom (custom waits for Apply).

#### Range navigation (`.tl-range-bar`)
| Control | element | onclick | effect |
|---|---|---|---|
| ‹ Previous | `<button class="tl-nav-btn">` | `tlNav(-1)` | step back one day/week/month |
| Today | `<button class="btn btn-ghost btn-sm">` | `tlNavToday()` | reset `TL_ANCHOR = new Date()` |
| range label | `<span class="tl-range-label" id="tl-range-label">` (default text `—`) | — | shows `_tlRange().label` |
| › Next | `<button class="tl-nav-btn">` | `tlNav(1)` | step forward |

- `tlNav(dir)` (`app.js.html:3495`): early-returns in custom mode (`if (TL_MODE === 'custom') return;` — arrows are dead in custom mode AND visually hidden via `visibility:hidden`); else mutates `TL_ANCHOR` by ±1 day / ±7 days / ±1 month; updates label; `loadTeamLogs()`.
- `tlNavToday()` (`app.js.html:3504`): also early-returns in custom mode.
- `_tlRange()` (`app.js.html:3460`) computes `{start,end,days[],label}` per mode. Labels use `toLocaleDateString('en-IN', …)`. Month label uses a hardcoded `MONTHS` array.

#### Custom date bar (`#tl-custom-bar`, hidden by default)
Field: **From**
- element: `<input type="date">`
- id/class: `tl-cust-from` / `fc tl-date-inp`
- placeholder: (none — native date input)
- required: yes (validated in `tlApplyCustomRange`)
- default: (none) — pre-filled by `setTLMode('custom')` from `_tlRange().start` on first switch
- handlers: (none directly — read on Apply)
- validation: see Apply button
- wired: yes
- data source: user input
- gaps: none

Field: **To**
- element: `<input type="date">`
- id/class: `tl-cust-to` / `fc tl-date-inp`
- placeholder: (none)
- required: yes
- default: (none) — pre-filled from `_tlRange().end`
- handlers: (none directly)
- wired: yes
- data source: user input

Button: **Apply** — `<button class="btn btn-primary btn-sm" onclick="tlApplyCustomRange()">`.
`tlApplyCustomRange()` (`app.js.html:3531`) validation:
```js
if (!from || !to)   { toast('Please select both a start and end date.', 'error'); return; }
if (from > to)      { toast('Start date must be before end date.', 'error'); return; }
var diffDays = Math.round((new Date(to) - new Date(from)) / 86400000);
if (diffDays > 90)  { toast('Custom range cannot exceed 90 days.', 'error'); return; }
```
On pass: sets `TL_CUSTOM_START`/`TL_CUSTOM_END`, updates label, `loadTeamLogs()`. **This is the 90-day cap toast.**

#### View-type tabs (`.tl-tabs`)
| Button | id | onclick | label (icon) |
|---|---|---|---|
| By Member | `tl-tab-member` (active default) | `switchTeamLogTab('member')` | person · By Member |
| By Date | `tl-tab-date` | `switchTeamLogTab('date')` | calendar_month · By Date |

`switchTeamLogTab(tab)` (`app.js.html:3560`): sets `TL_TAB`; toggles `.active` on the two tab buttons; toggles `#tl-member` `.hidden` so the member filter appears **only in By Date** (`toggle('hidden', tab === 'member')` → hidden when member tab); calls `renderTeamLogs()` (no re-fetch — uses cached `TL_RAW`).
warning sign Minor inconsistency: the member dropdown is hidden in By Member view, so the only way to focus a single member there is to click their card. This is intentional but means there is no member filter in the card view.

#### Member filter (`#tl-member`)
Field: **All Members**
- element: `<select>`
- id/class: `tl-member` / `fc tl-filter-inp hidden`
- default: `<option value="">All Members</option>` (only static option)
- options: dynamically populated by `_populateMemberFilter(TL_RAW)` (`app.js.html:3554`) — one `<option value="{Emp_ID}">{Emp_Name||Emp_ID}</option>` per distinct `Emp_ID` present in the loaded logs. Deduplicated via a `seen` map. **Verbatim static head:** `'<option value="">All Members</option>'`.
- handlers: `onchange="renderTeamLogs()"`
- wired: yes — `_tlByDate` reads `elVal('tl-member')` and filters logs to that `Emp_ID`.
- data source: `TL_RAW` (from `getTeamWorkLogs`)
- gaps: only members who already have a log in the loaded range appear (members with zero logs are not selectable).

#### Refresh — `<button class="btn btn-ghost btn-sm" onclick="loadTeamLogs()" title="Refresh">↻</button>`.

#### Data load — `loadTeamLogs()` (`app.js.html:3543`)
- google.script.run call: **`getTeamWorkLogs(APP.currentUser.email, tlRange.start, tlRange.end)`**
- called-from: `loadTeamLogs`
- params: current user email + server-side date range (`_tlRange().start`/`.end`)
- success handler: `TL_RAW = r.logs || []`, `TL_HOLIDAYS = r.holidays || []`, `_populateMemberFilter(TL_RAW)`, `renderTeamLogs()`, then fire-and-forget `_tlLoadDurationsAsync(tlRange.start, tlRange.end)` (Phase-2 durations).
- failure handler: replaces container with an error empty-state showing `err.message`.
- backend signature (`auth.gs:1464`):
```js
function getTeamWorkLogs(email, startDate, endDate) {
  var user = getCurrentUser(email);
  if (!_isManager(user.role)) throw new Error('Not authorized.');
  var all    = dbGetWorkLogs(null);
```
Returns `{ logs:[…+Emp_Name+Team], holidays:[…] }`. Admin → all logs; TC/TF → filtered to `_getTeamEmpIds(user)`. Merges intern logs from `Intern_Work_Log`. Holidays returned so the client can compute expected work days.
- Phase-2 durations: `_tlLoadDurationsAsync` → **`getTeamWorkDurationsRange(APP.currentUser.email, start, end)`** (`work-duration.gs:728`) returns `{ ok, data:{'empId:YYYY-MM-DD':'HH:MM:SS'} }`; merged into `TL_DURATIONS`; `_tlApplyDurations()` patches `#wl-dur-{empId}-{iso}` spans.

`renderTeamLogs()` → `_tlByMember()` (member tab) or `_tlByDate()` (date tab).

---

### 4.3.2 Member Cards (By Member view)

`_tlByMember()` (`app.js.html:3596`):
- Builds `members`: active employees; **Admin/SA** = all active employees (grouped by Team with `.tl-team-section` headers, teams sorted A→Z, members sorted by log-count desc then name); **TC/TF** = only employees whose `(e.Team || e.Sub_Department) === myTeam`, flat list.
- Builds `logMap[empId][iso] = log` from `TL_RAW` clipped to the range.
- `#tl-sub` text set to `"{range.label} — {active}/{total} members active"`.
- Renders cards per mode: **Day** → `_tlMemberDayCards`, **Week** → `_tlMemberWeekCards`, **Month/Custom** → `_tlMemberMonthCards` (both month and custom fall into the month branch — custom uses month-style cards). Month/custom calls are wrapped in `try/catch` that surfaces the JS error inline in red rather than leaving the spinner stuck.

All card variants are clickable: `onclick="openMemberLogDetail('{Emp_ID}')"`.

#### Day cards — `_tlMemberDayCards` (`app.js.html:3675`)
Per member: avatar (initial + `_avatarColor`), name, team, a "Done" badge (`background:#e8f5e9;color:#2e7d32`), optional `Tentative` badge when `log.Status === 'Tentative'`, effective hours via `_attEffHours(log.Attendance, log['Extra Hours']||log.Hours_Total)`, 1st-half text, 2nd-half text, and a blocker tag (`⚠ {Remark||Blockers}`). No-log members render a `.tlm-card.missing` card with `✗ Missing` badge and "Click to add work log."

#### Week cards — `_tlMemberWeekCards` (`app.js.html:3829`)
Renders 7 day-dots (`WDAY` letters S/M/T/W/T/F/S). Per dot: interns use `_internAttToCircle(att)`; regular employees use `_attDot(att)`; empty Sundays → `W`, alt-Saturdays → `AW`, holidays → `H`, else missing `–`. Footer: `"{totalHrs} hrs · {coloredDays} day(s) logged"`.
`_attDot` map (`app.js.html:3704`) — verbatim:
```
'Present':{cls:'att-present',lbl:'P'}, 'Extra Full Day':{cls:'att-extra-full',lbl:'EF'},
'Extra Half Day':{cls:'att-extra-half',lbl:'EH'}, 'Leave Full Day':{cls:'att-leave-full',lbl:'LF'},
'Leave Half Day':{cls:'att-leave-half',lbl:'LH'}, 'Alternate Week Off':{cls:'att-alt-week',lbl:'AW'},
'Week Off':{cls:'att-week-off',lbl:'W'}, 'Holiday':{cls:'att-holiday',lbl:'H'}
```
warning sign `totalHrs` in week cards is **not rounded** — `_attEffHours` sums raw floats so a card can show e.g. `40.5` or floating-point noise like `40.50000001`. (Month cards round; week cards do not.) Severity: low.

#### Month / Custom cards — `_tlMemberMonthCards` (`app.js.html:3918`)
Per member computes `totalHrs`, `otHrs`, `attCounts{}`, submitted-count vs `workDays.length`, percentage and color (`>=80% var(--ok)`, `>=50% var(--warn)`, else `var(--danger)`), a month progress bar, and an attendance breakdown row.

**OT formula (verbatim, `app.js.html:3942–3946`):**
```js
var extra  = parseFloat(log['Extra Hours'] || log.Hours_Total || 0) || 0;
totalHrs += _attEffHours(attVal, extra);
otHrs += extra;
if (attVal === 'Extra Full Day') otHrs += 9;
if (attVal === 'Extra Half Day') otHrs += 4;
```
i.e. **OT = Σ(Extra Hours) + (Extra Full Day count × 9) + (Extra Half Day count × 4)**, then `otHrs = Math.round(otHrs*10)/10`. OT badge rendered only when `otHrs > 0` as an orange pill `+{otHrs} OT` (`color:#e65100;background:#fff3e0`).

**Attendance-breakdown badges** driven by three module-level constants (`app.js.html:3890–3916`) — verbatim:

`_TLM_ATT_LABELS`:
```
'Present':'P', 'Extra Full Day':'EF', 'Extra Half Day':'EH',
'Leave Full Day':'LF', 'Leave Half Day':'LH', 'Leave':'LF',
'Alternate Week Off':'AW', 'Week Off':'W', 'Holiday':'H'
```
`_TLM_ATT_ORDER`:
```
['Present','Extra Full Day','Extra Half Day','Leave Full Day','Leave Half Day','Leave','Holiday','Week Off','Alternate Week Off']
```
`_TLM_ATT_COLORS`:
```
'Present':            'background:#e8f5e9;color:#2e7d32'
'Extra Full Day':     'background:#e0f2f1;color:#00695c'
'Extra Half Day':     'background:#e0f7fa;color:#00838f'
'Leave Full Day':     'background:#fff3e0;color:#e65100'
'Leave Half Day':     'background:#fff8e1;color:#f57f17'
'Leave':              'background:#fff3e0;color:#e65100'
'Holiday':            'background:#e3f2fd;color:#1565c0'
'Week Off':           'background:#f5f5f5;color:#757575'
'Alternate Week Off': 'background:#fafafa;color:#9e9e9e'
```
Badges render in `_TLM_ATT_ORDER` first (e.g. `P: 22`, `EF: 4`, `LH: 1`), then any unrecognised attendance keys appended in grey. Each badge is `{label}: {count}`.
warning sign `workDays` (the denominator for submitted%) is computed only for the **month** branch via `_tlExpectedWorkDays(range.days)` in `_tlByMember`. In **custom** mode the same `workDays` is passed (still `_tlExpectedWorkDays(range.days)` for the custom range), so the percentage is range-correct. No gap there. However, the percentage counts only logs that fall on expected work days (`empLogs[workDays[wi]]`), so logs on non-work days (e.g. weekend extra-day work) don't raise the % even though they raise hours — by design but potentially confusing. Severity: low.

### 4.3.3 Member Work Log Modal (`#member-log-modal`)

- id: `member-log-modal` (class `modal-bg hidden`, inner `.modal.modal-xl`)
- trigger: any member card click → `openMemberLogDetail(empId)`
- header: `#ml-header-avatar` (initial + avatar color) + `#ml-header-title` ("{name} — Work Log"), close `✕`
- sub-nav row (`.ml-week-nav`): `‹` `_mlPrevWeek()`, `#ml-week-label` (`—`), `›` `_mlNextWeek()`, **Today** `_mlGoToday()`, reload `↻` `loadMemberLogDetail(ML_MEMBER_ID)` (right-aligned).
- body: `#member-log-content` > `#member-log-body` (scrollable, `max-height:65vh`).
- close mechanism: backdrop `onclick="closeModal('member-log-modal',event)"` + `✕` `closeModal('member-log-modal')`. (No dedicated stop-timer on close — auto-save is debounce-only, see 4.3.3.4.)

#### 4.3.3.1 State carry-over from TL_* and the day-list (`_mlDays`)
`openMemberLogDetail(empId)` (`app.js.html:4105`) sets the modal context **from the team-view state**:
```js
ML_ANCHOR       = new Date(TL_ANCHOR || new Date());
ML_MODE         = TL_MODE;
ML_CUSTOM_START = TL_CUSTOM_START || '';
ML_CUSTOM_END   = TL_CUSTOM_END   || '';
```
then `el('member-log-modal').classList.remove('hidden')` and `loadMemberLogDetail()` (no arg = first open).

`_mlDays()` (`app.js.html:4211`) returns the displayed ISO day list per `ML_MODE`:
- **month** → every day of `ML_ANCHOR`'s month
- **custom** → every day `ML_CUSTOM_START..ML_CUSTOM_END`
- **week / day** → Mon–Sun of `ML_ANCHOR`'s week (day mode still shows the whole week)

`_mlRangeLabel(days)` (`app.js.html:4232`) — month → "Month YYYY"; custom/week → "d mon – d mon yyyy".

#### 4.3.3.2 Data load — `loadMemberLogDetail(memberId)` (`app.js.html:4120`)
Anchor derivation:
- **First open** (no `memberId`): month mode → if `TL_ANCHOR` is the current month, anchor = today's week-Monday, else = Monday of the 1st of that month; day/week/custom → `_mlWeekMonday(TL_ANCHOR)`.
- **Reload** (with `memberId`): `ML_ANCHOR = _mlWeekMonday(ML_ANCHOR || new Date())` (preserve position).

Fetch range (`mlRange`):
- month → full calendar month of `ML_ANCHOR`
- custom → exact `ML_CUSTOM_START..ML_CUSTOM_END`
- day/week → ±8-week window (`-8w start`, `+2w end`) so week nav within range avoids re-fetch.

google.script.run call: **`getMemberWorkLogs(targetId, APP._verifiedEmail, mlRange.start, mlRange.end)`**
- success handler: sets `ML_LOADED_START/END`, resets `ML_DATA/ML_DIRTY`, maps each log into `ML_DATA[iso]` (fields: `logId, attendance, leaveType, leaveReq, work1, work2, extraHrs, remark, status, adminComments`), then `_renderMemberLogModal()`.
- failure handler: error empty-state in `#member-log-body`.
- backend (`auth.gs:1372`):
```js
function getMemberWorkLogs(targetEmpId, adminEmail, startDate, endDate) {
  var user = getCurrentUser(adminEmail);
  if (!_isManager(user.role)) throw new Error('Not authorized.');
```
  Intern targets → `_getInternWlLogs`; else `dbGetWorkLogs(targetEmpId)` filtered by date.

#### 4.3.3.3 Rendering — `_renderMemberLogModal()` (`app.js.html:4487`)
warning sign **Two renderers exist for the same modal.** `renderMemberLogDetail()` (`app.js.html:4245`) is a legacy table renderer (8 columns incl. a per-row Save button `saveMemberEntry`, Status + Comments selects, leave-type/leave-request selects). The chip-based `_renderMemberLogModal()` (`app.js.html:4487`) is what `loadMemberLogDetail` actually invokes on data load. **But the week-nav functions `mlNav`/`mlNavToday` (`app.js.html:4197/4206`) call `renderMemberLogDetail()` (the legacy one), while `_mlPrevWeek`/`_mlNextWeek`/`_mlGoToday` (wired to the modal's actual `‹ › Today` buttons) call `_renderMemberLogModal()` (the chip one).** `mlNav`/`mlNavToday` are not referenced by any DOM button in the modal markup (the buttons use `_mlPrevWeek`/`_mlNextWeek`/`_mlGoToday`), so the legacy renderer is effectively dead via nav — but `saveMemberEntry`/`renderMemberLogDetail`/`mlOnAttChange`/`_mlUpdateSaveBtn`/`_mlRenderChips`(`ml-chips-` prefix vs `ml-cust-` chips)/`_mlRemoveChipItem`/`_mlEditChipItem` form an orphaned parallel implementation. Risk: divergent behavior / confusion. Severity: medium (UNUSED code).

`_renderMemberLogModal` sets `ML_RENDERING = true` during build (suppresses onchange-driven dirty), builds an 8-column table (Day, Attendance, Work Update 1st/2nd, Extra Hrs, Remark, Status, Comments) for each `_mlDays()` iso. Nav arrows hidden for month/custom: `document.querySelectorAll('#member-log-modal .tl-nav-btn').forEach(b => b.style.visibility = isFixedRange ? 'hidden' : '')`.

Per-row fields (all inline-edited, auto-saved):

Field: **Attendance**
- element: `<select class="wl-inp wl-att-sel ml-att">` (regular) OR `<input type="text" class="wl-inp wl-att-sel ml-att">` (intern, free-text)
- id/class: `data-iso="{iso}"`
- options (regular, from `WL_ATTENDANCE_OPTIONS`, verbatim): `Present`, `Leave Full Day`, `Leave Half Day`, `Alternate Week Off`, `Week Off`, `Holiday`, `Extra Full Day`, `Extra Half Day`
- placeholder (intern): `e.g. 9, 8.5, Holiday`
- default: saved value, else weekend/holiday pre-fill (`Week Off` on Sunday, `Holiday` on holiday dates)
- handlers: regular `onchange="_mlOnAttChange('{iso}')"`; intern `oninput="_mlInternWlOnAttChange(this)"` + `onblur` dirty+autosave
- validation: none client-side beyond off/leave field gating
- wired: yes
- data source: `ML_DATA[iso].attendance` / `WL_ATTENDANCE_OPTIONS`

Field: **Work Update — 1st Half / 2nd Half**
- element: `<textarea class="wl-cust-inp wl-inp ml-ta">` + add-as-chip button `↵` + chips div
- id/class: `id="ml-cust-{h1|h2}-{iso}"`, chips `id="ml-chips-{h1|h2}-{iso}"`, `data-half`
- placeholder: `Custom note — 1st half…` / `Custom note — 2nd half…`
- handlers: `oninput="_mlMarkDirtyFromInput(this);wlAutoResize(this)"`, `onblur="_mlOnBlur(this)"`, Enter(no-shift) → `_mlAddItem(iso,halfKey)`; `↵` button → `_mlAddItem`
- wired: yes — chips stored in `ML_ITEMS[iso:halfKey]`, serialized `'; '`-delimited (newlines → `\n`), parsed back on render
- data source: `ML_DATA[iso].work1/.work2`

Field: **Extra Hrs**
- element: `<input type="number" class="wl-inp wl-hrs-inp ml-hrs">`
- id/class: `id="ml-eh-{iso}"`, `data-iso`
- placeholder: `—`; min 0, max 12, step 0.5
- handlers: `oninput="ML_DIRTY['{iso}']=true;_mlUpdateOtBadge('{iso}')"`, `onblur="_mlOnBlur(this)"`
- wired: yes; data source `ML_DATA[iso].extraHrs`

Field: **Remark**
- element: `<input type="text" class="wl-inp ml-remark">`, `data-iso`
- placeholder: `Remark…`
- handlers: `oninput dirty`, `onblur="_mlOnBlur(this)"`
- wired: yes; data source `ML_DATA[iso].remark`

Field: **Status**
- element: `<select class="wl-inp wl-status-sel">` `data-date`/`data-emp`
- options (from `WL_STATUS_OPTIONS`, verbatim, with `''` shown as `—`): `''`(—), `Tentative`, `On Time`, `Late`, `Absent`, `Half Day`
- handlers: `onchange="if(!ML_RENDERING){ML_DIRTY['{iso}']=true;_mlAutoSave('{iso}')}"`
- wired: yes; data source `ML_DATA[iso].status`

Field: **Comments**
- element: `<input type="text" class="wl-inp ml-comment">` `data-date`/`data-emp`
- placeholder: `Comments…`
- handlers: `oninput dirty`, `onblur="_mlOnBlur(this)"`
- wired: yes; data source `ML_DATA[iso].adminComments`

red circle **Admin/TC/TF gating gap:** PRD intent (`_canEditWlStatus`, `app.js.html:208` = `MANAGER_ROLES.indexOf(role)!==-1`) is that only managers may set Status/Comments. In `_renderMemberLogModal` the Status and Comments fields are **always rendered editable for whoever opened the modal** — there is no `_canEditWlStatus(APP.currentUser.role)` check in the chip renderer. In practice the modal is only reachable from the manager-gated `team-logs` view and the backend (`adminUpdateWorkLog`/`adminSubmitWorkLog`) re-checks `_isManager`, so a non-manager cannot reach it; the gap is purely that the helper exists but is never consulted in this renderer. Severity: low (defense-in-depth only). Status: NOTE.

Off-day handling: non-intern off-day rows (`Week Off`/`Holiday`/`Alternate Week Off`) render a "Worked today" checkbox (`ml-odc-{iso}` → `_mlToggleOffDayWork`) that re-enables work fields; the `+OT` badge (`ml-ot-badge-{iso}`) toggles via `_wlShowsOtBadge(att, extraHrs, isOffDayWorked)`. Intern rows use free-text attendance + post-render disable logic.

Work-duration row: each Comments cell appends `_mlDurHtml(iso)` (`app.js.html:2495`) which renders a "Work duration" line with span `#ml-dur-{iso}` defaulting to `—`, populated by `_mlLoadDurationsAsync(start,end)` → **`getWorkDurationsForDates(ML_EMP.Email, start, end)`** (`work-duration.gs:693`) → `_mlApplyDurations()`.
red circle The duration line uses `<i class="ti ti-clock-hour-4">` — a **Tabler icon class with no Tabler font loaded** → renders as a blank box. Same stray `ti ti-*` appears in `_wlDurHtml` (`app.js.html:2421`). Severity: low (cosmetic). Status: BROKEN icon.

#### 4.3.3.4 Auto-save — `_mlSaveEntry` / `ML_DIRTY` / `ML_SAVING` / debounce
- `_mlAutoSave(iso)` (`app.js.html:2625`): shows "Saving…" immediately, debounces 500ms via `ML_DEBOUNCE[iso]`, then `if (!ML_DIRTY[iso] || ML_SAVING[iso]) return; _mlSaveEntry(iso, true)`.
- `_mlOnBlur(inputEl)` (`app.js.html:2662`): textarea blur with content → `_mlAddItem` (which auto-saves); other inputs → `_mlSaveEntry(iso, true)` if dirty.
- `_mlSaveEntry(iso, autoSave)` (`app.js.html:4878`):
  - In-flight guard: `if (ML_SAVING[iso]) { if(autoSave) _mlAutoSave(iso); return; }`
  - Serializes chips via `mlGetWorkText(halfKey)` (`'; '` join, newline → `\n`; on manual save also flushes the textarea's pending text — `!autoSave`).
  - `Leave Full Day` → work1/work2 forced empty.
  - Sets `ML_SAVING[iso]=true; delete ML_DIRTY[iso]` before the GAS call (edits during the call re-set dirty → retried in `onDone`).
  - **Intern member** → `adminSaveInternWorkLog({date,attendance,work1stHalf,work2ndHalf,extraHours,remark}, ML_MEMBER_ID, APP._verifiedEmail)` (expects `{ok, logId}`).
  - **Existing log** → `adminUpdateWorkLog(existingLogId, {Attendance, 'Work Update - 1st Half', 'Work Update - 2nd Half', 'Extra Hours', Remark, Status, Comments}, APP._verifiedEmail)` (expects `{ok}`).
  - **New log** → `adminSubmitWorkLog({date,attendance,work1stHalf,work2ndHalf,extraHours,remark,comments,status}, ML_MEMBER_ID, APP._verifiedEmail)` (returns raw `newId`).
  - `onDone(newLogId)`: `ML_SAVING[iso]=false`; store `logId`; if `ML_DIRTY[iso]` still set → re-`_mlAutoSave` (saves edits that arrived mid-flight); else show "Saved" via `_wlAsStatus('ml',iso,'saved')`.
  - `onFail`: `_wlAsStatus('ml',iso,'error')`; on autoSave retries if still dirty, else toast.
- google.script.run reactions:
  | call | success-shape check | DOM side-effect |
  |---|---|---|
  | `adminSubmitWorkLog` | truthy `newId` else fail | `ML_DATA[iso].logId = newId`, status "Saved" |
  | `adminUpdateWorkLog` | `r.ok` else fail | status "Saved" |
  | `adminSaveInternWorkLog` | `r.ok` (+ `r.logId`) | logId stored, "Saved" |

warning sign **Discrepancy in return-shape between the two save backends:** `adminSubmitWorkLog` (`auth.gs:1391`) returns a **bare string `newId`** (or `{ok:false,error}` on lock failure), whereas `adminUpdateWorkLog` (`auth.gs:1442`) returns `{ok:true}`. The frontend handles each correctly (different success handlers), but `adminSubmitWorkLog` returning `{ok:false,…}` on a lock-timeout would be treated as a truthy `newId` by `onSuccess(function(newId){ if(!newId){…} onDone(newId) })` and stored as the logId — a lock-busy response would be silently mis-stored. Severity: medium. Status: PARTIAL.

warning sign **No 30s fallback timer.** CLAUDE.md change #15 documents `_mlStartAutoTimer`/`_mlStopAutoTimer`/`_ML_AUTO_TIMER` started at end of `_renderMemberLogModal` and stopped on `closeModal('member-log-modal')`. These functions **do not exist in current source** (grep: no matches) — change #21 removed the 30s interval in favor of 500ms debounce. So closing the modal mid-edit relies on the debounce having already fired (≥500ms after the last keystroke) or a blur event; an unblurred field closed within 500ms of the last edit will not save. Severity: low. Status: NOTE (matches #21, contradicts #15).

#### 4.3.3.5 Week navigation in the modal
`_mlPrevWeek`/`_mlNextWeek` (`app.js.html:5022/5027`): clear `ML_DEBOUNCE`, shift `ML_ANCHOR` ±7 days, then `if (_mlNeedsReload()) loadMemberLogDetail(ML_MEMBER_ID); else _renderMemberLogModal()`. `_mlGoToday` → `ML_ANCHOR = _mlWeekMonday(new Date())` then same reload check.
`_mlNeedsReload()` (`app.js.html:4087`): compares the mode's needed range against `ML_LOADED_START/END`; month → full month, custom → custom range, day/week → Mon–Sun of `ML_ANCHOR`. In month/custom modes the nav arrows are visibility:hidden, so the only way to move weeks there is none — the modal stays on the loaded fixed range (intended).

---

### 4.3.4 Day View (By Date tab)

`_tlByDate()` (`app.js.html:4031`):
- Filters `TL_RAW` to the range, then by `#tl-member` selection if set.
- Empty → `emptyState('No work logs for this period','')`.
- Groups by ISO date (`byDate`), sorts dates descending.
- `#tl-sub` set to `"{label} — {n} entr(y/ies) across {d} day(s)"`.
- For each date: `.team-log-day` block with header `{fmtDate} — {n} submission(s)` and one `.tld-entry` per log: avatar initial, employee name, team, effective hours (`_attEffHours`), optional `Tentative` badge, 1st-half text, 2nd-half text, blocker (`⚠ {Remark||Blockers}`), and the work-duration row (`_wlDurHtml(iso, l.Emp_ID)`).

This view is **read-only** (no inline editing; clicking does not open the member modal — only the member cards do). The member filter dropdown is visible only here.

---

### 4.3.5 Gap summary for this slice
1. red circle Status/Comments fields in `_renderMemberLogModal` not gated by `_canEditWlStatus` client-side (backend re-checks `_isManager`; defense-in-depth only).
2. red circle Stray `ti ti-clock-hour-4` Tabler icon in `_mlDurHtml`/`_wlDurHtml` renders blank (no Tabler font).
3. warning sign `adminSubmitWorkLog` return shape inconsistency — a lock-busy `{ok:false}` would be mis-stored as a logId by the new-log success handler.
4. warning sign Dead/parallel renderer `renderMemberLogDetail` + `saveMemberEntry` + `mlOnAttChange` + `mlNav`/`mlNavToday` + `_mlRenderChips`/`_mlRemoveChipItem`/`_mlEditChipItem` (unused by the actual modal buttons).
5. warning sign Week-card `totalHrs` not rounded (float noise possible).
6. warning sign No 30s auto-save fallback timer (CLAUDE.md #15 functions removed per #21) — edits within 500ms of close without a blur are lost.
7. warning sign Member filter dropdown only lists members who already have ≥1 log in the range.


### 4.4–4.5 — Leave Approvals & Team Members

### 4.4 Leave Approvals

The **Leave Approvals** view (`#view-leave-approvals`, `index.html:2427`) is a manager-only page surfacing pending leave requests from the manager's direct reports (all teams for Admins/SAs). Reached via the sidebar nav item `data-view="leave-approvals"` (`index.html:1846`), which carries class `nav-mgr-only` (hidden until `onPayloadLoaded` reveals it for the four manager roles, `app.js.html:1047-1048`) and a count badge `#badge-leave-approvals` (`index.html:1848`).

#### View shell

| Element | id / class | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Page header title | `.ph-title` | "Leave Approvals" (static) |
| Page header sub | `.ph-sub` | "Pending leave requests from your team" (static) |
| List container | `#leave-approvals-container` | Filled by `loadLeaveApprovals()` → `renderLeaveApprovals()` |
| Sidebar nav item | `.nav-item.nav-mgr-only`, `data-view="leave-approvals"` | icon `pending_actions` (Material Symbols) |
| Count badge | `.nav-badge.hidden`, `#badge-leave-approvals` | text = pending count; hidden when 0 |

#### Navigation + badge wiring

- `navigate('leave-approvals')` (`app.js.html:1158`) calls `loadLeaveApprovals()`. There is **no client-side role gate** inside `navigate` (`app.js.html:1130-1166`) — access control is purely by hiding the nav item plus the backend `_isManager` checks in every GAS call. A TM who manually triggers the view would see a backend "Not authorized." error in the container (acceptable defense-in-depth, server-enforced).
- Badge initial value: `updateLeaveBadge(APP._pendingLeaveCount)` in `onPayloadLoaded` (`app.js.html:1064`). `APP._pendingLeaveCount = p.pendingLeaveCount || 0` (`app.js.html:1033`), sourced from `getInitialPayload` which computes `pendingLeaveCount` via `getPendingLeaveCount(user.email)` for managers only (`auth.gs:606-607`, `628`).
- `updateLeaveBadge(count)` (`app.js.html:8327-8332`): sets `#badge-leave-approvals` text and toggles `.hidden` when count is 0/falsy.
- After any approve/reject, `reviewLeave` re-fetches the full payload solely to refresh the badge: `getInitialPayload(...)` → `updateLeaveBadge(p.pendingLeaveCount || 0)` (`app.js.html:12895-12898`). ⚠️ This fires a full `getInitialPayload` (tasks+projects+functions+employees) just to update one number — heavier than calling `getPendingLeaveCount` directly. Minor performance gap.

#### `loadLeaveApprovals()` — `app.js.html:12839-12851`

```javascript
function loadLeaveApprovals() {
  var cont = el('leave-approvals-container');
  cont.innerHTML = '<div class="empty-state">…hourglass_empty…Loading...</div>';
  google.script.run
    .withSuccessHandler(function(r) {
      if (!r || !r.ok) { cont.innerHTML = '…error_outline…' + esc(r && r.error || 'Error'); return; }
      renderLeaveApprovals(r.leaves || []);
    })
    .withFailureHandler(function(err) { cont.innerHTML = '…error_outline…' + esc(err.message); })
    .getPendingLeaves(APP.currentUser.email);
}
```

| google.script.run call | params | success | failure |
|---|---|---|---|
| `getPendingLeaves(APP.currentUser.email)` | manager email | `renderLeaveApprovals(r.leaves)` | shows `error_outline` empty-state with `err.message` |

Loading + error states use Material Symbols icons (`hourglass_empty`, `error_outline`) — consistent with the ground-truth icon library.

#### List render — `renderLeaveApprovals(leaves)` — `app.js.html:12853-12877`

Empty: `emptyState('No pending leave requests', 'All caught up!')`. Otherwise a `<table>` in `.tbl-wrap`:

Columns (verbatim `<th>`): **Employee · Type · Start · End · Days · Reason · Requested · (blank actions)**

| Cell | Source field | Render |
|---|---|---|
| Employee | `l.Emp_Name \|\| l.Emp_ID` | `<strong>`, `esc`'d |
| Type | `l.Leave_Type` | `pill(...)` → `<span class="pill pill-{slug}">` |
| Start | `l.Start_Date` | `fmtDate` → `dd Mon yyyy` (en-IN) |
| End | `l.End_Date` | `fmtDate` |
| Days | `l.Days \|\| 1` | raw number (0.5 for Half Day) |
| Reason | `l.Reason \|\| '—'` | `esc`'d |
| Requested | `l.Created_At` | `fmtDate` |
| Actions | — | two buttons (below) |

**Approve button** — `<button class="btn btn-sm btn-accent" onclick="reviewLeave(this,'{Leave_ID}','Approved')">Approve</button>`
**Reject button** — `<button class="btn btn-sm btn-danger" onclick="reviewLeave(this,'{Leave_ID}','Rejected')">Reject</button>`

Both `Leave_ID` values are `esc`'d into the onclick string.

#### Approve / Reject flow — `reviewLeave(btn, leaveId, status)` — `app.js.html:12879-12902`

- **Reject path:** `notes = prompt('Reason for rejection (optional):')`. If `null` (Cancel) → abort. Otherwise `notes = notes || ''`.
- **Approve path:** `confirm('Approve this leave request?')` — abort if dismissed.
- `_btnLoading(btn, status==='Approved' ? 'Approving…' : 'Rejecting…')` swaps button to spinner.

| google.script.run call | params | success handler | failure handler |
|---|---|---|---|
| `reviewLeaveRequest(leaveId, status, notes, APP.currentUser.email)` | id, `'Approved'`/`'Rejected'`, notes, email | `_btnReset(btn)`; on `!r.ok` → `toast(error,'error')`; else `toast('Leave request '+status.toLowerCase()+'.', status==='Approved'?'success':'warn')`, `loadLeaveApprovals()`, then `getInitialPayload` → `updateLeaveBadge` | `_btnReset(btn)`, `toast(err.message,'error')` |

`wired:` **yes** — approve/reject both call the correct backend, refresh the list, and update the badge.

#### Backend: `reviewLeaveRequest` — `leaves.gs:48-90` (manager scoping)

```javascript
function reviewLeaveRequest(leaveId, status, notes, email) {
  try {
    var user = getCurrentUser(email);
    if (!_isManager(user.role)) throw new Error('Not authorized to review leave requests.');
    if (['Approved', 'Rejected'].indexOf(status) === -1) throw new Error('Invalid status.');
    …
```

Scoping logic:
- Validates session, requires `_isManager(user.role)`, validates `status ∈ {Approved, Rejected}`.
- Loads leave; 404 if not found; rejects if `Status !== 'Pending'` ("no longer pending").
- **Non-admin scoping:** `if (!_isAdmin(user.role))` → looks up the leave's employee and checks **`isDirectReport || isSameTeam`**: `isDirectReport = emp.Manager_ID === user.empId`; `isSameTeam = _getTeamEmpIds(user).has(leave.Emp_ID)`. If neither is true, throws "Not authorized to review this leave request." (`leaves.gs:60-67`).
- On approve: writes `Status/Reviewed_By/Reviewed_At/Review_Notes`, then attempts calendar sync (`_tryCalLeaveSync(..., 'CREATE')`) with the employee's name/email. On reject: if the leave already had a `Cal_Event_ID`, deletes that calendar event.
- Audits `{status}_LEAVE`. Returns `{ok:true}` or `{ok:false,error}`.

#### Backend: `getPendingLeaves` — `leaves.gs:105-133`

Requires `_isManager`. Builds `empMap{empId→{name,managerId}}`. Filters `Leaves` to `Status==='Pending'`; Admins see **all**; TC/TF see leaves where `empMap[Emp_ID].managerId === user.empId` **OR** `_getTeamEmpIds(user).has(Emp_ID)` (same-Team field match). `teamIds` is pre-computed once via `_getTeamEmpIds(user)` before the filter loop (null for admins — admin branch bypasses the filter). Returns each leave augmented with `Emp_Name`. Field names match the renderer (`Emp_Name`, `Leave_Type`, `Start_Date`, `End_Date`, `Days`, `Reason`, `Created_At`, `Leave_ID`).

#### Backend: `getPendingLeaveCount` — `leaves.gs:259-273`

Returns a **plain integer** (not a `{ok,count}` object). Admins → total pending; managers → count of direct-reports' pending; non-managers / error → `0`. Consumed only inside `getInitialPayload` (`auth.gs:607`). Frontend never calls it directly.
