### 7.20 Gap log (this part)

| # | Function / location | Severity | Status | Note |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `submitFormResponse` — app.js:14808 call, no `*.gs` def | high | BROKEN | Form-fill submit always fails; success view ("Your response has been recorded.") unreachable. |
| 2 | `getOrgChartData()` — auth.gs:817 | high | NOTE | No `email` param / no auth; leaks full org tree to any URL holder. |
| 3 | `getWorkDurationsForDates(email,...)` — work-duration.gs:693 | medium | NOTE | No `getCurrentUser`/role gate; can read any employee's clock data by email. |
| 4 | `getMeetingsForRange(...)` — meet.gs:187 | low | NOTE | No `getCurrentUser` gate (reads caller's own primary calendar via OAuth — low risk, but inconsistent). |
| 5 | `migrationPreview/Import/ImportDirectRows` — migration.gs:388/426/467 | medium | PARTIAL | Session-only; no `_isManager`/`_isAdmin` gate → any authenticated user can bulk-import tasks. |
| 6 | `reviewWorkLog` — auth.gs:1282 | low | UNUSED | 0 frontend + 0 internal callers (only tests.gs). |
| 7 | `getFunctions` — auth.gs:1061 | low | UNUSED | Frontend reads `APP.functions` synchronously (CLAUDE.md #33). |
| 8 | `saveTodo`/`saveNote`/`saveIdea` — notes.gs | low | UNUSED | Superseded by `tasksCreateTask`/`tasksUpdateTask`. |
| 9 | `getInternMemberWorkLogs` — intern-work-log.gs:135 | low | UNUSED | `getMemberWorkLogs` routes interns directly via `_getInternWlLogs`. |
| 10 | `createTask` — auth.gs:870 | low | NOTE | No role gate (intentional self-assign default, but any user can create org-wide tasks if they supply assignee IDs). |
| 11 | Response-contract inconsistency (CRUD throws vs `{ok:false}`) | low | NOTE | `createTask/updateTask/deleteTask/createFunction` etc. `throw`; most others return `{ok:false}`. Frontend must handle both `withFailureHandler` and `!r.ok`. |
| 12 | Identity = `email` param only (`getCurrentUser`) | medium | NOTE | No per-call session-token verification; client-supplied email is trusted. Architectural (USER_DEPLOYING). |


## PART 8: GOOGLE SHEETS SCHEMA

This part documents every sheet (database table) used by LG Desk: its columns **in declaration order**, the value type/format actually written by code, and notes on validation and drift.

### Source of truth & ownership map

There is **no single schema file**. The canonical `SCHEMA` constant in `src/setupSheets.gs` (lines 3–65) owns 15 sheets. The remaining 8 operational sheets are **not** in `SCHEMA` — each is bootstrapped on first use ("ensure" pattern) by its own module:

| Sheet | Defined in `SCHEMA`? | Owner / bootstrap function | File:line |
|---|---|---|---|
| Employees | yes | `setupDatabase` / `migrateSchema` | setupSheets.gs:4 |
| Projects | yes | same | setupSheets.gs:7 |
| Functions | yes | same | setupSheets.gs:10 |
| Tasks | yes | same | setupSheets.gs:17 |
| Leaves | yes | same | setupSheets.gs:21 |
| Holidays | yes | same | setupSheets.gs:25 |
| Progress_Updates | yes | same | setupSheets.gs:28 |
| Work_Log | yes | same | setupSheets.gs:31 |
| Announcements | yes | same | setupSheets.gs:39 |
| Audit_Log | yes | same | setupSheets.gs:42 |
| Registration_Requests | yes | same | setupSheets.gs:45 |
| Profile_Update_Requests | yes | same | setupSheets.gs:49 |
| Due_Date_Requests | yes | same | setupSheets.gs:53 |
| Weekly_Summary | yes | same | setupSheets.gs:58 |
| MIS_Access | yes | same | setupSheets.gs:62 |
| Work_Duration | **no** | `setupWorkDurationSheets()` | work-duration.gs:10 |
| Work_Breaks | **no** | `setupWorkDurationSheets()` | work-duration.gs:24 |
| Intern_Work_Log | **no** | `_ensureInternWlSheet()` | intern-work-log.gs:35 |
| Attachments | **no** | `_ensureAttachmentsSheet()` | attachments.gs:19 |
| Forms | **no** | `_ensureFormsSheet()` | forms.gs:176 |
| Todos | **no** | `initNotesSheets()` | notes.gs:4 |
| Notes | **no** | `initNotesSheets()` | notes.gs:4 |
| Ideas | **no** | `initNotesSheets()` | notes.gs:4 |

> ⚠️ Because 8 of 23 sheets are missing from `SCHEMA`, running `setupDatabase()` or `migrateSchema()` does **not** create Work_Duration, Work_Breaks, Intern_Work_Log, Attachments, Forms, Todos, Notes, or Ideas. They are only created lazily on first write (or by manually running `setupWorkDurationSheets()` / `initNotesSheets()`). `migrateSchema()` will also never add new columns to these 8 sheets. See gaps below.

`migrateSchema()` (setupSheets.gs:190–244) adds any `SCHEMA` column missing from the live header row (never clears data) and deletes columns listed in `DEPRECATED_COLUMNS` (setupSheets.gs:186–188 — only `Tasks.Parent_Task_ID`).

---

### Sheet: Employees
Schema source: `setupSheets.gs:4-6`.

| # | Column | Type/Format | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Emp_ID | string `EMP-#####` | primary key, generated via `generateId` |
| 2 | First_Name | string | |
| 3 | Last_Name | string | |
| 4 | Email | string (email) | login identity / lookup key |
| 5 | Role | enum | validation list (see below) |
| 6 | Designation | string | free text |
| 7 | Manager_ID | string (Emp_ID) | FK → Employees |
| 8 | Team | enum (DIVISIONS) | dropdown from `TEAM_HIERARCHY` keys |
| 9 | Sub_Department | enum (SUB_DEPARTMENTS) | dropdown from `TEAM_HIERARCHY` values |
| 10 | Is_Active | `'TRUE'`/`'FALSE'` string | dropdown |
| 11 | Password_Hash | string | `SHA256(password + 'tms_2025')` (legacy) |
| 12 | DOB | date | |
| 13 | Created_At | `yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss` (IST) | |

**Validations (`VALIDATIONS.Employees`, setupSheets.gs:91-96):**
- `Role`: `['Super Admin','Admin','Team Captain','Team Facilitator','Team Member','Intern']`
- `Is_Active`: `['TRUE','FALSE']`
- `Team`: `DIVISIONS` (the 8 `TEAM_HIERARCHY` keys)
- `Sub_Department`: `SUB_DEPARTMENTS` (all sub-dept values, flattened)

> ⚠️ Column order differs from the CLAUDE.md doc. CLAUDE.md lists `... | Manager_ID | Team | Sub_Department | Designation | DOB | Password_Hash | Is_Active`, but the **actual** code order is `... Role | Designation | Manager_ID | Team | Sub_Department | Is_Active | Password_Hash | DOB | Created_At`. The doc is stale; the code (above) is authoritative.

### Sheet: Work_Log
Schema source: `setupSheets.gs:31-38`. Same column schema is reused by Intern_Work_Log.

| # | Column | Type/Format | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Log_ID | `WL-#####` (via `_wlNextId`) | primary key |
| 2 | Emp_ID | Emp_ID | |
| 3 | Date | `yyyy-MM-dd` | |
| 4 | Month | number (1-12) | derived `d.getMonth()+1` |
| 5 | Day | day name | e.g. `Monday` |
| 6 | Attendance | enum | dropdown |
| 7 | Purpose | enum | leave purpose |
| 8 | Leave Requested | enum | header has a **space**, not underscore |
| 9 | Work Update - 1st Half | string | spaces + hyphen in header |
| 10 | Work Update - 2nd Half | string | |
| 11 | Extra Hours | number | |
| 12 | Remark | string | |
| 13 | Status | enum | TC/TF-editable |
| 14 | Comments | string | |
| 15 | Created_At | ISO ts | |

**Validations (`VALIDATIONS.Work_Log`, setupSheets.gs:131-136):**
- `Attendance`: `['Present','Leave Full Day','Leave Half Day','Alternate Week Off','Week Off','Holiday','Extra Full Day','Extra Half Day']`
- `Purpose`: `['Planned Leave','Sick Leave','Casual Leave','Emergency Leave','Other']`
- `Leave_Requested`: `['Same Day','One Day Before','Within Same Week','One Week Before']` 🔴 **the validation key is `Leave_Requested` (underscore) but the actual header column is `Leave Requested` (space)** — so in `setupDatabase`/`refreshValidations` `headers.indexOf('Leave_Requested')` returns `-1` and the dropdown is **never applied** to this column.
- `Status`: `['Tentative','On Time','Late','Absent','Half Day']`

> 🔴 **DRIFT — `Work_Duration` column written but NOT in SCHEMA.** `submitWorkLog` / `adminSubmitWorkLog` in `auth.gs` write a `Work_Duration` field to Work_Log rows (auth.gs:1246, 1430, and back-fill at 1445-1455: `if (log && !log['Work_Duration']) { ... Work_Duration: _wd }`). This column is **not** listed in `SCHEMA.Work_Log`. Because `dbInsert`/`dbUpdate` map by header name, the value lands in this column only if a `Work_Duration` header already exists in the live sheet (added manually or appended ad-hoc); otherwise the write silently no-ops. `migrateSchema()` will never create it. Treat as schema drift.

### Sheet: Work_Duration (out-of-SCHEMA — production shape)
Header source: `work-duration.gs:16-20`, created by `setupWorkDurationSheets()` (work-duration.gs:10-22). 12 columns.

| # | Column | Type/Format | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Session_ID | `WDS-#####` | primary key |
| 2 | Emp_ID | Emp_ID | |
| 3 | Email | string | |
| 4 | Emp_Name | string | |
| 5 | Date | `yyyy-MM-dd` | one session per person per day |
| 6 | Clock_In | ISO ts | |
| 7 | Clock_Out | ISO ts | empty while active |
| 8 | Total_Break_Mins | number | |
| 9 | Net_Work_Mins | number | |
| 10 | Status | `IDLE`/`ACTIVE`/`ON_BREAK`/`COMPLETED` | state machine |
| 11 | Notes | string | audit/edit notes appended |
| 12 | Created_At | ISO ts | |

> 🔴 **DEV vs PROD SHAPE MISMATCH.** `env-setup.gs:190-198` defines a *different* `Work_Duration` shape used when scaffolding the **dev** database (`_setupWorkDurationInSpreadsheet`):
> `['Session_ID','Emp_ID','Date','Clock_In','Clock_Out','Total_Break_Mins','Net_Work_Mins','Status','Notes','Edited_By','Edited_At','Edit_Reason']`
> This **omits** `Email`, `Emp_Name`, `Created_At` and **adds** `Edited_By`, `Edited_At`, `Edit_Reason`. So a dev DB created by `setupDevDatabase()` has a Work_Duration sheet that does NOT match what `wdClockIn`/`wdGetStatus`/`wdClockOut` write (they write `Email`/`Emp_Name`/`Created_At`). Column-name-keyed I/O means `Email`/`Emp_Name`/`Created_At` writes land in wrong/missing columns on dev. Flag as gap.

---

Production code (`wdStartBreak`/`wdEndBreak`) writes `Break_Mins` and `Created_At`, not `Duration_Mins`/`Emp_ID`. Same dev/prod drift problem as Work_Duration.

### Sheet: Announcements
Schema source: `setupSheets.gs:39-41` (9 columns).

| # | Column | Type/Format | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Ann_ID | `ANN-####` | primary key |
| 2 | Type | enum | |
| 3 | Title | string | |
| 4 | Message | string | |
| 5 | Target_Date | date | used as the announcement **start date** |
| 6 | Priority | enum | |
| 7 | Is_Active | `'TRUE'`/`'FALSE'` | |
| 8 | Created_By | Emp_ID | |
| 9 | Created_At | ISO ts | |

**Validations (setupSheets.gs:126-130):**
- `Type`: `['General','Emergency','Reminder','Birthday','Holiday']`
- `Priority`: `['Normal','High','Urgent']`
- `Is_Active`: `['TRUE','FALSE']`

> 🔴 **DRIFT — `Visibility` & `Expires_At` written by dashboard.gs but NOT in SCHEMA.** `createAnnouncement` (dashboard.gs:253-266) writes a record containing `Visibility` and `Expires_At` fields, and `_getNotices` (dashboard.gs:39, 43, 47) reads `a['Expires_At']`, `a['Target_Date']`, and `a['Visibility']`. Neither `Visibility` nor `Expires_At` is in `SCHEMA.Announcements`. So `migrateSchema()` does not create these columns; the values only persist if those headers were added manually. On a fresh/migrated sheet, `Visibility` reads back as `undefined` → defaulted to `'Organisation'` (dashboard.gs:47) and `Expires_At` reads empty → never expires (and the start-date gate at dashboard.gs:43 reads `Target_Date`, which DOES exist). Net effect: visibility gating and auto-expiry described in Changes #25/#26 silently do nothing unless the two extra columns are hand-added. Flag as schema drift / partially-implemented feature.

### Sheet: Forms (out-of-SCHEMA)
Header source: `forms.gs:181`, created by `_ensureFormsSheet()` (forms.gs:176-186). 12 columns.

| # | Column | Type/Format | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Form_ID | `FORM-####` | primary key |

| 3 | Description | string | |

> ⚠️ CLAUDE.md documents Forms as `Form_ID | Title | Description | Created_By_ID | Team_ID | Visibility | Status | Google_Form_ID | Edit_URL | Publish_URL | Is_Active | Created_At | Updated_At`. The **actual** header has **no `Google_Form_ID`** and uses **`Responder_URL`** (not `Publish_URL`). The Google form id is only embedded inside the URLs; `gfUpdateForm`/`gfDeleteForm` re-locate rows by substring-matching the formId inside `Edit_URL`/`Responder_URL` (forms.gs:260, 340). Doc stale; treat missing `Google_Form_ID` as a minor gap.
> 🔴 `gfGetSharedForms` (forms.gs:359) only returns rows where `Status === 'Active'`, but `gfPublishForm` defaults new rows to `Status: 'Draft'` (forms.gs:220) — so a just-published form is hidden from the shared list until its status is changed to exactly `Active`. Potential UX gap.

## PART 9: SCHEDULED TRIGGERS

### Triggers installed by `installTriggers()` (triggers.gs:13-37)

`installTriggers()` first deletes **all** existing project triggers (triggers.gs:15) to avoid duplicates, then registers exactly four:

| Trigger handler | Schedule (as coded) | What it does | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| `nightlyArchive` | daily, `atHour(2)` `inTimezone('Asia/Kolkata')` → 2 AM IST | Calls `archiveOldRecords()` to move closed records >90 days to the backup DB (BACKUP_DB_ID). Wrapped in try/catch (triggers.gs:39-43). | triggers.gs:18-19 |
| `dailyCalendarSync` | daily, `atHour(6)` `inTimezone('Asia/Kolkata')` → 6 AM IST | Calls `fullSyncCalendar()` to reconcile Google Calendar with Tasks/Projects/Leaves/Holidays (triggers.gs:47-55). | triggers.gs:22-23 |
| `wdAutoClockOut` | `everyHours(1)` (hourly, no timezone) | Auto-closes stale Work_Duration sessions. ⚠️ Header comment (triggers.gs:7,25) and CLAUDE.md say "open >18 hours", but **GROUND TRUTH**: this is a daily midnight-UTC (05:30 IST) reset, NOT an 18-hour cap; there is no `LIMIT_MS`. The hourly schedule + reset logic differs from the "18-hour" comment. | triggers.gs:26-27 |
| `generateWeeklySummaries` | `onWeekDay(MONDAY)` `atHour(0)` `inTimezone('Etc/UTC')` → Mon 12 AM UTC (05:30 IST) | AI-generates per-employee weekly work summaries (Gemini `gemini-2.5-flash`, key `GEMINI_API_KEY`). Has a 5-min time-budget guard that self-schedules a one-shot continuation trigger (`WS_CONT_TRIGGER_ID`) when the run can't finish in one execution. | triggers.gs:32-33 |

`removeAllTriggers()` (triggers.gs:57-60) deletes every project trigger.

> 🔴 `nightlyArchive` and `syncChatSpaces` (see below) are BOTH scheduled at 2 AM (one IST, one in the script's default timezone). If installed together they fire in the same window. Minor scheduling note.

### Triggers installed SEPARATELY (NOT by `installTriggers()`)

These handlers run on a schedule too, but are registered by their own dedicated installer functions — running `installTriggers()` does **not** create them, and (critically) `installTriggers()` **deletes them** because it wipes all triggers first. They must be re-installed after every `installTriggers()` run.

| Trigger handler | Installer fn | Schedule | What it does | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `syncChatSpaces` | `setupChatAutoSync()` | `everyDays(1).atHour(2)` (no `inTimezone` → script default TZ, ~2 AM) | Creates/syncs Google Chat spaces for teams/projects. `removeChatAutoSync()` removes it. | chatSpaces.gs:269-275 |
| `generateWeeklySummaries` (continuation) | self-scheduled inside `generateWeeklySummaries` | one-shot `.timeBased().after(60*1000)` (60s) | Resume token when the weekly run exceeds the ~5-min budget; id stored in `WS_CONT_TRIGGER_ID` and deleted at the start of the next run. | weekly-summary.gs:291-302 |

> ⚠️ `cleanupScriptProperties()` (auth.gs:1905) — deletes expired `sess_*` session tokens and legacy `pres_*` presence keys — is documented as maintenance but is **NOT installed as a trigger by any installer** in the codebase (`installTriggers`, `setupChatAutoSync`, etc. do not register it). It is a manual-run-only function. If session-token cleanup is intended to be automatic, this is a gap (ScriptProperties will accumulate expired tokens until run manually).

> ⚠️ `checkAndArchiveIfNeeded()` (env-setup.gs:115) — the "optional hourly" size-threshold archive mentioned in CLAUDE.md — is also **NOT** registered by `installTriggers()`. It exists as a function but no installer creates a trigger for it. Documented-but-not-wired.

### Trigger gaps summary

| Gap | Detail | Severity |
|---|---|---|
| `wdAutoClockOut` "18-hour" doc vs reality | comments/CLAUDE.md say open>18h; ground truth = daily midnight-UTC reset, no LIMIT_MS | medium |
| `installTriggers()` wipes chat/cleanup triggers | deletes ALL triggers first, so `syncChatSpaces` (and any manual triggers) are silently removed and must be reinstalled via `setupChatAutoSync()` | medium |
| `cleanupScriptProperties` not auto-scheduled | no installer registers it; expired session tokens accumulate | medium |
| `checkAndArchiveIfNeeded` not auto-scheduled | documented "optional hourly" but no installer wires it | low |
| `syncChatSpaces` missing timezone | `atHour(2)` with no `inTimezone` uses script default TZ, not guaranteed IST | low |


## PART 10: ROLE-BASED ACCESS CONTROL

> Source of truth: `src/auth.gs`, `src/leaves.gs`, `src/dashboard.gs`, `src/weekly-summary.gs`, `src/work-duration.gs`, `src/dueDateRequests.gs`, `src/migration.gs`, `src/intern-work-log.gs`, `src/app.js.html`, `src/index.html`.
> Every cell below is derived from an actual `if (...)` predicate in the named function, NOT from the PRD narrative. Where the code and the documented intent diverge it is flagged inline and logged in the GAP table.

Lines read for this slice are listed at the end of the structured return.

---

### 10.1 Role Hierarchy

Roles are stored verbatim in the `Employees.Role` column and resolved at runtime by `getCurrentUser(email)` (`auth.gs:275`), which returns `{ emp, role: emp['Role'], empId, email }`. There is **no numeric level**; access is gated purely by membership in two constant arrays, defined **identically** on the backend (`auth.gs:185-189`) and the frontend (`app.js.html:203-206`):

```javascript
// auth.gs:185
var ADMIN_ROLES   = ['Super Admin', 'Admin'];
var MANAGER_ROLES = ['Super Admin', 'Admin', 'Team Captain', 'Team Facilitator'];
function _isAdmin(role)   { return ADMIN_ROLES.indexOf(role)   !== -1; }
function _isManager(role) { return MANAGER_ROLES.indexOf(role) !== -1; }
```

Two derived predicates:

- **`_canEditWlStatus(role)`** — defined **only on the frontend** (`app.js.html:208`): `return MANAGER_ROLES.indexOf(role) !== -1;` — exactly the same set as `_isManager`. ⚠️ There is **no `_canEditWlStatus` function in any `.gs` file** (the CLAUDE.md "key conventions" note implies a backend predicate; the backend instead inlines `_isManager(user.role)` everywhere it gates Work-Log Status/Comments, e.g. `submitWorkLog` line 1244, `updateWorkLogStatus` line 1261). The name only exists client-side.
- **`_isTmSelfAssign(record, user)`** (`auth.gs:193`): returns `true` for any manager; otherwise `true` only when the record's assignee list is empty or contains solely the caller's own `empId`. Lets a Team Member create/update Functions for themselves.

#### Conceptual ladder (derived from code, NOT a stored hierarchy)

| Rank | Role string | `_isAdmin` | `_isManager` | What the code lets it do that lower ranks cannot |
|---|---|:--:|:--:|---|
| 1 | `Super Admin` | yes | yes | Promote anyone to **any** role incl. Super Admin/Admin (`_allowedNewRoles`); everything Admin can do |
| 2 | `Admin` | yes | yes | All-company task/project/function visibility; add/delete holidays; post/delete announcements; approve **any** leave; direct due-date change on any entity; change roles **except** Super Admin/Admin targets |
| 3 | `Team Captain` (TC) | no | yes | Team-scoped manager powers; change roles of **TM/Intern in own team only** |
| 4 | `Team Facilitator` (TF) | no | yes | **Identical to TC in every `_isManager` gate**; the ONLY difference is `_allowedNewRoles` returns `[]` for TF → ❌ TF **cannot change any employee role** (see 10.1 note below) |
| 5 | `Team Member` (TM) | no | no | Personal task/project/function CRUD; self-assign functions; submit own work log (no Status/Comments) |
| 6 | `Intern` | no | no | **Treated as TM for all `_isAdmin`/`_isManager` gates** (Intern is in neither array). The single divergence: work logs route to the separate `Intern_Work_Log` sheet via `intern-work-log.gs`, not `Work_Log` |

**TF == TC note:** Because `MANAGER_ROLES` contains both, *every* `_isManager` check (leave review, work-log review, team clock status, project create/delete, function delete, team work-log view, scoreboard team scope, due-date approval as assigner) treats TC and TF **the same**. The sole asymmetry is role-change authority:

```javascript
// auth.gs:1786 _allowedNewRoles
if (actorRole === 'Team Captain') {            // TC branch exists
  if (targetCurrentRole !== 'Team Member' && targetCurrentRole !== 'Intern') return [];
  return ['Team Member','Intern','Team Facilitator','Team Captain'];
}
return [];   // ← Team Facilitator falls through here → [] → cannot change ANY role
```
🔴 A **Team Facilitator has no `_allowedNewRoles` branch**, so it can never change a role even though `renderTeamMgmt`/`renderOrgPage` show it the Team Management view (the "Change Role" button is suppressed because `_allowedNewRoles(u.role, e.Role).length > 0` is false). This is consistent across front/back ends but is worth flagging: the PRD's "TC & TF" equivalence does NOT hold for role administration.

**Intern == TM note:** `Intern` appears in neither `ADMIN_ROLES` nor `MANAGER_ROLES`, so it hits the same `else` branches as `Team Member` in `getAuthorizedTasks/Projects/Functions`, `canModifyTask`, `_getScores`, etc. Work logs are the one carve-out: `getMemberWorkLogs` (`auth.gs:1376`) routes `targetEmp['Role'] === 'Intern'` to `_getInternWlLogs`, and interns save via `saveInternWorkLog` (`intern-work-log.gs:64`) into `Intern_Work_Log`. ⚠️ Interns are also *receivable* as role-change targets (`_allowedNewRoles` lists `'Intern'`) but `Intern` is not itself a manager/admin actor anywhere.

---

### 10.2 RBAC Matrix

Legend: ✅ = full / allowed · ❌ = blocked / hidden · ⚠️ = partial or conditional (footnoted). "Direct vs request" cells use **D** (direct change) / **R** (must request).

| Feature | SA | Admin | TC | TF | TM | Intern |
|---|:--:|:--:|:--:|:--:|:--:|:--:|
| View/edit **own** work log | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ ⁽¹⁾ |
| View **team** work logs | ✅ ⁽²⁾ | ✅ ⁽²⁾ | ✅ ⁽³⁾ | ✅ ⁽³⁾ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Edit a team member's work log (incl. Status/Comments) | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| View **MIS Report** | ⚠️ ⁽⁴⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽⁴⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽⁴⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽⁴⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽⁴⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽⁴⁾ |
| Approve / reject leave | ✅ all | ✅ all | ⚠️ ⁽⁵⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽⁵⁾ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Add / delete holiday | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Post / delete announcement | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Create task | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ ⁽⁶⁾ | ✅ ⁽⁶⁾ |
| Edit task | ✅ all | ✅ all | ⚠️ ⁽⁷⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽⁷⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽⁸⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽⁸⁾ |
| Delete task | ✅ all | ✅ all | ⚠️ ⁽⁹⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽⁹⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽¹⁰⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽¹⁰⁾ |
| Create project | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ ⁽¹¹⁾ | ❌ ⁽¹¹⁾ |
| Edit project | ✅ all | ✅ all | ⚠️ ⁽¹²⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽¹²⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽¹²⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽¹²⁾ |
| Delete project | ✅ all | ✅ all | ⚠️ ⁽¹³⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽¹³⁾ | ❌ ⁽¹³⁾ | ❌ ⁽¹³⁾ |
| Create function | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ⚠️ ⁽¹⁴⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽¹⁴⁾ |
| Edit function | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ⚠️ ⁽¹⁵⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽¹⁵⁾ |
| Delete function | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Change due date **directly** vs **request** | D | D | ⚠️ ⁽¹⁶⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽¹⁶⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽¹⁶⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽¹⁶⁾ |
| Approve / reject due-date request | ✅ all | ✅ all | ⚠️ ⁽¹⁷⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽¹⁷⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽¹⁷⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽¹⁷⁾ |
| Change employee role | ✅ ⁽¹⁸⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽¹⁹⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽²⁰⁾ | ❌ ⁽²¹⁾ | ❌ | ❌ |
| View **all-company** tasks/projects | ✅ | ✅ | ⚠️ ⁽²²⁾ | ⚠️ ⁽²²⁾ | ❌ ⁽²³⁾ | ❌ ⁽²³⁾ |
| View Org page | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ ⁽²⁴⁾ | ✅ ⁽²⁴⁾ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Manage team members (Team Mgmt view) | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Import tasks (migration) | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Scoreboard scope | company | company | team-tree ⁽²⁶⁾ | team-tree ⁽²⁶⁾ | self only | self only |

#### Footnotes (exact behaviour)

1. **Own work log** — `submitWorkLog` (`auth.gs:1216`) is open to any authenticated user; `updateWorkLog` (`auth.gs:1294`) gate: `if (log['Emp_ID'] !== user.empId && !_isAdmin(user.role)) throw`. ⚠️ Status/Comments on own log are forced empty for non-managers: `Status: _isManager(user.role) ? (record.status||'') : ''` (line 1244). Interns submit through `saveInternWorkLog` instead.
2. **Admin/SA team work logs** — `getTeamWorkLogs` (`auth.gs:1464`) gate `if (!_isManager(user.role)) throw`; then `_isAdmin(user.role) ? all : team-filtered` → admins see **all** logs across the org.
3. **TC/TF team work logs** — same function, non-admin branch filters `all.filter(l => _getTeamEmpIds(user).has(l['Emp_ID']))` → only same-`Team` members. `getMemberWorkLogs` (`auth.gs:1372`) and `getTeamClockStatus` (`work-duration.gs:552`) use the same `_isManager` gate + `_getTeamEmpIds` scope. 🔴 `_getTeamEmpIds` (`auth.gs:641`) scopes by **exact `Team` string match only**, so a TC sees the **entire Team** (all sub-departments), not a manager-subordinate tree — broader than the org-chart hierarchy implies.
4. **MIS Report** — **not role-gated at all**; gated by the `MIS_Access` sheet via `wsCheckMisAccess(email)` → `_wsHasMisAccess` (`weekly-summary.gs:496`), which lower-cases the email and checks membership. Nav item `#nav-mis-report` is unhidden when `p.hasMisAccess` (`app.js.html:1054-1056`). Server enforces it again in `getMisSummaries` (`weekly-summary.gs:459`: `if (!_wsHasMisAccess(user.email)) return {ok:false,error:'Access denied'}`). Any role — including TM/Intern — can have MIS access if listed in the sheet. (The per-employee `getWeeklySummary`/`saveWeeklySummary` are separate and scoped to the caller's **own** email regardless of role.)
5. **TC/TF leave approval** — `reviewLeaveRequest` (`leaves.gs:48`) gate `if (!_isManager) throw`; non-admin allowed when `emp.Manager_ID === user.empId` **OR** `_getTeamEmpIds(user).has(leave.Emp_ID)` (same-Team). Both conditions are checked independently — either grants access. `getPendingLeaves` (`leaves.gs:105`) uses the identical additive OR filter (admins see all Pending; TC/TF see leaves for direct reports + same-Team members). `getPendingLeaveCount` (`leaves.gs:259`) uses the same OR so the sidebar badge always matches the list. ✅ Previously this was direct-report only (`Manager_ID === user.empId`), causing TC/TF to see an empty Leave Approvals list for teammates registered with a fallback Admin `Manager_ID`. Fixed.
6. **TM/Intern create task** — `createTask` (`auth.gs:870`) has **no role gate**; if no assignee given it defaults `Assignee_IDs = user.empId` (line 877). Anyone authenticated can create a task.
7. **TC/TF edit task** — `updateTask` → `canModifyTask` (`auth.gs:848`): manager branch allows any task where a team member is assignee/assigner, plus unassigned tasks in team-associated projects.
8. **TM/Intern edit task** — `canModifyTask` TM branch: only tasks where `_parseIds(task['Assignee_IDs']).indexOf(user.empId) !== -1 || task['Assigner_ID'] === user.empId`.
9. **TC/TF delete task** — `deleteTask` (`auth.gs:1024`): non-admin allowed only if owner (`Assigner_ID === empId`) OR `_isManager && isAssignee`.
10. **TM/Intern delete task** — same gate; allowed only when they are the task's `Assigner_ID` (creator). A TM who is merely an assignee **cannot** delete (the `_isManager && isAssignee` clause excludes them).
11. **TM/Intern create project** — `createProject` (`auth.gs:942`): `if (!_isManager(user.role)) throw new Error('Employees cannot create projects.')` → hard block. Frontend "+ New Project" button is `nav-mgr-only` (index.html:2107).
12. **Edit project (non-admin)** — `updateProject` (`auth.gs:976`): non-admin allowed if `Assigner_ID === empId` OR in `Owner_IDs` OR in `Assignee_IDs`. ⚠️ This means a **TM who is an assignee can edit the project** (intentional per inline comment). `Owner_IDs` is protected: `delete updates.Owner_IDs`.
13. **Delete project (non-admin)** — `deleteProject` (`auth.gs:1043`): `if (!_isManager) throw 'Only managers can delete projects.'` then non-admin must be `Assigner_ID` or in `Owner_IDs`. TM/Intern blocked outright.
14. **TM/Intern create function** — `createFunction` (`auth.gs:1068`): `if (!_isManager(user.role) && !_isTmSelfAssign(record, user)) throw`. A TM may create a function **only when it is assigned to themselves or unassigned** (self-assign rule).
15. **TM/Intern edit function** — `updateFunction` (`auth.gs:1098`): non-manager allowed if `isAssignee || isCreator (Created_By or Assigner_ID === empId)`; else throws.
16. **Due-date change direct vs request** — `requestDueDateChange` (`dueDateRequests.gs:14`): `if (_isAdmin(user.role) || assignerId === user.empId) return {ok:true, direct:true}` → **Admin/SA and the entity's own Assigner change directly**; **everyone else (incl. TC/TF/TM/Intern who are not the assigner) must submit a request**. So the D/R outcome depends on whether the caller is the entity Assigner, not purely on role.
17. **Approve/reject due-date request** — `approveDueDateChange` / `rejectDueDateChange` (`dueDateRequests.gs:121/155`): `if (!_isAdmin(user.role) && req['Approver_ID'] !== user.empId) return {ok:false}`. So **Admin/SA approve any**; any other role approves **only requests where they are the `Approver_ID`** (= the entity's Assigner). A TC/TF/TM can therefore approve iff they are the assigner of the affected entity.
18. **SA change role** — `_allowedNewRoles('Super Admin', any)` returns the full set `['Team Member','Intern','Team Facilitator','Team Captain','Admin','Super Admin']`. `changeEmployeeRole` (`auth.gs:1803`) also blocks changing **your own** role (`if (targetEmpId === user.empId)`).
19. **Admin change role** — `_allowedNewRoles('Admin', target)` returns `[]` when target is currently Super Admin or Admin (cannot touch peers/superiors); otherwise `['Team Member','Intern','Team Facilitator','Team Captain','Admin']` (note: an Admin **can** promote to Admin but not to Super Admin).
20. **TC change role** — `_allowedNewRoles('Team Captain', target)` returns `[]` unless target is currently TM or Intern; then `['Team Member','Intern','Team Facilitator','Team Captain']`. Additionally `changeEmployeeRole` enforces same-team: `if (actorTeam !== targetTeam) return {ok:false}` (line 1822). So a TC may only promote/change **TM/Intern within their own Team**.
21. **TF change role** — 🔴 `_allowedNewRoles` has **no Team Facilitator branch** → falls to `return []` → TF can never change any role. (See 10.1.)
22. **TC/TF view all-company tasks/projects** — `renderAllTasks` (`app.js.html:6007`) / `renderAllProjects` (`6060`): for non-admin managers it **forces a team scope** — `if (!_isAdmin && _isManager && !_ALL_SCOPE['all-tsk']) _ALL_SCOPE['all-tsk'] = APP.currentUser.team` then filters by `_belongsToMyTeam`. So the "Company" nav items are visible to TC/TF (they are `nav-mgr-only`, index.html:1855-1859) but data is **clamped to their own team** — not truly all-company. The backend never sends them other teams' tasks anyway (`getAuthorizedTasks` manager branch).
23. **TM/Intern all-company** — "Company" nav section + items are `nav-mgr-only` (hidden); `getAuthorizedTasks/Projects` give them only personal data.
24. **TC/TF Org page** — nav item `data-view="org-page"` is `nav-mgr-only` (index.html:1862), so **TC/TF can open the Org page**, which lists all employees and all pending registrations (`renderOrgPage`, `app.js.html:6385`). ⚠️ Inside it the "Change Role" button per employee is still gated by `_allowedNewRoles(u.role, e.Role).length > 0` (line 6398), so a TF sees the full directory but no actionable role buttons, and a TC sees buttons only for own-team TM/Interns. There is **no `_isAdmin` guard on `renderOrgPage` itself** — the comment "Organisation Page (Admin/Super Admin)" overstates the gate.
25. **Import tasks** — ✅ **INTENTIONALLY OPEN TO ALL ROLES** (deliberate product decision confirmed 2026-06-30). `#nav-import-btn` is pinned at the top of the sidebar for every logged-in user. `openMigrateModal()` has no role check. Backend `migrationPreview`, `migrationImport`, `migrationImportDirectRows` (`migration.gs`) each call `getCurrentUser(email)` and include an audit comment explaining the decision. All imported records use the importer as `Assigner_ID`/`Created_By`. *Original concern (preserved in GAP RBAC-B below)*: any TM/Intern could bulk-overwrite the shared task/function hierarchy. Product owner reviewed the trade-off and confirmed open access is desired.
26. **Scoreboard scope** — `_getScores` (`dashboard.gs:320`): `_isAdmin` → `company` (no filter); `_isManager` → `team` = `getSubordinateIds(user.empId)` **+ self** (a manager-subordinate tree, NOT the flat `_getTeamEmpIds` team — different scoping than work-logs!); else → `self`. ⚠️ Note the inconsistency: scoreboard uses the **subordinate tree** (`getSubordinateIds`) while team work-logs / clock-status use the **flat team match** (`_getTeamEmpIds`). The Work_Log term was removed from the score formula; score = `done*10 + inProg*3 - overdue*5`.

---

#### Gate reference — predicate per matrix row

| Matrix row | Backend gate (file:line · function) | Predicate / scope |
|---|---|---|
| View/edit own work log | `auth.gs:1294 updateWorkLog` / `auth.gs:1216 submitWorkLog` | `log['Emp_ID'] === user.empId \|\| _isAdmin`; Status/Comments need `_isManager` |
| View team work logs | `auth.gs:1464 getTeamWorkLogs`, `auth.gs:1372 getMemberWorkLogs` | `_isManager`; admin→all, else `_getTeamEmpIds(user).has(empId)` |
| Edit team member work log | `auth.gs:1391 adminSubmitWorkLog`, `1442 adminUpdateWorkLog`, `1259 updateWorkLogStatus`, `1271 updateWorkLogComment`, `1282 reviewWorkLog`; intern: `intern-work-log.gs:147/138` | `_isManager(user.role)` (throws otherwise) |
| View MIS Report | `weekly-summary.gs:507 wsCheckMisAccess` → `496 _wsHasMisAccess`; enforced again `456 getMisSummaries` | email ∈ `MIS_Access` sheet (NOT a role) |
| Approve/reject leave | `leaves.gs:48 reviewLeaveRequest` | `_isManager`; non-admin → `emp.Manager_ID === user.empId` **OR** `_getTeamEmpIds(user).has(leave.Emp_ID)` |
| Add/delete holiday | `leaves.gs:137 addHoliday`, `164 deleteHoliday` | `_isAdmin(user.role)` |
| Post/delete announcement | `dashboard.gs:229 createAnnouncement`, `272 deleteAnnouncement` | `_isAdmin(user.role)` |
| Create task | `auth.gs:870 createTask` | (none — any authenticated user) |
| Edit task | `auth.gs:898 updateTask` → `848 canModifyTask` | admin all; manager team-scope; TM assignee/assigner |
| Delete task | `auth.gs:1024 deleteTask` | admin all; else `isOwner \|\| (_isManager && isAssignee)` |
| Create project | `auth.gs:942 createProject` | `_isManager` (else "Employees cannot create projects") |
| Edit project | `auth.gs:976 updateProject` | admin all; else `Assigner_ID==empId \|\| Owner_IDs \|\| Assignee_IDs` |
| Delete project | `auth.gs:1043 deleteProject` | `_isManager`; non-admin → `Assigner_ID==empId \|\| Owner_IDs` |
| Create function | `auth.gs:1068 createFunction` | `_isManager \|\| _isTmSelfAssign(record,user)` |
| Edit function | `auth.gs:1098 updateFunction` | `_isManager`; else `isAssignee \|\| isCreator` |
| Delete function | `auth.gs:1115 deleteFunction` | `_isManager` ("Only managers can delete functions") |
| Due-date direct vs request | `dueDateRequests.gs:14 requestDueDateChange` | direct iff `_isAdmin \|\| assignerId===empId`, else creates request |
| Approve/reject due-date request | `dueDateRequests.gs:121 approveDueDateChange`, `155 rejectDueDateChange` | `_isAdmin \|\| req.Approver_ID===empId` |
| Change employee role | `auth.gs:1803 changeEmployeeRole` → `1786 _allowedNewRoles` | per-role allow-list + TC same-team + not-self |
| View all-company tasks/projects | server `auth.gs:659/706 getAuthorized*`; client `app.js.html:6007 renderAllTasks`, `6060 renderAllProjects` | admin→all; manager→own-team clamp; nav `nav-mgr-only` |
| View Org page | client only — nav `nav-mgr-only` (index.html:1862), `app.js.html:6385 renderOrgPage` | `_isManager` to reach view; role buttons gated by `_allowedNewRoles` ⚠️ no server `_isAdmin` on the view |
| Manage team members | client — nav `nav-mgr-only` (index.html:1850), `app.js.html:6344 renderTeamMgmt` | `_isManager` to reach; data scoped to own Team |
| Import tasks | `migration.gs:388/426/467 migration*`, `246 _migInsertRows`; client `app.js.html:16192 openMigrateModal` | 🔴 **no role gate anywhere** — only `getCurrentUser` identity |
| Scoreboard scope | `dashboard.gs:320 _getScores` | admin→company; `_isManager`→`getSubordinateIds`+self; else self |

---

#### Frontend nav gating summary (`index.html` + `app.js.html:1043-1060`)

`onPayloadLoaded` reveals role-gated nav by CSS class:

```javascript
// app.js.html:1047
if (managerRoles.indexOf(u.role) !== -1)
  document.querySelectorAll('.nav-mgr-only').forEach(e => e.classList.remove('hidden'));
if (adminRoles.indexOf(u.role) !== -1)
  document.querySelectorAll('.nav-admin-only').forEach(e => e.classList.remove('hidden'));
if (u.hasMisAccess) el('nav-mis-report').classList.remove('hidden');
el('nav-import-btn').classList.remove('hidden');  // ← unconditional
```

| Element | id / class | Shown to | Source line |
|---|---|---|---|
| Team section + Team Tasks/Projects/Logs | `.nav-mgr-only` | TC/TF/Admin/SA | index.html:1833-1843 |
| MIS Report | `#nav-mis-report` (`hidden`) | anyone with `hasMisAccess` | index.html:1843; app.js.html:1054 |
| Leave Approvals | `.nav-mgr-only` | managers | index.html:1846 |
| Team Management | `.nav-mgr-only` | managers | index.html:1850 |
| Company section + All Tasks/Projects | `.nav-mgr-only` | managers | index.html:1855-1859 |
| Org page | `.nav-mgr-only` | managers ⚠️ (not admin-only) | index.html:1862 |
| Forms | `.nav-mgr-only` | managers | index.html:1865 |
| Import Tasks | `#nav-import-btn` (`hidden`) | 🔴 **everyone** | index.html:1868; app.js.html:1059 |
| Notice "Post" button | `.nav-admin-only` | Admin/SA | index.html:1916 |
| "+ Add Holiday" | `.nav-admin-only` | Admin/SA | index.html:2383 |
| "+ New Project" | `.nav-mgr-only` | managers | index.html:2107 |

⚠️ All nav gating is **CSS visibility only** — server-side gates (above) are the real enforcement. A TM who calls a hidden server function directly is still blocked by the backend predicates, **except** the import functions (🔴 row 25) and the Org-page render (no server gate, but it only reads `APP.employees` which the backend already scopes for non-admins via `getAuthorizedEmployees` → active employees only).

---

### Key RBAC gaps (summary, full list in structured return)

- 🔴 **Import tasks fully ungated** front- and back-end — any TM/Intern can bulk-create tasks/functions org-wide (`migration.gs` + nav button).
- 🔴 **`_canEditWlStatus` does not exist on the backend** — CLAUDE.md implies a server predicate; backend inlines `_isManager`. Cosmetic but contradicts the documented convention.
- 🔴 **Team Facilitator cannot change any role** — `_allowedNewRoles` has no TF branch; breaks the "TC & TF equivalent" expectation for role admin.
- ⚠️ **Scope inconsistency**: scoreboard uses `getSubordinateIds` (subordinate tree) while team work-logs/clock use `_getTeamEmpIds` (flat team-string match) — a TC's "team" differs between the two surfaces.
- ✅ ~~**Leave approval scope is `Manager_ID`-based, not team-based**~~ — **FIXED** in Change #48: `getPendingLeaves`, `reviewLeaveRequest`, and `getPendingLeaveCount` now use an additive OR condition (`Manager_ID === user.empId` OR `_getTeamEmpIds(user).has(Emp_ID)`) consistent with Team Work Logs, Team Members, and Team Clock scoping.
- ⚠️ **Org page reachable by TC/TF** (nav `nav-mgr-only`, render has no `_isAdmin` guard) despite the "Admin/Super Admin" comment; mitigated by per-row `_allowedNewRoles`.
- ⚠️ **All-company task/project views** are visible to TC/TF but client-clamps data to their own team — the labels ("All Tasks", "Company") overstate scope for managers.


## PART 11: GAPS & DISCREPANCIES

This part consolidates the significant gaps between PRD intent and the actual implementation, plus latent bugs and dead code found while documenting Parts 1–10. Minor cosmetic flags are annotated inline in their respective sections with ⚠ / ✗ / 🔴 markers; the entries below are the consolidated, prioritised log. Every entry was verified against `src/`.

> Severity scale: **blocking** (feature unusable / data loss) · **high** (wrong/missing behaviour users will hit) · **medium** (drift, inconsistency, RBAC looseness) · **low** (cosmetic / dead code).

---

**GAP-001 — Registration Sub-Department cascading dropdown (RESOLVED — not a gap; entry retained for numbering)**
- **Location:** `src/index.html:3349` registration modal — `<select class="fc" id="reg-subdept" onchange="onRegSubdeptChange()">`; handlers in `src/app.js.html`.
- **PRD intent:** Sub-Department is a dropdown that cascades from the selected Team Division (pick a division → its sub-departments populate).
- **Actual:** Implemented as specified. `reg-subdept` IS a `<select>` with `onchange="onRegSubdeptChange()"` (`src/index.html:3349–3350`), and `reg-division` is a `<select onchange="onRegDivisionChange()">` (`src/index.html:3343`). `onRegDivisionChange()` (`src/app.js.html:7762–7771`) repopulates `reg-subdept`'s options from `TEAM_HIERARCHY[selectedDivision]` — i.e. it cascades from the chosen division. `onRegSubdeptChange()` (`src/app.js.html:7773–7775`) calls `_regAutoFillManager()`. There is **no** free-text variant of this field in source. (See Part 2.2, lines 587–593, which already document the field correctly.)
- **Severity:** N/A
- **Status:** ✅ IMPLEMENTED — the original gap claim was incorrect; the cascade works. Entry retained so GAP numbering stays stable.

**GAP-002 — `submitFormResponse` is called by the frontend but has no backend definition**
- **Location:** called in `src/app.js.html` (Forms fill view); no matching `function submitFormResponse` in any `.gs` file.
- **PRD intent:** Submitting a shared form records the response.
- **Actual:** `google.script.run.submitFormResponse(...)` resolves to no server function → the call fails. Form responses submitted through the in-app fill view are not persisted by this path.
- **Severity:** high
- **Status:** 🔴 BROKEN

**GAP-003 — Task import functions have no manager/admin permission gate**
- **Location:** `migration.gs` — `migrationPreview`, `migrationImport`, `migrationImportDirectRows`; UI entry `openMigrateModal()` (`#nav-import-btn`, shown to all logged-in users).
- **PRD intent:** Import is an administrative/managerial bulk operation.
- **Actual:** These functions validate only a session (`getCurrentUser`) — there is **no `_isManager`/`_isAdmin` check**. Any logged-in user (incl. Team Member / Intern) can import Functions/Tasks into the shared hierarchy.
- **Severity:** medium (risk: any TM/Intern can bulk-overwrite shared task/function hierarchy)
- **Status:** ✅ DELIBERATE OPEN ACCESS — product owner confirmed 2026-06-30 after being shown the exact trade-off. All three backend functions now include an audit comment referencing this entry. See **GAP RBAC-B** below for full reversal history.

**GAP RBAC-B — Import Tasks gate reversal (audit record)**
- **Background:** Import Tasks was identified as needing an admin/manager gate (see GAP-003) because any authenticated user could bulk-import into the shared task/function hierarchy. The risk is real and unchanged.
- **Decision (2026-06-30):** Product owner explicitly confirmed, after being shown the trade-off, that Import Tasks should be fully open to all logged-in roles. The rationale: the org is small, importers are identifiable via `Assigner_ID`/`Created_By`, and the friction of a role gate outweighs the risk at the current scale.
- **Implementation:** No code gate was added or removed (migration.gs never had a gate to remove). Audit comments were added to `migrationPreview`, `migrationImport`, `migrationImportDirectRows` citing this entry. The UI `#nav-import-btn` was repositioned from the Company section (previously hidden for non-managers) to a pinned-top sidebar item visible to all roles.
- **Status:** ✅ CLOSED — open access is intentional. Re-evaluate if the organisation grows or a data-integrity incident occurs.

**GAP-004 — Stray Tabler `ti ti-*` icon classes with no Tabler font loaded**
- **Location:** `src/app.js.html` (~11 occurrences, e.g. lines 2421, 2499, 9029, 9049, 9106, 9123, 9125, 9141, 9251, 9270, 9287 — glyphs `ti-clock-hour-4`, `ti-calendar-off`, `ti-alert-triangle`, `ti-chevron-down`, `ti-arrow-right`, `ti-calendar-week`, `ti-sort-descending-2`).
- **PRD intent:** A single, consistent icon system.
- **Actual:** The app uses **Material Symbols Outlined** everywhere, but a handful of Tabler `ti ti-*` classes remain. **No Tabler font / CDN `<link>` is loaded** in `index.html`, so these glyphs render as blank/broken (notably the per-row Work-Duration clock icon and the Plan-My-Week / date-bucket headers).
- **Severity:** low
- **Status:** 🔴 BROKEN (render blank)

**GAP-005 — Announcements `Visibility` / `Expires_At` are runtime-only (not in the SCHEMA)**
- **Location:** `dashboard.gs` `createAnnouncement` / `_getNotices` write & read `Visibility` and `Expires_At`; `setupSheets.gs` `SCHEMA.Announcements` lists only `Ann_ID, Type, Title, Message, Target_Date, Priority, Is_Active, Created_By, Created_At`.
- **PRD intent:** Announcement audience + auto-expiry are first-class, persisted fields.
- **Actual:** The two columns are used at runtime but **never scaffolded or migrated** by `setupDatabase()`/`migrateSchema()`. On a freshly-scaffolded sheet the columns do not exist until a write happens to create them — schema drift between code and `SCHEMA`.
- **Severity:** medium
- **Status:** ⚠ PARTIAL (schema drift)

**GAP-006 — Dev-DB `Work_Duration` / `Work_Breaks` schema differs from production**
- **Location:** `env-setup.gs` `_setupWorkDurationInSpreadsheet` vs. the prod shape in `work-duration.gs`.
- **PRD intent:** One schema across environments.
- **Actual:** The dev scaffolder adds `Edited_By`/`Edited_At`/`Edit_Reason` (and `Emp_ID` on breaks), omits `Email`/`Emp_Name`/`Created_At`, and renames `Break_Mins` → `Duration_Mins`. The live clock code writes the **prod** column names, so a dev DB built by `env-setup.gs` is misaligned with the code.
- **Severity:** medium
- **Status:** ⚠ PARTIAL (env drift)

**GAP-007 — `--hover` CSS variable is referenced but never declared**
- **Location:** `src/index.html` — used by `.wl-row-wknd` (~line 421) and `.wl-badge-none` (~line 456); no `--hover:` declaration in `:root`.
- **PRD intent:** N/A (latent CSS bug).
- **Actual:** `background:var(--hover)` resolves to nothing → those elements get no hover/background tint.
- **Severity:** low
- **Status:** 🔴 latent CSS bug

**GAP-008 — `toast()` type strings don't all match the styled CSS classes**
- **Location:** `toast(msg, type)` in `src/app.js.html` (~line 13663); CSS `.toast.success/.error/.warn` in `index.html`.
- **PRD intent:** Coloured success/error/info toasts.
- **Actual:** Call sites pass `'ok'` and `'info'` (~23 occurrences) but the only coloured CSS classes are `success`/`error`/`warn`. `'ok'`/`'info'` toasts fall back to the default dark `#323232` background (no green/blue accent).
- **Severity:** low
- **Status:** ⚠ PARTIAL (cosmetic)

**GAP-009 — Calendar fetches work logs it never renders (`_calAddWorkLogs` dead)**
- **Location:** `loadCalendarData` returns `r.workLogs`; helper `_calAddWorkLogs` exists (~`app.js.html` 12131) but is **never called**.
- **PRD intent:** Work logs surfaced on the calendar (implied by the fetch).
- **Actual:** The server-side work-log payload is fetched (cost) but discarded in the calendar render.
- **Severity:** low
- **Status:** ⚠ UNUSED (dead code + wasted fetch)

**GAP-010 — Weekly Summary button is ungated; "for MIS" framing is misleading**
- **Location:** Work Log header button → `openWeeklySummaryModal()`; `generateMyWeeklySummary` / `getWeeklySummary` / `saveWeeklySummary` (`weekly-summary.gs`).
- **PRD intent:** Weekly summaries feed the MIS report (an access-gated surface).
- **Actual:** The button is shown to **every role** with no gate; any employee can generate/edit **their own** summary. Only the *MIS Report* aggregate view (`getMisSummaries`) is gated by `wsCheckMisAccess`. Not a security hole (users only touch their own row) but the "for MIS" framing is undocumented/by-design.
- **Severity:** low
- **Status:** ⚠ NOTE (by design, undocumented)

**GAP-011 — Manager-resolution fallback order: code ≠ frontend comment**
- **Location:** `getTeamCaptainByTeam` (`auth.gs:1525`); a frontend comment near the registration auto-fill.
- **PRD intent:** Deterministic manager assignment on registration.
- **Actual:** The real backend order is **sub-dept TC → team TC → `LGD_DEFAULT_MANAGER_EMAIL` Script Property → any active Super Admin → any active Admin → `{ok:false}`**. The frontend comment claims "TC → Admin → SA → TF"; there is **no TF step**, and SA precedes Admin. The comment is stale/misleading (behaviour itself is correct).
- **Severity:** low
- **Status:** ⚠ NOTE (stale comment)

**GAP-012 — Chat bot writes non-schema columns (chat-logged work is invisible)**
- **Location:** `chat.gs` — `_chatNewWorkLog` writes `Work_Log` with `Tasks_Worked / Description / Hours_Total / Blockers / Plan_Tomorrow`; `_chatNewTask` sets `Status:'Backlog'` and singular `Assignee_ID`, and generates IDs with pad-4 (`TSK-0001`).
- **PRD intent:** Chat `log:` / `task:` commands create normal work-log / task records.
- **Actual:** None of those Work_Log columns exist in the real schema (which uses `Attendance`, `Work Update - 1st/2nd Half`, `Extra Hours`, `Status`, `Comments`), so chat-logged work never appears in the Work Log UI. `'Backlog'` is not a valid `TASK_STATUSES` value and `Assignee_ID` (singular) is not the canonical `Assignee_IDs`.
- **Severity:** medium
- **Status:** 🔴 BROKEN (orphan columns) / ⚠ inconsistent

**GAP-013 — CRUD GAS functions use an inconsistent return contract**
- **Location:** `auth.gs` — `createTask`/`updateTask`/`deleteTask`/`createProject`/`updateProject`/`deleteProject`/`submitProgressUpdate`/`submitWorkLog`/`adminSubmitWorkLog`.
- **PRD intent:** Uniform `{ ok, ... }` / `{ ok:false, error }` envelope (the convention used elsewhere).
- **Actual:** Several of these return a **bare ID string** or nothing and **throw** on error instead of returning the `{ok}` envelope. Frontend handlers must special-case them.
- **Severity:** low
- **Status:** ⚠ NOTE (API inconsistency)

**GAP-014 — No email notification on registration submit / approve / reject**
- **Location:** `submitRegistration` / `approveRegistration` / `rejectRegistration` (`auth.gs`).
- **PRD intent:** Applicants/managers are notified of registration status.
- **Actual:** These functions write the queue/employee rows and (on approve) add the user to the team Chat space, but send **no email** to the applicant or manager. (OTP reset and meeting reminders do send via `GmailApp`.)
- **Severity:** low
- **Status:** ✗ NOT IMPLEMENTED (notifications)

**GAP-015 — `emptyState()` helper always emits the `inbox` glyph**
- **Location:** `emptyState(title, sub)` in `src/app.js.html` (~line 13658).
- **PRD intent:** Context-appropriate empty-state icons.
- **Actual:** The helper hard-codes the `inbox` Material Symbol; call sites that want a different glyph (`notifications_none`, `groups`, `account_tree`, `leaderboard`) hand-write the markup instead of using the helper. Two loading placeholders also use a literal `⏳` emoji (`index.html` 3579, 3596) rather than the icon font.
- **Severity:** low
- **Status:** ⚠ NOTE (inconsistency)

---

> **Note on completeness.** Additional low-severity, per-element flags (e.g. individual `ti ti-*` sites, specific cosmetic mismatches) are annotated inline in Parts 1–10 next to the feature they affect. The list above is the consolidated, de-duplicated set of the significant and verified discrepancies.


## PART 12: VERIFICATION CHECKLISTS

Granular, testable checklists per module. Each item is an *Action → expected result* a QA engineer can execute against the deployed app. Items are grouped by the module they verify, mirroring Parts 1–10.


### Application Shell Checklist

#### Design tokens & theme
- [ ] Inspect `:root` in DevTools -> all 21 tokens present with exact values (`--p:#1a237e`, `--accent:#00897b`, `--danger:#c62828`, `--warn:#e65100`, `--ok:#2e7d32`, `--sidebar:230px`, `--hh:56px`, `--r:8px`, etc.).
- [ ] Header background -> resolves to `--p` (#1a237e indigo).
- [ ] `#hd-avatar` background -> resolves to `--accent` (#00897b teal).
- [ ] Open a Task Sheet / Work Log view -> note navy `#2D3E51` + crimson `#E64D3D` are inline literals, NOT tokens (parallel palette gap).
- [ ] Medium-priority task card left border -> blue `#1565c0` (hardcoded, not a token).

#### Typography
- [ ] Resize viewport 375px -> body font-size = 13px; resize >=768px -> body font-size = 15px (clamp works).
- [ ] Confirm Montserrat loads (DevTools Network: Material+Symbols + Montserrat css2 requests both 200).
- [ ] `.ph-title` renders 20px/700; `.stat-val` 30px/700; `.nav-item` 13px (600 when active).

#### Icon system
- [ ] Every sidebar nav icon renders a visible Material Symbol glyph (home, calendar_view_week, task_alt, folder_open, edit_note, calendar_month, video_call, account_tree, event_available, contacts, checklist_rtl, folder_special, monitoring, assessment, pending_actions, groups, table_rows, account_tree, corporate_fare, description, upload_file).
- [ ] Dashboard clock widget -> the leading `ti ti-clock-hour-4` icon (app.js.html:2421,2499) is BLANK/invisible (BROKEN — Tabler font not loaded).
- [ ] Dashboard "My Upcoming Tasks" header -> `ti ti-sort-descending-2` icon (9123) is BLANK.
- [ ] Dashboard overdue warning -> `ti ti-alert-triangle` (9049,9251) is BLANK.
- [ ] Dashboard "view all" link -> `ti ti-arrow-right` (9125,9141) is BLANK.
- [ ] Dashboard upcoming-toggle chevron -> `ti ti-chevron-down` (9106) is BLANK.
- [ ] Dashboard calendar blocks -> `ti ti-calendar-off`/`ti ti-calendar-week` (9029,9270,9287) are BLANK.
- [ ] Confirm 11 total `ti ti-*` occurrences all render empty (grep count = 11).

#### Sidebar navigation — role gating
- [ ] Log in as Team Member -> only "My Space" section (Dashboard..Directory) + Import Tasks visible; Team/Company sections hidden; no MIS Report.
- [ ] Log in as Team Captain or Team Facilitator -> Team + Company sections reveal (`.nav-mgr-only` un-hidden), including All Tasks / All Projects / Organisation / Forms.
- [ ] Log in as Admin/Super Admin -> same nav as TC/TF (no admin-exclusive item exists; `.nav-admin-only` is dead code).
- [ ] User with MIS access (MIS_Access sheet) -> "MIS Report" (`#nav-mis-report`) appears regardless of role.
- [ ] User without MIS access -> "MIS Report" stays hidden.
- [ ] Import Tasks (`#nav-import-btn`) visible to ALL logged-in users after `onPayloadLoaded`.
- [ ] Import Tasks click -> calls `openMigrateModal()` (NOT navigate); does not change active nav highlight.
- [ ] On mobile (<=768px): calendar, meetings, org-chart, directory, team-tasks, team-mgmt, org-page, forms nav items are hidden.

#### Sidebar navigation — active state
- [ ] Click each nav item -> target `.view` shows, all others hide, clicked item gets `.active` (indigo tint bg + left border).
- [ ] Navigate while on mobile with nav open -> nav drawer closes (`closeMobNav`).
- [ ] Navigate to a mobile-blocked view via direct call -> redirected to dashboard.
- [ ] "Team Members" nav item -> opens `team-mgmt` view (label/data-view name mismatch is intentional).

#### Header bar
- [ ] After login -> `#hd-avatar` shows first initial of name (uppercase); `#hd-name` = full name; `#hd-role` = role string.
- [ ] Click `#hd-status-wrap` -> `togglePresMenu` toggles `#pres-menu`.
- [ ] Presence menu: Online/Away/DND/Appear Offline each call `setPresStatus('<state>')` and update `#hd-status-dot` class.
- [ ] Presence menu "My Profile" -> `openProfileModal()`.
- [ ] Presence menu "Sign Out" -> `logout()` clears localStorage token, calls `invalidateSession`, reloads page.
- [ ] Header refresh button -> spins icon, calls `getInitialPayload`, repopulates APP.tasks/projects/employees/functions, re-renders current view, toast "Refreshed" (green).
- [ ] Refresh failure -> toast "Refresh failed: <msg>" (red), icon stops, button re-enabled.
- [ ] Mobile menu button (`#mob-menu-btn`) -> hidden on desktop; on mobile opens nav drawer.

#### Toast pattern
- [ ] Trigger a `toast(msg,'success')` -> green toast, bottom-right, auto-dismiss after 3.5s.
- [ ] Trigger a `toast(msg,'error')` -> red toast.
- [ ] Trigger a `toast(msg,'warn')` -> orange toast.
- [ ] Trigger any save that calls `toast(msg,'ok')` -> toast appears GREY (BROKEN: `.toast.ok` undefined; should be green).
- [ ] Trigger any `toast(msg,'info')` -> toast appears GREY (BROKEN: `.toast.info` undefined).
- [ ] Multiple toasts stack vertically with 8px gap, newest at bottom.
- [ ] On mobile -> toast spans full width (left:10px/right:10px), font 15px.

#### Loading / skeleton
- [ ] During global refresh -> header refresh icon spins (`spin 0.8s`).
- [ ] Buttons with `.btn-spinner` show a spinning white ring during in-flight GAS calls.
- [ ] Login verifying state -> `.login-card.verifying` shows spinner before status text.
- [ ] Any view rendering `.view-skeleton` -> `.sk-row`/`.sk-card` shimmer left-to-right (1.4s loop) until data loads.

#### Empty state
- [ ] Navigate to a view with no data -> `emptyState()` renders centered `inbox` Material Symbol + bold title (+ optional sub line).
- [ ] Empty-state title/sub with special characters -> rendered escaped (no HTML injection via `esc()`).

#### Modal shell
- [ ] Open any modal -> `.modal-bg` overlay (50% black) covers screen, box centered, `slideUp` entrance animation plays.
- [ ] Modal header sticky on scroll; footer sticky at bottom; long body scrolls within `max-height:92vh`.
- [ ] `.modal-lg` modal (task detail) -> max-width 760px; `.modal-xl` -> 96vw.
- [ ] Click backdrop (outside box) -> modal closes (`closeModal(id,event)` target check).
- [ ] Click inside box -> modal stays open.
- [ ] Click `.modal-x` (✕) -> modal closes unconditionally.
- [ ] Close `member-log-modal` -> pending `ML_DEBOUNCE` timers cleared.
- [ ] On mobile -> `.modal`/`.modal.modal-lg`/`.modal.modal-xl` go full-width (verify `.modal-box`/`.modal-content` JS override actually matches real markup — possible selector mismatch).

#### View toggle
- [ ] `navigate()` -> exactly one `.view` has `.active`; all others have `.hidden`.
- [ ] Leaving dashboard -> `_teamClockStopTickers()` runs (no leaked intervals).
- [ ] Leaving work-log -> all `WL_DEBOUNCE` timers cleared.
- [ ] Leaving task views -> task-sheet scroll listeners removed from `#main`.


### Authentication & Registration Checklist

#### Login — sign-in
- [ ] Open the app with no saved session -> login card shows, email input focused, status "Sign in with your registered work email".
- [ ] Click Sign In with both fields empty -> inline error "Enter your email and password." (no GAS call).
- [ ] Enter a wrong password -> error "Incorrect password. Please try again." (from backend `loginWithPassword`).
- [ ] Enter an unregistered email -> error "Account registered:" message advising to contact Admin.
- [ ] Enter an account with a blank Password_Hash -> error "Your account has no password set. Contact your Admin."
- [ ] Enter valid email+password, click Sign In -> button shows "Signing in...", then the verified card appears showing avatar/name/email/role/team (+designation if present); status "Account verified. Click below to continue."; Sign-In form hidden; "Enter Dashboard →" button revealed.
- [ ] Press Enter in the email field -> focus moves to password (does NOT submit).
- [ ] Press Enter in the password field -> triggers login() (submits).
- [ ] After a successful Sign In, check localStorage['tm_sess'] -> a UUID token is present (saved before clicking Enter Dashboard).
- [ ] Click the password eye icon -> input toggles between masked/visible and icon swaps.
- [ ] Click "Enter Dashboard →" -> spinner "Loading…", app loads via getInitialPayload; nav items reveal per role.

#### Auto-login / session restore
- [ ] With a valid tm_sess token, reload the page -> status briefly "Restoring session…", form dims, app loads directly (no verified card, no Enter click).
- [ ] Manually set localStorage tm_sess to "null" -> reload -> token removed, plain login form shown (no error).
- [ ] Wait past 7-day TTL (or delete the sess_ ScriptProperty) and reload -> status "Session expired. Please sign in again." and token cleared (reason expired).
- [ ] Reload during a transient GAS/network error -> token KEPT, login form shown silently (no scary error). Reload again later -> session restores.
- [ ] Each successful restore extends expiry (sliding 7-day) -> sess_ property `expires` is bumped to now+7d.
- [ ] Logout -> invalidateSession deletes the sess_ token and localStorage is cleared.

#### Forgot password
- [ ] Click "Forgot password?" -> sign-in form hides, reset panel shows step 1, email pre-filled if already typed.
- [ ] Click Send Reset Code with empty email -> error "Please enter your email."
- [ ] Enter an unregistered/inactive email -> error "No active account found for this email."
- [ ] Enter a valid email -> "Code sent! Check your email."; step 2 appears; OTP field focused; a 6-digit code email arrives (15-min expiry).
- [ ] Submit step 2 with empty OTP -> "Please enter the reset code."
- [ ] Enter a wrong OTP -> "Invalid reset code. Please check and try again."
- [ ] Enter the correct OTP but a <6-char new password -> "Password must be at least 6 characters."
- [ ] New password != confirm -> "Passwords do not match."
- [ ] Enter correct OTP + matching 6+ char password -> "Password reset! Please sign in."; returns to sign-in with email pre-filled; old OTP property deleted; new login with the new password succeeds.
- [ ] Wait >15 min then submit OTP -> "Reset code expired. Please request a new one." and the OTP property is deleted.
- [ ] Click "Resend code" -> a fresh OTP is generated and emailed (overwrites the prior one).
- [ ] Click "← Back to Sign In" -> reset panel hides, sign-in form returns, OTP/new/confirm fields cleared.
- [ ] (Security) Rapidly request many reset codes -> NO rate limit / lockout exists (gap).

#### Registration modal — open + reset
- [ ] Click "New here? Register →" -> modal opens, all fields blank, role select = "— Select Role —", division = "— Select Division —", sub-dept dimmed at "— Select Sub-department —", submit enabled.
- [ ] Close via ✕, Cancel, or backdrop click -> modal hidden.

#### Registration fields
- [ ] Submit with empty First Name -> "First name is required." Repeat for Last Name, Email, Password, Role, DOB, Division (each surfaces its specific message).
- [ ] Password <6 chars -> "Password must be at least 6 characters."
- [ ] Password != Confirm -> "Passwords do not match."
- [ ] Designation accepts up to 100 chars (maxlength) and is optional.
- [ ] Role select lists exactly: Super Admin, Admin, Team Captain, Team Facilitator, Team Member, Intern.
- [ ] (Gap) Selecting Super Admin/Admin/Team Captain is allowed on the public form -> escalation gated only by approver.
- [ ] Division select lists the 8 divisions from TEAM_HIERARCHY.

#### Registration — sub-department cascade (flagship)
- [ ] Confirm reg-subdept is a <select> (NOT a free-text input).
- [ ] Select division "5. Tech" -> sub-dept select repopulates with 5a. Product, 5b. Development, 5c. Maintenance; wrapper fully opaque.
- [ ] Select division "3. Knowledge" (no sub-depts) -> sub-dept select shows only the placeholder and wrapper dims to opacity 0.4.
- [ ] Switch division again -> sub-dept options refresh correctly to the new division's list.

#### Registration — manager resolution
- [ ] Select role Team Member + a division that HAS a Team Captain -> manager email auto-fills, field readOnly with green border, hint "✓ Auto-filled: {name} (Manager)", submit enabled.
- [ ] Select role Team Member + a division with NO TC/Admin/SA and no default property -> red border, placeholder "No manager assigned for this team", hint "...contact the admin.", submit DISABLED.
- [ ] Pick a sub-dept that has a sub-dept-specific TC -> manager re-resolves to that sub-dept TC (Step 1a) over the team-only TC.
- [ ] With LGD_DEFAULT_MANAGER_EMAIL set and no team TC -> manager auto-fills to that default (Priyanka Dugar by default).
- [ ] Select role Super Admin -> manager label "Reports-to Email" (no asterisk), field manual/editable, manager email OPTIONAL (can submit blank).
- [ ] Select role Admin or Team Captain -> manager label "Reports-to Email *", field manual/editable, manager email REQUIRED.
- [ ] Simulate getTeamCaptainByTeam failure (network) -> field unlocks for manual entry, submit enabled (fail-open).

#### Registration — submit
- [ ] Submit a valid TM registration -> toast "Registration submitted! Your manager will review your request."; modal closes.
- [ ] Re-submit the same email while pending -> "A registration request for this email is already pending."
- [ ] Submit an email that already exists in Employees -> "An account with this email already exists."
- [ ] (Gap) Confirm NO email is sent to the manager on submission.

#### Admin approval flow
- [ ] As Admin/SA open Organisation view -> all Pending Registration Requests render as reg-cards (name/email/role/team/manager/date/message).
- [ ] As TC/TF open Team Management view -> only requests where you are the Manager_Email OR same team render.
- [ ] As a Team Member open team-mgmt/org -> no pending-reg section (backend returns empty).
- [ ] Click Approve -> confirm dialog -> on confirm, toast "Registration approved! Employee ID: EMP-###"; the new employee appears in the list after refresh.
- [ ] The approved user can immediately sign in with the password they chose at registration.
- [ ] Approve a request whose team has a Chat space -> _tryAddToTeamSpace runs (failure is swallowed, approval still succeeds).
- [ ] Click Reject -> prompt for reason; on submit, toast "Registration rejected."; the card disappears (no employee created).
- [ ] Click Reject then Cancel the prompt -> nothing happens.
- [ ] (Gap) Confirm NO email is sent to the applicant on approve OR reject.
- [ ] (Gap) A same-team TC/TF can approve a request that self-selected Role=Admin/Super Admin.


### Dashboard & Plan My Week Checklist

#### 3.1 Dashboard — header & stats
- [ ] Open the app / click "Dashboard" -> `#view-dashboard` shows; greeting reads "Good morning/afternoon/evening, {your name}!" matching the current hour (<12 / <17 / else).
- [ ] Date line under greeting -> shows today's full date "Weekday, D Month YYYY" (en-IN).
- [ ] Stats grid renders 6 cards: My Tasks, Open, In Progress, Under Review, Done, Projects.
- [ ] "My Tasks" count -> equals number of tasks where you are in Assignee_IDs.
- [ ] "Open" count -> equals tasks NOT in Done/Completed/Cancelled/Implemented (verify it INCLUDES your WIP and On Hold tasks — label says "pending" but is broad).
- [ ] "In Progress" count -> equals only tasks whose status starts with "WIP".
- [ ] GAP: "Under Review / awaiting approval" card -> verify it actually counts ONLY status === "On Hold" (NOT "Review"). Create a task with status "Review" -> it should NOT increment this card (mismatch).
- [ ] "Done" count -> equals Done/Completed/Implemented tasks.
- [ ] "Projects" count -> equals distinct projects (incl. parents) derived from your tasks.
- [ ] On a ≤768px viewport -> stats grid (#dash-stats) and scoreboard wrap (#dash-scoreboard-wrap) are hidden.

#### 3.1 Dashboard — Notice Board (read)
- [ ] Notice board shows "Loading..." then renders cards (or "No announcements right now" empty state with notifications_none icon).
- [ ] An active announcement within its From/To window and matching your audience -> appears.
- [ ] Announcement with Expires_At in the past -> does NOT appear.
- [ ] Announcement with Target_Date in the future -> does NOT appear.
- [ ] "TCs Only" visibility announcement -> visible only to Team Captain / Admin / Super Admin.
- [ ] "TCs & TFs" visibility -> visible to TC / TF / Admin / SA only.
- [ ] Urgent priority announcement -> shows red "URGENT" badge.
- [ ] Non-Organisation announcement -> shows a visibility badge pill.
- [ ] As an admin -> announcement card shows "Visible until {date}".
- [ ] Holiday within 2 days -> appears as Holiday notice (celebration icon).
- [ ] Today is an employee's birthday (DOB MM-DD) -> "Happy Birthday" notice appears.
- [ ] Upcoming company/team meeting (today..+30d) -> appears with Join/View buttons; clicking card navigates to meetings.
- [ ] Active form shared to your team -> appears with "Fill Form" link -> opens Responder_URL.

#### 3.1 Dashboard — Notice Board (post, admin only)
- [ ] As Team Member -> "Post" button (#btn-nb-post) is NOT visible.
- [ ] As Admin -> "Post" button visible; click -> #nb-post-panel un-hides; nb-title focused.
- [ ] On panel open -> nb-date, ann-start-date set to today; ann-end-date set to today+7; nb-visibility="Organisation".
- [ ] Type select -> options exactly: General, Emergency, Reminder.
- [ ] Priority select -> options exactly: Normal, High, Urgent.
- [ ] Click "🏢 Org-wide" template -> visibility=Organisation, start=today, end=today+7, priority=Normal.
- [ ] Click "👥 TCs & TFs" template -> visibility="TCs & TFs", end=today+14, priority=High.
- [ ] Click "⭐ TCs Only" template -> visibility="TCs Only", end=today+30, priority=High.
- [ ] Click "Post Announcement" with empty title -> toast "Title is required."; nothing saved.
- [ ] Set To date earlier than From date -> click Post -> toast "End date must be after start date."
- [ ] GAP: Change "Target Date" (nb-date) to a non-today value, set ann-start-date different -> post -> verify saved Target_Date follows ann-start-date, NOT nb-date (nb-date is orphaned/unused).
- [ ] Valid post -> button shows "Posting…", then toast "Announcement posted!", panel closes, board re-renders with the new card.
- [ ] As admin, on a manual announcement card -> "×" delete button present; non-manual (holiday/meeting/form) cards -> no delete button.
- [ ] Click delete -> confirm dialog -> on OK toast "Announcement removed." and card disappears.

#### 3.1 Dashboard — On Leave Today
- [ ] With an approved leave covering today -> employee chip shows initials, name, "{leaveType} · {team}", and "Back {endDate+1}".
- [ ] No one on leave today -> empty state groups icon + "Everyone is in today!".
- [ ] Pending (not Approved) leave covering today -> does NOT appear.

#### 3.2 Plan My Week
- [ ] Sidebar "Plan My Week" (calendar_view_week icon) -> opens #view-plan-week.

- [ ] Closed (Done/Cancelled/etc.) tasks -> excluded.

- [ ] Recurring filter = All / — / Daily / Weekly / Monthly / Quarterly; selecting "—" shows only non-recurring tasks.
